You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_51.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 51: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[51]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_51.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 51: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content: 2735 /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py

1	"""S4 OUTLINE — Agentic outliner loop (W0 + W1).
2	
3	Authoritative design: ``docs/fsr_build_plan.md`` section "AGENTIC OUTLINER LOOP — FABLE
4	AUTHORITATIVE DESIGN" (2026-07-11, effort max). This module is the THIN driver
5	(``OutlineAgent``, ~ design target 300 LOC for the core loop; this file additionally carries
6	the workspace/ledger/tool wiring the design assigns to the same file) that WIRES the existing
7	ReAct contract — ``ToolRegistry`` / ``ToolDefinition`` / ``ToolResult`` / ``AnalysisNotebook``
8	(``src/polaris_graph/tools/tool_registry.py`` + ``analysis_notebook.py``) and the
9	``ReactDecision`` schema (``src/polaris_graph/tools/react_agent.py``) — into a NEW small
10	per-turn decide/execute loop for the OUTLINE stage. It does **not** subclass the 8k-line
11	``ReactAnalysisAgent`` (react-mode is ~150 LOC of that file; the rest is the compose-writer,
12	an unrelated concern). It does **not** import smolagents.
13	
14	CONTROL FLOW (verbatim from the design):
15	  seed via existing ``_call_outline`` (un-starved) -> sufficiency review#0 -> gap_ledger.
16	  Loop while turns < PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_MAX_TURNS(24): ``_decide`` picks ONE of
17	  {analysis tool | inspect_basket | search_more_evidence | update_outline | finish_outline};
18	  execute; if search: fold-in + re-check aspect; if update_outline: validated parse/apply via
19	  ``outline_revise``; if finish: sufficiency checklist — deficiencies+budget => bounce & continue;
20	  clean/exhausted => exit. EXIT: write cp4 (typed plans + unfilled_gaps[] + notebook ref + flag
21	  slate) via ``outline_checkpoint.build_cp4_payload`` / ``write_cp4_outline_snapshot``.
22	
23	GAP TRIGGER — 3 detectors feeding ONE ``GapLedger``:
24	  (1) checklist (LLM, per-section exhaustive-coverage / density / compute-need) at seed,
25	      after every fold-in, and at every ``finish_outline`` attempt;
26	  (2) TOOL-FAILURE deterministic (empty comparison / zero datapoints / empty SQL / <2 studies);
27	  (3) agent-initiated (the decide step names a NEW aspect when calling ``search_more_evidence``).
28	  Per-aspect retry ``PG_OUTLINE_GAP_RETRIES_PER_ASPECT`` (default 2), then UNFILLED + disclosed;
29	  dedup by ``(section, aspect)``.
30	
31	FAITHFULNESS: the strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / provenance engine is UNTOUCHED and stays the
32	ONLY hard gate downstream of this module. Every row this module folds into the corpus is a real
33	fetched-and-classified row from ``run_live_retrieval`` (same production path the rest of the
34	pipeline uses) — this module manufactures ZERO evidence text and never marks anything "verified"
35	itself. The id-collision seam at fold-in is a HARD fail-loud assert (new-ids intersect
36	existing-ids must be empty) — see ``_offset_renumber``.
37	
38	Seat: ``PG_OUTLINE_AGENT`` (default OFF). OFF => the seam at
39	``multi_section_generator._call_outline`` call site is a pure pass-through — byte-identical to
40	pre-existing behavior (see ``run_outline_agent_or_legacy`` at the bottom of this file, which is
41	what the generator actually calls).
42	"""
43	
44	from __future__ import annotations
45	
46	import asyncio
47	import dataclasses
48	import json
49	import logging
50	import os
51	import time
52	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
53	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
54	
55	from src.polaris_graph.outline._fold_in import (
56	    _offset_renumber,
57	    _stamp_and_delete,
58	    fold_in_fetched_rows,
59	)
60	from src.polaris_graph.tools.analysis_notebook import AnalysisNotebook, AnalysisStep
61	from src.polaris_graph.tools.react_agent import ReactDecision
62	from src.polaris_graph.tools.tool_registry import (
63	    ToolDefinition,
64	    ToolRegistry,
65	    ToolResult,
66	    build_default_registry,
67	)
68	
69	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph")
70	
71	_OFF_VALUES = ("0", "false", "no", "off", "")
72	
73	
74	def _env_flag(name: str, default_on: bool) -> bool:
75	    raw = os.getenv(name, "1" if default_on else "0")
76	    return str(raw).strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
77	
78	
79	def _env_int(name: str, default: int) -> int:
80	    try:
81	        return int(os.getenv(name, str(default)))
82	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
83	        return default
84	
85	
86	def _env_float(name: str, default: float) -> float:
87	    try:
88	        return float(os.getenv(name, str(default)))
89	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
90	        return default
91	
92	
93	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
94	# Seat + knobs (LAW VI — zero hard-coding; every knob is env-tunable)
95	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
96	
97	def outline_agent_enabled() -> bool:
98	    """``PG_OUTLINE_AGENT`` kill-switch. DEFAULT OFF => the legacy ``_call_outline``-only path
99	    is byte-identical (the agentic loop never runs, never imports its heavy dependencies beyond
100	    this module's own top-level imports, and never touches the corpus)."""
101	    return _env_flag("PG_OUTLINE_AGENT", default_on=False)
102	
103	
104	PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_MODEL_DEFAULT = "z-ai/glm-5.2"
105	PG_OUTLINER_CODE_MODEL_DEFAULT = "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro"
106	PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_MAX_TURNS_DEFAULT = 24
107	PG_OUTLINE_GAP_RETRIES_PER_ASPECT_DEFAULT = 2
108	PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_WALL_SECONDS_DEFAULT = 900
109	# Iter-6 P0 fix (Fable-confirmed real-corpus crash: matched_comparison_run5.log,
110	# ReasoningFirstTruncationError at _run_checklist on the dense DRB-72 AI-labor corpus —
111	# glm-5.2 is a reasoning-first model in _ALWAYS_REASON_MODELS; on a dense corpus its
112	# unbounded-effort reasoning prelude alone ran to ~84453 chars (~21k tokens), blowing past
113	# the 16384-token TOTAL budget before content was ever reached. Iter-5 re-ran the SAME
114	# starved defaults and reproduced the identical crash — the fix below was never actually
115	# applied to code in iters 1-5, only described. Per CLAUDE.md §9.1.8 ("reasoning effort +
116	# max_tokens ALWAYS go MAX, never starve; read the API don't guess") the real OpenRouter cap
117	# for z-ai/glm-5.2 is top_provider.max_completion_tokens=128000 (verified via GET
118	# https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models 2026-07-11). These three control-plane budgets are
119	# raised well below that real ceiling (comfortable headroom for a large multi-page reasoning
120	# prelude) and — see the call sites below — now pass an EXPLICIT ``reasoning_max_tokens``
121	# (the same PG_OUTLINE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS knob the seed ``_call_outline`` already uses,
122	# default 32768) so the reasoning pool is bounded within the total instead of running
123	# unbounded-effort until it eats the whole budget. Belt-and-suspenders: _run_checklist ALSO
124	# now degrades fail-open (one retry at 2x budget, then "no new deficiencies" + disclosure)
125	# instead of letting a control-plane truncation abort the entire outline stage.
126	# Un-starved to the CONFIRMED real OpenRouter provider cap for z-ai/glm-5.2
127	# (top_provider.max_completion_tokens=131072, GET /api/v1/models 2026-07-11 per
128	# CLAUDE.md §9.1.8 "read the API, don't guess" — NOT a moderate raise). Reasoning
129	# is bounded separately via PG_OUTLINE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS (32768) so the reasoning
130	# prelude cannot eat the whole budget before content — leaving ~98k tokens of content
131	# headroom, comfortably above the ~21k-token prelude that caused the real-corpus crash.
132	PG_OUTLINE_DECIDE_MAX_TOKENS_DEFAULT = 131072
133	PG_OUTLINE_CHECKLIST_MAX_TOKENS_DEFAULT = 131072
134	PG_OUTLINE_QUERY_DERIVE_MAX_TOKENS_DEFAULT = 131072
135	# W2 P0 fix: outer belt-and-suspenders wall on the whole ``agent.run()`` call (grace
136	# added on top of the loop's own internal wall-clock check, covering a single LLM call
137	# that hangs past its own inner timeout). Never the primary bound — ``_wall_seconds()``
138	# is — this only guarantees the outer ``run_outline_agent_or_legacy`` caller is never
139	# wedged indefinitely.
140	# P1 DEGRADE-TAIL FIX (2026-07-12, this wheel): the internal loop stops STARTING new turns
141	# at ``_wall_seconds()`` (900s) but a turn ALREADY in flight runs to completion — and a single
142	# legitimate ``search_more_evidence`` mega-fetch (observed: 162/200 URLs in 466.2s, bounded only
143	# by the retrieval deadline which is checked BETWEEN per-query fetches, not mid-batch) can push
144	# that final in-flight turn well past the wall. With the old 180s grace the outer
145	# ``asyncio.wait_for`` CANCELLED that legitimately-progressing final turn -> TimeoutError ->
146	# DEGRADE-TO-SEED (cp4_used='agentic-degraded-seed'), throwing away a good agentic run over a
147	# slow-but-honest fetch. The mission fix is to PARK the wall: give the final in-flight turn enough
148	# grace to COMPLETE (quality-preserving, even if slower — NO fetch-cap, NO turn cut, zero coverage
149	# loss), after which the loop sees elapsed>=wall and returns NORMALLY as agentic. Raised 180 -> 600
150	# to comfortably cover the documented ~466s mega-fetch overshoot with margin; still an ABSOLUTE
151	# ceiling (wall+grace = ~25min) that catches a TRUE hang, because every inner call is itself bounded
152	# (entailment 150s/call, retrieval deadline, decide/checklist max_tokens). Env-overridable (LAW VI).
153	PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_RUN_TIMEOUT_GRACE_SECONDS_DEFAULT = 600
154	
155	
156	def outliner_agent_model() -> str:
157	    """ReAct decide + tool-calling model (§9.1.8 lock: glm-5.2, 1M ctx — context compounds
158	    across a growing digest + draft + ledger + notebook)."""
159	    return os.getenv("PG_OUTLINER_AGENT_MODEL", PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_MODEL_DEFAULT)
160	
161	
162	def outliner_code_model() -> str:
163	    """Write+run-Python / outline-prose model (§9.1.8 lock: deepseek-v4-pro — already the
164	    sweep's generator)."""
165	    return os.getenv("PG_OUTLINER_CODE_MODEL", PG_OUTLINER_CODE_MODEL_DEFAULT)
166	
167	
168	# Tools whose executor actually CONSUMES the ``client`` kwarg (LLM codegen). Not the same set as
169	# ``requires_llm=True``: search_more_evidence carries that flag but builds its own clients.
170	_CODEGEN_TOOLS = frozenset({"execute_python"})
171	
172	
173	def _max_turns() -> int:
174	    return _env_int("PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_MAX_TURNS", PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_MAX_TURNS_DEFAULT)
175	
176	
177	def _wall_seconds() -> int:
178	    return _env_int("PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_WALL_SECONDS", PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_WALL_SECONDS_DEFAULT)
179	
180	
181	# Iter-6 P0 fix: shared reasoning-pool knob (same env var + default the seed ``_call_outline``
182	# uses in multi_section_generator.py, so ONE setting governs the whole outline stage's
183	# reasoning budget — LAW VI, no duplicated config surface). Bounds the reasoning slice WITHIN
184	# the total max_tokens ceiling so a reasoning-first model (glm-5.2) always reaches content.
185	PG_OUTLINE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS_DEFAULT = 32768
186	# Real OpenRouter provider cap for z-ai/glm-5.2 (top_provider.max_completion_tokens), verified
187	# via GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models 2026-07-11 per §9.1.8 "read the API, don't
188	# guess". Used only as a safety ceiling on the fail-open retry-with-bigger-budget path below —
189	# never worth requesting more than the provider will honor.
190	PG_GLM52_REAL_MAX_COMPLETION_TOKENS = 131072  # confirmed via GET /api/v1/models 2026-07-11
191	
192	
193	def _reasoning_max_tokens() -> int:
194	    return _env_int("PG_OUTLINE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS", PG_OUTLINE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS_DEFAULT)
195	
196	
197	def _gap_retries_per_aspect() -> int:
198	    return _env_int(
199	        "PG_OUTLINE_GAP_RETRIES_PER_ASPECT", PG_OUTLINE_GAP_RETRIES_PER_ASPECT_DEFAULT,
200	    )
201	
202	
203	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
204	# Gap ledger — 3 detectors -> ONE ledger; per-aspect retry cap; UNFILLED+disclosed
205	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
206	
207	# iter-2 P1-1 fix: a fresh checklist round re-words the SAME underlying gap almost every
208	# time (GLM does not reproduce its own exact phrasing across calls). Exact-string keying
209	# then spawns a brand-new PENDING todo each round and the per-aspect retry cap never bites
210	# — the loop re-fetches the same aspect forever. This is a ROUTING dedup (does this new
211	# phrase point at a todo we already have?), not a quality/faithfulness judgment about the
212	# corpus, so token-overlap collapse is in-scope here even though the SAME mechanism is
213	# banned for faithfulness verdicts (§-1.1 ghost-mindset) — those are two different jobs.
214	_ASPECT_DEDUP_JACCARD_THRESHOLD_DEFAULT = 0.4
215	_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
216	    "a", "an", "the", "and", "or", "of", "in", "on", "for", "to", "with", "vs", "versus",
217	    "is", "are", "was", "were", "be", "been", "its", "it", "this", "that", "any", "no",
218	    "not", "at", "by", "from", "as", "about", "into", "over", "than", "other",
219	})
220	
221	
222	def _canon_tokens(text: str) -> frozenset[str]:
223	    """Lowercase, punctuation-stripped, stopword-dropped token SET — the canonical form used
224	    ONLY to collapse checklist paraphrases onto one ledger todo (see module note above)."""
225	    import re as _re
226	    words = _re.findall(r"[a-z0-9]+", str(text or "").lower())
227	    return frozenset(w for w in words if w and w not in _STOPWORDS)
228	
229	
230	def _jaccard(a: frozenset, b: frozenset) -> float:
231	    if not a and not b:
232	        return 1.0
233	    if not a or not b:
234	        return 0.0
235	    inter = len(a & b)
236	    union = len(a | b)
237	    return inter / union if union else 0.0
238	
239	
240	def _normalize_section_label(section: str) -> str:
241	    """Whitespace/case-insensitive key for ``OutlineWorkspace.unhomeable_sections`` — a section
242	    LABEL (not a specific aspect wording) is what's proven unhomeable, so this key deliberately
243	    ignores aspect text entirely (see the field's docstring)."""
244	    return " ".join(str(section or "").strip().lower().split())
245	
246	
247	def _aspect_dedup_threshold() -> float:
248	    try:
249	        return float(os.getenv(
250	            "PG_OUTLINE_ASPECT_DEDUP_JACCARD", str(_ASPECT_DEDUP_JACCARD_THRESHOLD_DEFAULT),
251	        ))
252	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
253	        return _ASPECT_DEDUP_JACCARD_THRESHOLD_DEFAULT
254	
255	
256	# iter-4 P0-1 fix knobs (real-corpus proof the checklist only ever proposed a phantom
257	# "Summary Table" section under the locked 4-section DRB-72 deliverable, then correctly
258	# vetoed it, leaving zero genuine within-section gaps found): two mechanical (never-LLM)
259	# thresholds that let a checklist/agent-proposed section label that does not resolve to a
260	# current outline title EXACTLY still be recognized/routed instead of silently vetoed.
261	_SECTION_TITLE_FUZZY_JACCARD_DEFAULT = 0.5
262	_SECTION_REMAP_JACCARD_DEFAULT = 0.06
263	
264	
265	def _section_title_fuzzy_threshold() -> float:
266	    """Threshold for recognizing a proposed section label as the SAME section under a
267	    different/short wording (e.g. 'Summary Table' vs 'Summary Table: Application Cases,
268	    Impacts, and Risks by Industry/Occupation') — high bar, this is identity recognition."""
269	    return _env_float(
270	        "PG_OUTLINE_SECTION_TITLE_FUZZY_JACCARD", _SECTION_TITLE_FUZZY_JACCARD_DEFAULT,
271	    )
272	
273	
274	def _section_remap_jaccard_threshold() -> float:
275	    """Threshold for remapping a facet to the EXISTING section whose own topic (title+focus)
276	    it most overlaps, when no section in the outline is even fuzzily the SAME section — low
277	    bar by design: under a locked required-structure deliverable, a genuine content facet
278	    must live in SOME existing section, never invent a new one, and 'weight don't filter'
279	    (§-1.3) means routing beats discarding whenever there is ANY real signal."""
280	    return _env_float("PG_OUTLINE_SECTION_REMAP_JACCARD", _SECTION_REMAP_JACCARD_DEFAULT)
281	
282	
283	# iter-2 P0-1 fix: the checklist's anti-invention grounding gate. A candidate deficiency line
284	# must carry a verbatim quote from the research question; these two helpers are the mechanical
285	# (never-LLM) check that the quote is real, not a paraphrase or a hallucinated snippet.
286	_MIN_GROUNDING_QUOTE_WORDS = 2
287	
288	
289	_QUOTE_WRAP_CHARS = "\"'‘’“”‹›«»`"
290	
291	
292	def _normalize_for_quote_check(text: str) -> str:
293	    import re as _re
294	    stripped = str(text or "").strip().strip(_QUOTE_WRAP_CHARS).strip()
295	    return _re.sub(r"\s+", " ", stripped.lower())
296	
297	
298	def _quote_is_grounded(quote: str, question_norm: str) -> bool:
299	    """True iff ``quote`` (whitespace/case-normalized, and with any wrapping quote-mark
300	    PUNCTUATION the model added around its own answer stripped — iter-4 robustness fix: a
301	    model reply like the literal three characters ``"foo bar"`` around an otherwise-correct
302	    verbatim excerpt must not be mechanically rejected just because those wrapping marks are
303	    not themselves part of the source text) is a literal substring of the (already-normalized)
304	    research question AND carries at least ``_MIN_GROUNDING_QUOTE_WORDS`` words (a bare
305	    one-word quote like the subject's name is not a specific-enough justification for a
306	    claimed missing FACET)."""
307	    q = _normalize_for_quote_check(quote)
308	    if not q or len(q.split()) < _MIN_GROUNDING_QUOTE_WORDS:
309	        return False
310	    return q in question_norm
311

313	@dataclass
314	class GapTodo:
315	    section: str
316	    aspect: str
317	    needed_kind: str = "coverage"   # "coverage" | "numeric_rows" | "density"
318	    status: str = "PENDING"          # PENDING | IN_PROGRESS | COMPLETE | UNFILLED
319	    attempts: int = 0
320	    source: str = "checklist"        # checklist | tool_failure | agent
321	    disclosure: str = ""
322	
323	    def key(self) -> tuple[str, str]:
324	        return (str(self.section), str(self.aspect))
325	
326	
327	class GapLedger:
328	    """Dedup-by-(section,aspect) EXACT match, falling back to same-section token-overlap
329	    (Jaccard >= threshold) paraphrase collapse; per-aspect retry cap; UNFILLED + disclosed
330	    on exhaustion.
331	
332	    §-1.3.1 / §-1.1: this ledger is a ROUTING signal (what to search next), never a quality
333	    verdict about the corpus — it names todos, it does not score the pipeline's own output.
334	    """
335	
336	    def __init__(self, max_retries_per_aspect: Optional[int] = None):
337	        self._todos: dict[tuple[str, str], GapTodo] = {}
338	        self._max_retries = (
339	            max_retries_per_aspect
340	            if max_retries_per_aspect is not None
341	            else _gap_retries_per_aspect()
342	        )
343	
344	    def _find_paraphrase(self, section: str, aspect: str) -> Optional[GapTodo]:
345	        """Same-section, token-overlap match against an EXISTING todo (any status). Iter-2
346	        P1-1: collapses re-worded checklist gaps onto the one todo already tracking that
347	        aspect so the per-aspect retry cap actually bites."""
348	        canon_new = _canon_tokens(aspect)
349	        if not canon_new:
350	            return None
351	        threshold = _aspect_dedup_threshold()
352	        best: Optional[GapTodo] = None
353	        best_score = 0.0
354	        for (sec2, asp2), todo in self._todos.items():
355	            if sec2 != section:
356	                continue
357	            score = _jaccard(canon_new, _canon_tokens(asp2))
358	            if score >= threshold and score > best_score:
359	                best, best_score = todo, score
360	        return best
361	
362	    def add(
363	        self, section: str, aspect: str,
364	        needed_kind: str = "coverage", source: str = "checklist",
365	    ) -> GapTodo:
366	        section = str(section or "").strip()
367	        aspect = str(aspect or "").strip()
368	        key = (section, aspect)
369	        existing = self._todos.get(key)
370	        if existing is not None:
371	            return existing
372	        paraphrase = self._find_paraphrase(section, aspect)
373	        if paraphrase is not None:
374	            return paraphrase
375	        todo = GapTodo(
376	            section=section, aspect=aspect, needed_kind=needed_kind, source=source,
377	        )
378	        self._todos[key] = todo
379	        return todo
380	
381	    def add_unfillable(
382	        self, section: str, aspect: str, reason: str,
383	        needed_kind: str = "coverage", source: str = "checklist",
384	    ) -> GapTodo:
385	        """Iter-3 P0 fix (real-corpus degeneration — 'Summary Table' gap re-fired ~10x over
386	        945s while every fetched row stayed orphaned in the pool): a gap whose named section
387	        can NEVER be routed to retrieval — it does not match any current outline title, and
388	        under a locked required-structure deliverable there is no way to add a new section for
389	        it — is a STRUCTURAL limitation, not a content gap. Recording it UNFILLED immediately
390	        (never PENDING) means ``next_pending()`` never selects it and the decide-loop never
391	        fires ``search_more_evidence`` for it, so the loop cannot burn its turn/wall budget
392	        re-fetching the same unhomeable aspect under a slightly different checklist wording
393	        each round. Still disclosed (§-1.3 — no silent drop), just never retried."""
394	        todo = self.add(section=section, aspect=aspect, needed_kind=needed_kind, source=source)
395	        if todo.status not in ("UNFILLED", "COMPLETE"):
396	            todo.status = "UNFILLED"
397	            todo.disclosure = reason
398	        return todo
399	
400	    def get(self, section: str, aspect: str) -> Optional[GapTodo]:

401	        return self._todos.get((str(section or "").strip(), str(aspect or "").strip()))
402	
403	    def next_pending(self) -> Optional[GapTodo]:
404	        for todo in self._todos.values():
405	            if todo.status == "PENDING":
406	                return todo
407	        return None
408	
409	    def mark_in_progress(self, todo: GapTodo) -> None:
410	        todo.status = "IN_PROGRESS"
411	        todo.attempts += 1
412	
413	    def mark_complete(self, todo: GapTodo) -> None:
414	        todo.status = "COMPLETE"
415	
416	    def mark_retry_or_unfilled(self, todo: GapTodo, reason: str) -> None:
417	        """Per-aspect retry: PENDING again while attempts < cap; UNFILLED + disclosed at cap."""
418	        if todo.attempts >= self._max_retries:
419	            todo.status = "UNFILLED"
420	            todo.disclosure = reason
421	        else:
422	            todo.status = "PENDING"
423	
424	    @property
425	    def pending_count(self) -> int:
426	        return sum(1 for t in self._todos.values() if t.status == "PENDING")
427	
428	    @property
429	    def unfilled(self) -> list[GapTodo]:
430	        return [t for t in self._todos.values() if t.status == "UNFILLED"]
431	
432	    @property
433	    def all_todos(self) -> list[GapTodo]:
434	        return list(self._todos.values())
435	
436	    def as_list(self) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
437	        return [dataclasses.asdict(t) for t in self._todos.values()]
438	
439	    def summary_for_llm(self) -> str:
440	        if not self._todos:
441	            return "  (empty — no gaps identified yet)"
442	        lines = []
443	        for t in self._todos.values():
444	            lines.append(
445	                f"  [{t.status}] section={t.section!r} aspect={t.aspect!r} "
446	                f"kind={t.needed_kind} attempts={t.attempts}/{self._max_retries} source={t.source}"
447	            )
448	        return "\n".join(lines)
449

455	@dataclass
456	class OutlineWorkspace:
457	    research_question: str
458	    ev_store: dict[str, dict[str, Any]]              # cp2 rows keyed by evidence_id, S2-stamped
459	    outline_draft: list[Any] = field(default_factory=list)   # list[SectionPlan]
460	    gap_ledger: GapLedger = field(default_factory=GapLedger)
461	    notebook: Optional[AnalysisNotebook] = None
462	    basket_menu: Any = None                            # outline_digest menu (or None)
463	    budgets: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
464	    disclosures: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
465	    # W4: successful execute_python payloads, so a computed value is no longer discarded at
466	    # every exit. EXPLORATORY / NOT RENDERABLE — every row carries renderable=False +
467	    # bar_reason (see _compute_result_check). Telemetry + planner provenance only; this is
468	    # NOT a render path and never reaches a writer prompt.
469	    compute_results: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
470	    # W3-render (2026-07-11): run-scoped registry of VERIFIED quantified models, keyed by
471	    # (model_id, spec_hash) exactly as ``strict_verify(quantified_models=...)`` expects. This is
472	    # the ONLY compute-render surface: a number lands here solely by re-derivation through the
473	    # fail-closed ModelSpec lane (verified_compute.run_verified_compute -> build_quantified_spec ->
474	    # execute_quantified_model), and renders solely via its ``[#calc:model:hash:field]`` token,
475	    # which strict_verify force-routes to ``verify_modeled_atom``. Exploratory execute_python output
476	    # (compute_results above, renderable=False) NEVER enters this dict — that separation is the
477	    # faithfulness invariant (a derived number can never reach the [#ev]/[CITE] render path).
478	    quantified_models: dict = field(default_factory=dict)  # (model_id, spec_hash) -> QuantifiedResult
479	    # MOAT DETERMINISTIC EMISSION (2026-07-11): the render-ready [#calc:] claim SENTENCES the agent
480	    # actually derived through ``verified_compute``, so the FULL-CORPUS composer can inject them
481	    # into the target section body deterministically (an LLM writer cannot be trusted to copy an
482	    # unguessable spec_hash verbatim). Each record:
483	    #   {"section": <target section title or "" for auto-home>, "sentence": <render_sentence>,
484	    #    "calc_token": <[#calc:model:hash:field]>, "input_ev_ids": [<ev_id>, ...]}
485	    # Populated by the ``verified_compute`` tool alongside the ``quantified_models`` registration.
486	    # The sentence is NON-RENDERING on its own: it only survives strict_verify if that same
487	    # registry verifies its token (verify_modeled_atom), so emission stays fail-closed and a
488	    # derived number STILL cannot reach the [#ev:]/[CITE:] path.
489	    computed_claims: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
490	    turn: int = 0
491	    started_monotonic: float = field(default_factory=time.monotonic)
492	    checkpoint_dir: Optional[str] = None
493	    total_input_tokens: int = 0
494	    total_output_tokens: int = 0
495	    # Iter-2 fix: the deliverable's REQUIRED section titles (empty => no structure lock, the
496	    # common case for the free-form acceptance/real-corpus runs). When non-empty, the outline's
497	    # section STRUCTURE is governed by the caller (S4 ORCH-2 PUSH 1 / `_call_outline`'s own
498	    # `required_sections` conform already enforced this at SEED time) and this module must never
499	    # silently add a 5th section behind the caller's back — see `_tool_update_outline` and the
500	    # auto-assign fallback in `OutlineAgent._execute_tool`.
501	    required_titles: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
502	    # RACE-FLOOR fix (2026-07-12, this wheel): the corpus-DERIVED thematic-coverage floor. The SEED
503	    # outline (built by ``_call_outline`` from the evidence/baskets — query-agnostic, NOT a hardcoded
504	    # task-72 structure) is the corpus's own theme decomposition. Two byte-identical renders diverged
505	    # (RACE 0.4447 vs 0.3518) because the agentic loop, over more turns, freely issued ``merge`` ops
506	    # that COLLAPSED distinct corpus themes (e.g. Wage-Inequality, Policy) into fewer, thinner
507	    # sections — pure non-determinism that thinned comprehensiveness. This floor caps NET thematic
508	    # reduction: the loop may still split / add / retitle / reassign / search (all coverage-improving),
509	    # but a ``merge`` that would drop the live section count below the seed count is DEFERRED as a
510	    # disclosed no-op. GENERAL (floor = seed's own section count), faithfulness-NEUTRAL (pure
511	    # structural placement; strict_verify / NLI / [#calc] lane untouched). 0 => no floor (legacy).
512	    min_sections: int = 0
513	    # Iter-3 P0 fix (real-corpus THINNED run: 8 search_more_evidence calls, 31 genuinely NEW
514	    # fetched rows, ZERO landed in any required section — the whole retrieval budget was spent
515	    # re-chasing the SAME unhomeable section label, e.g. "Summary Table", under 8 differently
516	    # worded aspects that each failed the (section,aspect) paraphrase dedup because the ASPECT
517	    # text varied enough while the SECTION stayed constant). Once a section label is proven
518	    # unhomeable (checklist- or agent-initiated), it is banked here (normalized) and vetoed —
519	    # cheaply, before any real fetch — for the rest of the run, regardless of aspect rewording.
520	    unhomeable_sections: set = field(default_factory=set)
521	    # S3 FEED (Research Planning Gate ↔ outline agent seam). All default to the
522	    # inert/empty value so a workspace built without a gate artifact (every
523	    # champion caller today) is BYTE-IDENTICAL: no scope threaded into the gap
524	    # search, no term-ledger validation on update_outline, no seeded gaps.
525	    #
526	    # ``gate_research_frame`` / ``gate_gap_protocol``: THE BUG FIX. The gate's
527	    # retrieval scope (the SAME ``research_frame`` + anchor ``protocol`` used by
528	    # initial retrieval), threaded into ``_tool_search_more_evidence``'s
529	    # ``run_live_retrieval`` call so the outline-stage gap search stays IN SCOPE
530	    # instead of running unscoped (both designers found this bug). None => the
531	    # champion ``run_live_retrieval`` defaults (research_frame=None/protocol=None).
532	    gate_research_frame: Any = None
533	    gate_gap_protocol: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None
534	    # ``contract_hash``: the pinned artifact's contract SHA (provenance only — the
535	    # resolved outline records which gate seed it refined). "" => no gate.
536	    contract_hash: str = ""
537	    # ``term_ledger``: {binding_term_owners, locked_titles, ordered_titles,
538	    # binding_term_ids}. When non-empty, ``_tool_update_outline`` rejects a
539	    # revision that drops an explicit title lock or the LAST owner of a binding
540	    # term (build via ``outline_gate_feed.build_term_ledger``). Empty => no ledger
541	    # validation (champion behavior).
542	    term_ledger: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
543	    _checkpoint_seq: int = 0
544	
545	    def next_ev_offset(self) -> int:
546	        """Highest numeric suffix across current ev_ids, +1. Rows that don't parse as
547	        ``ev_<digits>`` are ignored for the offset computation (never crash the offset)."""
548	        mx = -1
549	        for eid in self.ev_store.keys():
550	            tail = str(eid).rsplit("_", 1)[-1]
551	            if tail.isdigit():
552	                mx = max(mx, int(tail))
553	        return mx + 1
554	
555	    def existing_urls(self) -> set[str]:
556	        urls: set[str] = set()
557	        for row in self.ev_store.values():
558	            if isinstance(row, dict):
559	                u = str(row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "").strip()
560	                if u:
561	                    urls.add(u)
562	        return urls
563	
564	    def disclose(self, text: str) -> None:
565	        self.disclosures.append(text)
566	        logger.info("[outline_agent] %s", text)
567	
568	    def elapsed_seconds(self) -> float:
569	        return time.monotonic() - self.started_monotonic
570	
571	    def checkpoint(self, event: str) -> None:
572	        """Best-effort checkpoint-after-every-mutation (crash-resume). NEVER raises — a
573	        checkpoint failure must not abort the loop (mirrors ``write_cp4_outline_snapshot``'s
574	        own best-effort contract one layer up)."""
575	        if not self.checkpoint_dir:
576	            return
577	        try:
578	            from src.polaris_graph.generator.outline_checkpoint import (  # noqa: PLC0415
579	                build_cp4_payload, write_cp4_outline_snapshot,
580	            )
581	            self._checkpoint_seq += 1
582	            plans_dicts = [_plan_to_dict(p) for p in self.outline_draft]
583	            payload = build_cp4_payload(
584	                question_sha="",
585	                upstream=None,
586	                run_config_sha="",
587	                flag_slate={"PG_OUTLINE_AGENT": "1", "event": str(event)},
588	                adjustments_applied=None,
589	                final_plans=plans_dicts,
590	                revision_audit={
591	                    "gap_ledger": self.gap_ledger.as_list(),
592	                    "disclosures": list(self.disclosures),
593	                    "turn": self.turn,
594	                    "checkpoint_seq": self._checkpoint_seq,
595	                    "notebook_steps": (
596	                        [
597	                            {
598	                                "tool_name": s.tool_name,
599	                                "reasoning": s.reasoning[:200],
600	                                "success": s.result.success,
601	                            }
602	                            for s in self.notebook.steps
603	                        ] if self.notebook is not None else []
604	                    ),
605	                },
606	                digest_stats={
607	                    "ev_store_size": len(self.ev_store),
608	                    "elapsed_seconds": round(self.elapsed_seconds(), 1),
609	                },
610	            )
611	            write_cp4_outline_snapshot(self.checkpoint_dir, payload)
612	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — checkpoint is best-effort, never fatal
613	            logger.warning("[outline_agent] checkpoint write skipped (fail-open): %s", exc)
614

616	def _plan_field(plan: Any, key: str, default: Any = "") -> Any:
617	    """Iter-2 fix (found via offline smoke test, not in the original Fable P0/P1 list): plans in
618	    ``workspace.outline_draft`` are NOT a uniform type across the loop's lifetime — the SEED plans
619	    from ``_call_outline`` are ``SectionPlan`` dataclass instances, but ``outline_revise.apply_
620	    revision_ops`` (which BOTH ``update_outline`` and the new iter-2 auto-assign call after every
621	    successful search) always returns plain ``dict`` plans (``RevisionApplyResult.new_plans:
622	    list[dict]``). A bare ``getattr(p, 'title', default)`` silently returns ``default`` for a dict
623	    (dicts have no attribute access) — so the FIRST time any update_outline/auto-assign op ran,
624	    every downstream title lookup in THIS module (decide's outline summary, the checklist's
625	    section block, ``inspect_basket``'s assignment check, auto-assign's own section-title
626	    resolver) would have silently gone blank, breaking the very mutation this iteration exists to
627	    fix. Mirrors ``outline_revise._plan_get`` exactly (dict-or-attribute duck typing) — reproduced
628	    here rather than imported to keep this module's OFF-path import graph unchanged (LAW VI/V:
629	    small, side-effect-free helper; not worth a cross-module coupling for 4 lines)."""
630	    if isinstance(plan, dict):
631	        return plan.get(key, default)
632	    return getattr(plan, key, default)
633	
634	
635	def _plan_to_dict(plan: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
636	    if isinstance(plan, dict):
637	        return dict(plan)
638	    if dataclasses.is_dataclass(plan):
639	        return dataclasses.asdict(plan)
640	    return {
641	        "title": _plan_field(plan, "title", ""),
642	        "focus": _plan_field(plan, "focus", ""),
643	        "ev_ids": list(_plan_field(plan, "ev_ids", []) or []),
644	        "basket_ids": list(_plan_field(plan, "basket_ids", []) or []),
645	        "archetype": _plan_field(plan, "archetype", ""),
646	        "undersupplied": _plan_field(plan, "undersupplied", False),
647	    }
648

650	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
651	# search_more_evidence — the retrieval pipe as a tool (the sharp seam)
652	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
653	
654	def _sync_llm_bridge(model: str, max_tokens: int) -> Callable[[str], str]:
655	    """Build a SYNCHRONOUS ``str -> str`` callable bridging into the async
656	    ``OpenRouterClient.generate`` — the exact thread+contextvars pattern
657	    ``run_honest_sweep_r3.py``'s ``_topic_llm`` uses (that module is not importable here without
658	    dragging in the whole sweep script, so the ~20-line bridge is reproduced, not re-derived).
659	    Required because ``classify_topic_relevance`` (topic_relevance_gate.py) takes a sync
660	    callable and this module runs inside an ALREADY-RUNNING asyncio loop (the outline agent's
661	    own loop), where a bare ``asyncio.run()`` would raise."""
662	
663	    def _llm(prompt: str) -> str:
664	        import concurrent.futures as _futures
665	        import contextvars as _contextvars
666	
667	        async def _run() -> str:
668	            from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: PLC0415
669	            client = OpenRouterClient(model=model)
670	            try:
671	                resp = await client.generate(prompt=prompt, max_tokens=max_tokens, temperature=0.0)
672	                return (resp.content or "").strip()
673	            finally:
674	                if hasattr(client, "close"):
675	                    try:
676	                        await client.close()
677	                    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — client close is best-effort
678	                        pass
679	
680	        parent_ctx = _contextvars.copy_context()
681	
682	        def _worker() -> str:
683	            return parent_ctx.run(lambda: asyncio.run(_run()))
684	
685	        with _futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) as pool:
686	            return pool.submit(_worker).result()
687	
688	    return _llm
689	
690	
691	async def _tool_search_more_evidence(
692	    workspace: OutlineWorkspace,
693	    agent_model: str,
694	    *,
695	    section: str = "",
696	    aspect: str = "",
697	    domain: Optional[str] = None,
698	    retrieval_deadline_monotonic: Optional[float] = None,
699	    **_ignored: Any,
700	) -> ToolResult:
701	    """The retrieval pipe as a ReAct tool. Steps (design §): per-todo query derivation
702	    (scope-anchored, ``_is_status_leak`` guard) -> ``run_live_retrieval(anchor_seed=False)`` ->
703	    ``merge_retrieval_results`` -> offset-renumber (hard assert) + URL-dedup vs existing ->
704	    S2 stamp pass (chrome delete + topic-judge fail-open) -> fold into ``ev_store`` -> disclosed
705	    ``ToolResult``. One retrieval in flight at a time (§8.4 — this coroutine is awaited, never
706	    fired concurrently by the driver)."""
707	    aspect_text = str(aspect or "").strip() or str(section or "").strip()
708	    if not aspect_text:
709	        return ToolResult(
710	            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
711	            markdown="search_more_evidence requires a `section` or `aspect`.",
712	            error="missing_aspect",
713	        )
714	
715	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.fs_researcher_query_gen import (  # noqa: PLC0415
716	        _is_status_leak,
717	    )
718	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: PLC0415
719	
720	    query_derive_max_tokens = _env_int(
721	        "PG_OUTLINE_QUERY_DERIVE_MAX_TOKENS", PG_OUTLINE_QUERY_DERIVE_MAX_TOKENS_DEFAULT,
722	    )
723	    client = OpenRouterClient(model=agent_model)
724	    try:
725	        derive_prompt = (
726	            "Write ONE web-search query for the SUB-TOPIC below, kept STRICTLY within the "
727	            "scope of the RESEARCH QUESTION (carry its subject, domain and key entities; do "
728	            "NOT broaden the query into the sub-topic's generic field). Query only, one line, "
729	            "no quotes.\n\n"
730	            f"RESEARCH QUESTION:\n{workspace.research_question}\n\nSUB-TOPIC:\n{aspect_text}"
731	        )
732	        try:
733	            # Iter-6 P0 fix: same reasoning-pool bound as the checklist/decide calls above —
734	            # this call already fails open on ANY exception (including a truncation), but an
735	            # unbounded-effort reasoning prelude on a dense corpus still needlessly degrades a
736	            # one-line query derivation to the raw aspect text every time; bounding it means the
737	            # derived (scope-anchored) query actually gets used instead of the coarser fallback.
738	            resp = await client.generate(
739	                prompt=derive_prompt, max_tokens=query_derive_max_tokens, temperature=0.2,
740	                reasoning_max_tokens=_reasoning_max_tokens(),
741	            )
742	            derived = (resp.content or "").strip().splitlines()[0].strip().strip('"') if (
743	                resp.content or ""
744	            ).strip() else ""
745	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — query derivation is a small text call; degrade to raw aspect
746	            logger.warning("[outline_agent] query derivation failed, using raw aspect: %s", exc)
747	            derived = ""
748	    finally:
749	        if hasattr(client, "close"):
750	            try:
751	                await client.close()
752	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
753	                pass
754	
755	    query = derived or aspect_text
756	    if _is_status_leak(query, workspace.research_question):
757	        return ToolResult(
758	            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
759	            markdown=f"Derived query was a status-leak, aborted: {query[:120]!r}",
760	            error="status_leak_aborted",
761	        )
762	
763	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.live_retriever import (  # noqa: PLC0415
764	        LiveRetrievalResult, run_live_retrieval,
765	    )
766	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.fs_researcher_query_gen import (  # noqa: PLC0415
767	        merge_retrieval_results,
768	    )
769	
770	    t0 = time.monotonic()
771	    try:
772	        # BUG FOUND IN W1 LIVE ACCEPTANCE RUN (fixed same-iter): ``anchor_seed=False`` makes
773	        # ``run_live_retrieval`` build ``all_queries`` from ``amplified_queries`` ONLY (the
774	        # verbatim ``research_question`` is NOT auto-fired when the anchor is suppressed — see
775	        # the function's own docstring). The gap query MUST be passed via ``amplified_queries``;
776	        # ``research_question`` stays the WORKSPACE question (scope-validator anchor text) so a
777	        # scoped sub-query is validated against the real topic, not against itself.
778	        # S3 BUG FIX (both designers): thread the gate's retrieval SCOPE into the
779	        # outline-stage gap search. Before this, ``research_frame`` / ``protocol``
780	        # were silently dropped here, so a gap search run under a hard journal /
781	        # English / jurisdiction contract went out UNSCOPED and could dilute a
782	        # correctly-scoped first pass. When the workspace carries no gate scope
783	        # (every champion caller — both stay None/None), this is byte-identical to
784	        # the champion ``run_live_retrieval`` defaults. A HARD-scope frame is never
785	        # a post-fetch filter here: it ROUTES the SAME backends/anchor the initial
786	        # retrieval used, so the gap query keeps that scope from its first fetch.
787	        raw_result = await asyncio.to_thread(
788	            run_live_retrieval,
789	            research_question=workspace.research_question,
790	            amplified_queries=[query],
791	            domain=domain,
792	            anchor_seed=False,
793	            retrieval_deadline_monotonic=retrieval_deadline_monotonic,
794	            research_frame=workspace.gate_research_frame,
795	            protocol=workspace.gate_gap_protocol,
796	        )
797	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a single retrieval round must not crash the loop
798	        return ToolResult(
799	            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
800	            markdown=f"run_live_retrieval raised for query {query!r}: {exc}",

801	            error=str(exc)[:500],
802	        )
803	    elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0
804	
805	    merged = merge_retrieval_results([raw_result], LiveRetrievalResult)
806	    fetched_rows = list(getattr(merged, "evidence_rows", None) or [])
807	    n_fetched_of = f"{len(fetched_rows)} of {getattr(merged, 'candidates_total', len(fetched_rows))}"
808	
809	    # THE shared fold-in seam (outline/_fold_in.py): url-dedup -> offset-renumber (hard
810	    # id-collision assert) -> S2 stamp/delete -> insert. fetch_url re-enters through the same unit.
811	    fold = await fold_in_fetched_rows(
812	        workspace, fetched_rows,
813	        research_question=workspace.research_question, agent_model=agent_model,
814	    )
815	    kept_rows = fold.kept_rows
816	    n_kept = fold.n_kept
817	    n_deleted = fold.n_deleted
818	    disclosure = (
819	        f"search_more_evidence[{aspect_text[:60]!r}] query={query!r} fetched {n_fetched_of}, "
820	        f"url-dup dropped {fold.n_url_dup}, kept {n_kept}, deleted {n_deleted} "
821	        f"(chrome={len(fold.deleted_chrome)}, off-topic={len(fold.deleted_offtopic)}) in {elapsed:.1f}s"
822	    )
823	    workspace.disclose(disclosure)
824	
825	    md_lines = [f"**{disclosure}**", ""]
826	    for row in kept_rows[:10]:
827	        md_lines.append(
828	            f"- {row.get('evidence_id')}: {str(row.get('title') or '')[:80]} "
829	            f"[CITE:{row.get('evidence_id')}]"
830	        )
831	    if len(kept_rows) > 10:
832	        md_lines.append(f"- ... and {len(kept_rows) - 10} more")
833	
834	    return ToolResult(
835	        success=n_kept > 0,
836	        tool_name="search_more_evidence",
837	        markdown="\n".join(md_lines),
838	        source_evidence_ids=[r["evidence_id"] for r in kept_rows],
839	        error=("" if n_kept > 0 else "no_new_evidence_survived_screen"),
840	    )

848	    registry: "ToolRegistry", *, name: str = "", category: str = "", **_ignored: Any,
849	) -> ToolResult:
850	    """Meta-tool: return the FULL spec (description + params) of ONE tool by ``name``, or a one-line
851	    listing of every tool in ``category``. The on-demand discovery path for the decide menu's
852	    collapsed non-core index (redesign 'Scaling the decide step past 20 tools'). No LLM, no network."""
853	    nm = str(name or "").strip()
854	    cat = str(category or "").strip().lower()
855	    if nm:
856	        tool = registry.get_tool(nm)

862	        md = "\n".join(registry._describe_full(tool, True))  # noqa: SLF001 — same package intent
863	        return ToolResult(success=True, tool_name="list_tools", markdown=md)
864	    matches = [
865	        t for t in (registry.get_tool(n) for n in registry.available_tools(True))
866	        if t is not None and (not cat or cat in [str(x).lower() for x in (t.tags or [])])
867	    ]
868	    if not matches:

872	            error="no_tools_in_category",
873	        )
874	    lines = [f"**{len(matches)} tool(s)"
875	             f"{f' in [{cat}]' if cat else ''}:**"]
876	    for t in sorted(matches, key=lambda x: x.name):
877	        lines.append(f"- {t.name}: {t.description.split('. ')[0][:100]}")
878	    return ToolResult(success=True, tool_name="list_tools", markdown="\n".join(lines))

920	async def _tool_update_outline(
921	    workspace: OutlineWorkspace, *, ops: Any = None, **_ignored: Any,
922	) -> ToolResult:
923	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.outline_revise import (  # noqa: PLC0415
924	        apply_revision_ops, parse_revision_ops,
925	    )
926	
927	    if ops is None:
928	        return ToolResult(
929	            success=False, tool_name="update_outline",
930	            markdown="update_outline requires `ops` (a revision op list).",
931	            error="missing_ops",
932	        )
933	    allowed_ev_ids = set(workspace.ev_store.keys())
934	    plan_titles = [str(_plan_field(p, "title", "")) for p in workspace.outline_draft]
935	    raw = ops if isinstance(ops, (dict, str)) else {"ops": ops}
936	    parse_result = parse_revision_ops(
937	        raw, allowed_ev_ids=allowed_ev_ids, plan_titles=plan_titles,
938	    )
939	    if parse_result.parse_failed or not parse_result.ops:
940	        return ToolResult(
941	            success=False, tool_name="update_outline",
942	            markdown=(
943	                f"No ops applied (parse_failed={parse_result.parse_failed}, "
944	                f"rejected={parse_result.rejected})."
945	            ),
946	            error="no_ops_applied",
947	        )
948	
949	    # Iter-2 fix: thread the deliverable's required-structure lock (if any) into apply — mirrors
950	    # `scripts/orchestrator_lab/outline_lab.py`'s `_required` threading. Without this, a
951	    # required_sections-governed outline (e.g. the DRB-72 4-section canonical structure) could
952	    # silently grow a 5th section via an agent-issued `add` op, breaking the caller's exact-N-in-
953	    # order structural contract that `_call_outline` already conformed the seed to.
954	    # Snapshot the pre-op section titles so the S3 term-ledger guard can compare
955	    # them against the post-op set (below). Cheap; only used when a ledger exists.
956	    _titles_before = [str(_plan_field(p, "title", "")) for p in workspace.outline_draft]
957	
958	    apply_result = apply_revision_ops(
959	        workspace.outline_draft, parse_result,
960	        required_titles=(workspace.required_titles or None),
961	        min_sections=(workspace.min_sections or 0),
962	    )
963	
964	    # ── S3 term-ledger guard: reject a revision that drops an EXPLICIT title lock
965	    # or the LAST owner of a binding term (design §6). This is a SECOND, coverage-
966	    # aware guard on top of ``apply_revision_ops``'s ``required_titles`` structural
967	    # lock: the required-titles lock protects an exact-N-in-order structure, while
968	    # the term ledger protects an explicit heading / a binding requirement's last
969	    # home even when no full structure lock is present. When the workspace carries
970	    # no ledger (every champion caller — ``term_ledger`` empty) this is a no-op and
971	    # byte-identical. On a violation the WHOLE op set is refused (the seed stands),
972	    # disclosed, never silently applied — the loop may retry a compliant revision.
973	    if workspace.term_ledger:
974	        try:
975	            from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_gate_feed import (  # noqa: PLC0415
976	                validate_revision_against_ledger,
977	            )
978	            _titles_after = [str(d.get("title", "")) for d in apply_result.new_plans]
979	            _violations = validate_revision_against_ledger(
980	                workspace.term_ledger,
981	                titles_before=_titles_before,
982	                titles_after=_titles_after,
983	            )
984	        except Exception as _led_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — guard is additive, never fatal
985	            _violations = []
986	            logger.warning(
987	                "[outline_agent] S3 ledger guard skipped (fail-open): %s", _led_exc,
988	            )
989	        if _violations:
990	            workspace.disclose(
991	                "update_outline REFUSED: revision would violate the pinned contract "
992	                f"term ledger ({'; '.join(_violations)}) — keeping the prior outline"
993	            )
994	            return ToolResult(
995	                success=False, tool_name="update_outline",
996	                markdown=(
997	                    "No ops applied — the revision would drop an explicit title lock "
998	                    "or the last owner of a binding contract term: "
999	                    + "; ".join(_violations)
1000	                ),
1001	                error="contract_term_ledger_violation",
1002	            )
1003	    # Iter-2 fix (found via offline smoke test): ``apply_revision_ops`` ALWAYS returns
1004	    # ``list[dict]`` (``outline_revise`` is dict-native; it is not currently wired into
1005	    # production composition at all — grep confirms its only callers before this module were
1006	    # the offline ``outline_lab.py`` harness). But THIS module's ``workspace.outline_draft`` is
1007	    # handed straight back to ``generate_multi_section_report`` as ``parse_result.plans``, and
1008	    # every downstream composition site (M-44 primary injection, section-budget code, the
1009	    # manifest's ``[p.title for p in multi.outline]``, etc.) does direct ``.title``/``.ev_ids``
1010	    # ATTRIBUTE access on real ``SectionPlan`` dataclass instances. Handing dicts downstream
1011	    # would ``AttributeError`` the first real composition run the moment any update_outline (or
1012	    # the iter-2 auto-assign) op fires. Reconcile back to ``SectionPlan`` HERE — the one seam
1013	    # where outline_revise's dict world meets the rest of the pipeline's dataclass world — so
1014	    # every OTHER call site in this module (and every downstream consumer) can keep doing plain
1015	    # ``SectionPlan`` attribute access unconditionally.
1016	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import SectionPlan  # noqa: PLC0415
1017	    workspace.outline_draft = [
1018	        SectionPlan(
1019	            title=str(d.get("title", "")),
1020	            focus=str(d.get("focus", "")),
1021	            ev_ids=list(d.get("ev_ids", []) or []),
1022	            archetype=str(d.get("archetype", "")),
1023	            undersupplied=bool(d.get("undersupplied", False)),
1024	            basket_ids=list(d.get("basket_ids", []) or []),
1025	        )
1026	        for d in apply_result.new_plans
1027	    ]
1028	
1029	    # Route the reviser's gap_queries into the shared gap ledger (design §).
1030	    for gq in (parse_result.gap_queries or []):
1031	        workspace.gap_ledger.add(
1032	            section="(unassigned)", aspect=str(gq), needed_kind="coverage", source="agent",
1033	        )
1034	
1035	    md = (
1036	        f"Applied {len(apply_result.applied_ops)} op(s); recompose={apply_result.recompose_titles}; "
1037	        f"kept={len(apply_result.kept_titles)}; rejected={len(parse_result.rejected)}; "
1038	        f"deferred={len(apply_result.deferred_ops)}; gap_queries routed to ledger: "
1039	        f"{len(parse_result.gap_queries or [])}"
1040	    )
1041	    workspace.disclose(f"update_outline: {md}")
1042	    return ToolResult(success=True, tool_name="update_outline", markdown=md)

1053	class OutlineAgent:
1054	    """The per-turn decide/execute loop over the outline workspace. Mirrors the SHAPE of
1055	    ``ReactAnalysisAgent._run_react`` (react_agent.py:690) — decide -> execute -> record ->
1056	    check-stop — without inheriting from it (design: WIRING, not building)."""

1061	        *,
1062	        agent_model: Optional[str] = None,
1063	        max_turns: Optional[int] = None,
1064	        wall_seconds: Optional[int] = None,
1065	        domain: Optional[str] = None,
1066	        retrieval_deadline_monotonic: Optional[float] = None,
1067	    ):
1068	        self.workspace = workspace
1069	        self.agent_model = agent_model or outliner_agent_model()
1070	        self.max_turns = max_turns if max_turns is not None else _max_turns()
1071	        self.wall_seconds = wall_seconds if wall_seconds is not None else _wall_seconds()
1072	        self.domain = domain
1073	        # Iter-2 P1-3 fix: the OUTER while-loop only checked the wall BETWEEN turns, so a
1074	        # single in-flight ``search_more_evidence`` call could itself run well past the wall
1075	        # (observed: 218s overshoot). ``run_live_retrieval`` already has its own internal
1076	        # mid-fetch wall defense (WALL-03, ``retrieval_deadline_monotonic``) but nothing wired
1077	        # it in — the parameter was accepted here and silently stayed ``None`` (unbounded) on
1078	        # every call. Default it from the SAME wall the outer loop uses, anchored at workspace
1079	        # start, so the in-flight fetch/enrich/classify sub-waits inside a single search call
1080	        # are ALSO bounded by the agent's own wall. An explicit override (e.g. a shared
1081	        # pipeline-wide deadline) still wins.
1082	        self.retrieval_deadline_monotonic = (
1083	            retrieval_deadline_monotonic
1084	            if retrieval_deadline_monotonic is not None
1085	            else workspace.started_monotonic + self.wall_seconds
1086	        )
1087	        self.registry: ToolRegistry = self._build_registry()

1090	                query=workspace.research_question, evidence_ids=list(workspace.ev_store.keys()),

1208	    def _best_remap_title(self, aspect: str) -> Optional[str]:
1209	        """Iter-4 P0-1 fix: when a proposed section label matches NO current outline title —
1210	        not even fuzzily via ``_resolve_section_title`` — do not treat the underlying facet as
1211	        automatically unhomeable. On the real DRB-72 AI-labor corpus the checklist's ONLY
1212	        candidate gap was a phantom 'Summary Table' section describing facets (specific
1213	        industries/occupations, impacts, risks) that are each genuinely topical to one of the
1214	        FOUR LOCKED sections the deliverable actually has — vetoing the whole line as a
1215	        'deliverable-level formatting request' discarded real, retrievable content gaps
1216	        instead of routing them. Mechanical (never-LLM) token-overlap: score the aspect text
1217	        against each existing section's OWN topic (title + focus, since focus was itself
1218	        derived from the research question by the seed outline call, so this stays
1219	        question-agnostic — no hardcoded domain vocabulary). This is a ROUTING decision, not a
1220	        quality verdict about the corpus (§-1.3.1 — 'weight/route, don't drop'). Returns
1221	        ``None`` (caller falls back to UNFILLED, honestly disclosed) if nothing clears the
1222	        (deliberately low) floor."""
1223	        aspect_canon = _canon_tokens(aspect)
1224	        if not aspect_canon:
1225	            return None
1226	        threshold = _section_remap_jaccard_threshold()
1227	        best_title: Optional[str] = None
1228	        best_score = 0.0
1229	        for p in self.workspace.outline_draft:
1230	            title = str(_plan_field(p, "title", "") or "").strip()
1231	            if not title:
1232	                continue
1233	            focus = str(_plan_field(p, "focus", "") or "")
1234	            score = _jaccard(aspect_canon, _canon_tokens(f"{title} {focus}"))
1235	            if score > best_score:
1236	                best_title, best_score = title, score
1237	        if best_title is not None and best_score >= threshold:
1238	            return best_title
1239	        return None

1623	    def _tool_failure_gap_check(self, step: AnalysisStep) -> None:
1624	        """Deterministic: empty comparison / zero datapoints / empty SQL / <2 studies -> auto
1625	        todo. This is a ROUTING signal from a mechanical check (empty result), not a quality
1626	        verdict about the corpus (§-1.1 — the mechanical check here fires a SEARCH action, it
1627	        never states a quality number)."""
1628	        result = step.result
1629	        if step.tool_name in (
1630	            "statistical_summary", "comparison_table", "meta_analysis", "query_evidence_sql",
1631	        ):
1632	            empty = (
1633	                not result.success
1634	                or (not result.data_points_produced and not result.statistics)
1635	            )
1636	            if empty:
1637	                self.workspace.gap_ledger.add(
1638	                    section="(unassigned)",
1639	                    aspect=f"{step.tool_name} returned no usable rows — need more numeric data",
1640	                    needed_kind="numeric_rows", source="tool_failure",
1641	                )
1642	        # W3 (2026-07-11): a FAILED compute was previously invisible here — the agent moved on with
1643	        # no record that the number it wanted was never derived. Record it UNFILLABLE, not PENDING:
1644	        # a compute failure is NOT retrievable, and a PENDING "(unassigned)" todo is routed to
1645	        # search_more_evidence (decide rule 1, :1182) — which burns real web fetches on an error
1646	        # string and can auto-assign a section literally titled "(unassigned)" (:1681). add_unfillable
1647	        # lands it UNFILLED + disclosed immediately and it is never selected by next_pending().
1648	        elif step.tool_name == "execute_python" and not result.success:
1649	            # section="(compute)", NOT "(unassigned)": add_unfillable delegates to add(), which
1650	            # paraphrase-collapses (Jaccard) against SAME-SECTION todos of ANY status. The
1651	            # retrievable numeric_rows tool-failure gaps above also live in "(unassigned)", so a
1652	            # reworded compute-failure aspect could collapse onto a genuine PENDING todo and flip it
1653	            # to UNFILLED — silently killing a real search. A distinct section label removes the
1654	            # collision surface; the todo is UNFILLED so the label is never routed anywhere.
1655	            self.workspace.gap_ledger.add_unfillable(
1656	                section="(compute)",
1657	                aspect=(
1658	                    f"execute_python failed ({result.error or 'unknown error'}) — the value it was "
1659	                    f"asked to derive was NOT computed"
1660	                ),
1661	                reason="compute failure is not retrievable — no web fetch can fill it",
1662	                needed_kind="computed_value", source="tool_failure",
1663	            )

1665	    def _compute_result_check(self, step: AnalysisStep) -> None:
1666	        """W4 (2026-07-11): a SUCCESSFUL compute must be DISCLOSED and CARRIED, never silently
1667	        dropped — and must NOT thereby become a render path.
1668	
1669	        Before W4 a successful ``execute_python`` was the worst of both worlds: success meant
1670	        ``_tool_failure_gap_check`` recorded NO gap (so nothing was disclosed), while the value it
1671	        computed was discarded at every exit (``summary_for_llm`` stripped it; the cp4 checkpoint
1672	        keeps only tool_name/reasoning/success; the entry-point return carried no notebook payload).
1673	        An UNDISCLOSED UNREACHABLE RESULT — the tool burned a turn + an OpenRouterClient + a sandbox
1674	        exec for zero observable effect, i.e. it was strictly net-NEGATIVE.
1675	
1676	        What this does: records the computed statistics on the workspace (so they survive the exit,
1677	        see :2049 ``notebook_compute``) and DISCLOSES them as EXPLORATORY.
1678	
1679	        What this deliberately does NOT do (binding invariant, docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md):
1680	        it does not stamp these numbers ``[#calc:]`` and does not hand them to the renderer.
1681	        Exploratory ``execute_python`` output is BARRED from rendering. The verified lane
1682	        (``tradeoff_modeler.ModelSpec`` -> ``quantified_analysis.execute_quantified_model``) accepts
1683	        only ``SourcedInput``s carrying an evidence span (``ev_id`` + ``raw_literal`` +
1684	        ``literal_start``/``literal_end``) and RE-DERIVES the number by re-executing a validated
1685	        formula, pinning ``display_value``; ``verify_modeled_atom`` then re-checks the rendered digits
1686	        against that re-execution. A bare ``{"npv": 1234567.89}`` returned by an LLM-written script
1687	        has no evidence span and no re-derivable formula, so wrapping it in a ModelSpec would FORGE
1688	        provenance through the only hard gate. The number therefore stays planner-facing until the
1689	        verified lane derives it independently; the disclosure below is what keeps that honest
1690	        instead of silent.
1691	        """
1692	        result = step.result
1693	        if step.tool_name not in _CODEGEN_TOOLS or not result.success:
1694	            return
1695	        stats = result.statistics if isinstance(result.statistics, dict) else {}
1696	        if not stats:
1697	            return
1698	
1699	        self.workspace.compute_results.append({
1700	            "turn": step.step_number,
1701	            "tool_name": step.tool_name,
1702	            "reasoning": step.reasoning,
1703	            "statistics": dict(stats),
1704	            "insights": list(result.insights or []),
1705	            "source_evidence_ids": list(result.source_evidence_ids or []),
1706	            "renderable": False,
1707	            "bar_reason": (
1708	                "exploratory execute_python output — BARRED from rendering; a computed number may "
1709	                "render only via the verified [#calc:] / ModelSpec lane, which re-derives it from "
1710	                "sourced evidence spans"
1711	            ),
1712	        })
1713	        pairs = ", ".join(f"{k}={v}" for k, v in stats.items())
1714	        self.workspace.disclose(
1715	            f"execute_python (turn {step.step_number}) computed {len(stats)} value(s) [{pairs}] — "
1716	            "EXPLORATORY ONLY: visible to the planner, BARRED from the report. Rendering a computed "
1717	            "number requires the verified [#calc:] lane to re-derive it from sourced evidence spans."
1718	        )

1982	    async def run(self) -> OutlineWorkspace:
1983	        ws = self.workspace
1984	        await self._run_checklist(trigger="seed")
1985	        ws.checkpoint(event="seed_checklist")
1986	
1987	        while ws.turn < self.max_turns and ws.elapsed_seconds() < self.wall_seconds:
1988	            ws.turn += 1
1989	            try:
1990	                decision = await self._decide()
1991	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a decide failure ends the loop, not the run
1992	                logger.warning("[outline_agent] decide failed at turn %d: %s", ws.turn, exc)
1993	                ws.disclose(f"decide failed at turn {ws.turn}: {exc}; stopping loop")
1994	                break
1995	
1996	            if decision.action == _FINISH_ACTION:
1997	                deficiencies = await self._run_checklist(trigger=f"finish_attempt_turn_{ws.turn}")
1998	                pending = ws.gap_ledger.pending_count
1999	                budget_remains = ws.turn < self.max_turns and ws.elapsed_seconds() < self.wall_seconds
2000	                if (deficiencies or pending) and budget_remains:

2005	                    )
2006	                    continue
2007	                ws.disclose(

2016	        # (turns/wall ran out before the agent ever picked it up) — is honestly UNFILLED, not
2017	        # silently dropped. The two reasons are disclosed distinctly (§-1.3: every gap that
2018	        # wasn't filled is disclosed, never silently absent from the coverage/limitations trail).

2031	    def _max_retries_display(self) -> int:
2032	        return self.workspace.gap_ledger._max_retries  # noqa: SLF001 — same-module access
