2035	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
2036	# Corpus-derived THEME-COVERAGE floor (RACE-FLOOR fix, 2026-07-12, this wheel).
2037	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
2038	
2039	# General web-scraping / bibliographic / UI boilerplate — NOT task-specific. Stripped so themes are
2040	# derived from real content, never from Cloudflare bot-pages, PDF stream tokens, or nav chrome.
2041	_THEME_BOILERPLATE = frozenset("""
2042	https http www com org net edu gov html htm pdf doi isbn vol pp volume issue journal abstract retrieved
2043	accessed copyright rights reserved cookie cookies privacy terms login sign researchgate gmbh amp nbsp
2044	check security detected verify verifying browser javascript enable disable pages spring aeaweb article
2045	articles figure table download springer elsevier wiley arxiv ssrn nber ray unusual moment activity
2046	continue client network cloudflare captcha robot bot connection obj endobj stream xref startxref
2047	endstream trailer font width height media proxy requests request blocked access denied wait complete
2048	required click please loading error page site website home menu search submit just need
2049	""".split())
2050	
2051	_THEME_URL_RE = None  # compiled lazily
2052	
2053	
2054	def _theme_row_text(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
2055	    """The text a THEME is derived from — the evidence row's OWN words only (title + statement +
2056	    the direct_quote with URLs / non-letters stripped). NEVER the research question / task wording,
2057	    so clustering is query-agnostic and cannot hardcode any single benchmark task's sections."""
2058	    global _THEME_URL_RE  # noqa: PLW0603
2059	    if _THEME_URL_RE is None:
2060	        import re as _re  # noqa: PLC0415
2061	        _THEME_URL_RE = _re.compile(
2062	            r"https?://\S+|www\.\S+|\S+\.(?:com|org|net|edu|gov|io|co)\b"
2063	        )
2064	    import re as _re  # noqa: PLC0415
2065	    dq = _THEME_URL_RE.sub(" ", str(row.get("direct_quote", "") or ""))
2066	    dq = _re.sub(r"[^A-Za-z ]", " ", dq)
2067	    return " ".join([
2068	        str(row.get("title", "") or ""),
2069	        str(row.get("statement", "") or ""),
2070	        dq,
2071	    ]).strip()
2072	
2073	
2074	def _titlecase_terms(terms: list[str]) -> str:
2075	    """Deterministic, human-ish section title from a cluster's top distinctive terms. Near-duplicates
2076	    (regulation/regulatory, skill/skills) are collapsed by a shared 5-char prefix so a title never
2077	    reads 'Regulation and Regulatory'; the first 2-3 distinct terms are joined readably."""
2078	    seen: list[str] = []
2079	    seen_pref: set[str] = set()
2080	    for t in terms:
2081	        t = str(t).strip()
2082	        pref = t.lower()[:5]
2083	        if t and pref not in seen_pref:
2084	            seen.append(t.capitalize())
2085	            seen_pref.add(pref)
2086	        if len(seen) >= 3:
2087	            break
2088	    if not seen:
2089	        return "Additional Corpus Theme"
2090	    if len(seen) == 1:
2091	        return seen[0]
2092	    return ", ".join(seen[:-1]) + " and " + seen[-1]
2093	
2094	
2095	def _derive_theme_coverage_sections(
2096	    evidence: list[dict[str, Any]],
2097	    seed_plans: list[Any],
2098	    *,
2099	    min_frac: float,
2100	    max_new: int,
2101	    cover_thresh: float,
2102	) -> tuple[list[Any], list[dict[str, Any]]]:
2103	    """Query-agnostic corpus theme-coverage floor.
2104	
2105	    Cluster the evidence rows by their OWN text (``_theme_row_text``) using a DETERMINISTIC pipeline
2106	    (TF-IDF + agglomerative clustering — no random init, so the same corpus always yields the same
2107	    clusters). Any cluster that holds ``>= min_frac`` of all rows yet is NOT concentrated in any
2108	    existing seed section (its members are scattered / unhomed rather than owned by one section —
2109	    ``max per-section share < cover_thresh``, and its top terms don't already appear in a seed
2110	    section's title/focus) is a corpus theme the seed outline UNDER-COVERS. For each such theme this
2111	    synthesizes ONE dedicated ``SectionPlan`` carrying the cluster's own real ev_ids, so the agentic
2112	    loop starts from a corpus-complete outline instead of being free to (non-deterministically) merge
2113	    the theme away.
2114	
2115	    Faithfulness-NEUTRAL: pure structural placement. Every ev_id on a new section already exists in
2116	    the corpus; no text is authored here; strict_verify / NLI / [#calc] / fold-in all run downstream
2117	    exactly as before. GENERAL: nothing here reads the research question or any task-specific string.
2118	
2119	    Returns ``(new_section_plans, diagnostics)``. Fail-open: any import/runtime error yields
2120	    ``([], [...])`` so the caller keeps its legacy outline untouched.
2121	    """
2122	    diag: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
2123	    rows = [
2124	        (str(r.get("evidence_id")), _theme_row_text(r))
2125	        for r in evidence
2126	        if isinstance(r, dict) and r.get("evidence_id") and _theme_row_text(r)
2127	    ]
2128	    # deterministic input order (cluster labels are order-invariant here, but stable ordering makes
2129	    # the representative-ev_id selection reproducible run-to-run).
2130	    rows.sort(key=lambda t: t[0])
2131	    n = len(rows)
2132	    if n < 40 or min_frac <= 0:
2133	        return [], [{"skipped": "too_few_rows", "n": n}]
2134	    min_size = max(2, int(round(min_frac * n)))
2135	
2136	    try:
2137	        import numpy as _np  # noqa: PLC0415
2138	        from sklearn.cluster import KMeans  # noqa: PLC0415
2139	        from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import TfidfVectorizer  # noqa: PLC0415
2140	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open, keep legacy outline
2141	        return [], [{"skipped": "sklearn_unavailable", "err": str(exc)}]
2142	
2143	    ev_ids = [e for e, _ in rows]
2144	    texts = [t for _, t in rows]
2145	    _english = TfidfVectorizer(stop_words="english").get_stop_words() or []
2146	    stop = list(set(_english) | set(_THEME_BOILERPLATE))
2147	    vec = TfidfVectorizer(
2148	        stop_words=stop, max_df=0.4, min_df=4, max_features=6000, sublinear_tf=True,
2149	        token_pattern=r"(?u)\b[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z]{2,}\b",
2150	    )
2151	    try:
2152	        X = vec.fit_transform(texts)
2153	    except ValueError as exc:  # empty vocabulary etc.
2154	        return [], [{"skipped": "empty_vocab", "err": str(exc)}]
2155	    terms = vec.get_feature_names_out()
2156	    Xd = X.toarray()
2157	    # corpus content-salience vocab: the corpus's own top document-frequency terms (query-agnostic).
2158	    # A candidate theme must be anchored in this vocabulary or it is treated as a scraping artifact
2159	    # (e.g. a Cloudflare bot-page or PDF-stream cluster) rather than a real corpus theme.
2160	    _df = (Xd > 0).sum(axis=0)
2161	    _salient_vocab = {str(terms[i]) for i in _np.argsort(_df)[::-1][:60]}
2162	    # cluster count: scale with the corpus, bounded. DETERMINISTIC (fixed random_state, fixed n_init).
2163	    k = int(min(max(len(seed_plans) * 2, 12), 24, max(2, n // 10)))
2164	    labels = KMeans(n_clusters=k, random_state=0, n_init=10).fit_predict(Xd)
2165	
2166	    # seed section footprint: ev_id -> owning section, each section's title/focus text, AND — the
2167	    # robust coverage signal — each section's OWN centroid top-terms (derived from its assigned
2168	    # evidence the SAME way the cluster terms are). Comparing cluster-terms to section-terms answers
2169	    # "is there already a section ABOUT this theme?" independent of the ev_id-count mismatch (seed
2170	    # sections hold ~20-40 rows, clusters 100-260, so a cluster-normalized ev_id overlap is always
2171	    # tiny and cannot be the primary coverage test).
2172	    _row_of = {e: i for i, e in enumerate(ev_ids)}
2173	    seed_ev_sets: list[set[str]] = []
2174	    seed_txt: list[str] = []
2175	    seed_term_sets: list[set[str]] = []
2176	    for p in seed_plans:
2177	        s_ev = {str(e) for e in (getattr(p, "ev_ids", None) or [])}
2178	        seed_ev_sets.append(s_ev)
2179	        seed_txt.append(
2180	            (str(getattr(p, "title", "")) + " " + str(getattr(p, "focus", ""))).lower()
2181	        )
2182	        s_rows = [_row_of[e] for e in s_ev if e in _row_of]
2183	        if s_rows:
2184	            s_cent = Xd[s_rows].mean(axis=0)
2185	            seed_term_sets.append({str(terms[i]) for i in _np.argsort(s_cent)[::-1][:10]
2186	                                   if s_cent[i] > 0})
2187	        else:
2188	            seed_term_sets.append(set())
2189	    seed_titles_lower = {str(getattr(p, "title", "")).strip().lower() for p in seed_plans}
2190	
2191	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import SectionPlan  # noqa: PLC0415
2192	
2193	    new_plans: list[Any] = []
2194	    # rank clusters by size desc, deterministic tie-break on label.
2195	    clusters = sorted(
2196	        ({"label": c} for c in set(labels.tolist())),
2197	        key=lambda d: d["label"],
2198	    )
2199	    sized = []
2200	    for c in clusters:
2201	        idx = [i for i, l in enumerate(labels) if l == c["label"]]
2202	        sized.append((c["label"], idx))
2203	    sized.sort(key=lambda t: (-len(t[1]), t[0]))
2204	
2205	    for label, idx in sized:
2206	        size = len(idx)
2207	        if size < min_size:
2208	            continue
2209	        member_ids = [ev_ids[i] for i in idx]
2210	        # centroid TF-IDF -> top distinctive terms (deterministic).
2211	        centroid = Xd[idx].mean(axis=0)
2212	        top_term_i = list(_np.argsort(centroid)[::-1][:8])
2213	        top_terms = [str(terms[i]) for i in top_term_i if centroid[i] > 0][:6]
2214	        # coverage: is this cluster OWNED by some existing seed section? Use TWO ev_id normalizations
2215	        # (max of cluster-share and section-share): a section OWNS the theme if it holds most of the
2216	        # cluster OR is itself mostly about the cluster.
2217	        member_set = set(member_ids)
2218	        best_share = 0.0
2219	        for s_ev in seed_ev_sets:
2220	            if not member_set or not s_ev:
2221	                continue
2222	            inter = len(member_set & s_ev)
2223	            if not inter:
2224	                continue
2225	            share = max(inter / float(size), inter / float(len(s_ev)))
2226	            if share > best_share:
2227	                best_share = share
2228	        # salience gate: a genuine corpus theme is anchored in the corpus's own top-DF vocabulary.
2229	        # A cluster whose top terms are NONE of the salient vocab is a scraping/boilerplate artifact
2230	        # (bot-page, PDF stream, nav chrome) — never promote it to a section.
2231	        salient = sum(1 for t in top_terms[:5] if t in _salient_vocab)
2232	        # PRIMARY coverage signal: does a seed section's OWN centroid vocabulary already name this
2233	        # theme? >=2 shared terms between the cluster's top-5 and some section's top-10 terms.
2234	        cl_top5 = {t for t in top_terms[:5]}
2235	        term_named = any(len(cl_top5 & sset) >= 2 for sset in seed_term_sets if sset)
2236	        # secondary: title/focus lexical overlap (>=2 of cluster top-4 terms in a section heading).
2237	        top4 = {t.lower() for t in top_terms[:4]}
2238	        lexically_named = any(
2239	            sum(1 for t in top4 if t and t in stxt) >= 2 for stxt in seed_txt
2240	        )
2241	        covered = (best_share >= cover_thresh) or term_named or lexically_named
2242	        rec = {
2243	            "size": size, "top_terms": top_terms, "salient": salient,
2244	            "term_named": term_named,
2245	            "best_section_share": round(best_share, 3),
2246	            "lexically_named": lexically_named, "covered": covered,
2247	        }
2248	        if salient < 2:
2249	            rec["skipped"] = "low_salience_artifact"
2250	            diag.append(rec)
2251	            continue
2252	        if covered or len(new_plans) >= max_new:
2253	            diag.append(rec)
2254	            continue
2255	        title = _titlecase_terms(top_terms)
2256	        if title.strip().lower() in seed_titles_lower:
2257	            rec["skipped"] = "title_collision"
2258	            diag.append(rec)
2259	            continue
2260	        # representative ev_ids: prefer rows NOT already owned by a seed section (true orphans),
2261	        # ordered by centroid-projection weight, then top up from owned members so the section is
2262	        # never undersupplied. Cap so one theme can't swallow the pool.
2263	        assigned_any = set().union(*seed_ev_sets) if seed_ev_sets else set()
2264	        proj = {ev_ids[i]: float(Xd[i][top_term_i].sum()) for i in idx}
2265	        orphans = sorted(
2266	            (m for m in member_ids if m not in assigned_any),
2267	            key=lambda m: -proj.get(m, 0.0),
2268	        )
2269	        owned = sorted(
2270	            (m for m in member_ids if m in assigned_any),
2271	            key=lambda m: -proj.get(m, 0.0),
2272	        )
2273	        chosen = (orphans + owned)[:40]
2274	        if len(chosen) < 8:
2275	            chosen = (orphans + owned)[: max(8, len(member_ids))][:40]
2276	        plan = SectionPlan(
2277	            title=title,
2278	            focus=(
2279	                f"Corpus-derived theme (auto-added by the theme-coverage floor): synthesize what "
2280	                f"the evidence says about {', '.join(top_terms[:4])}."
2281	            ),
2282	            ev_ids=list(chosen),
2283	        )
2284	        new_plans.append(plan)
2285	        rec["added_title"] = title
2286	        rec["n_ev"] = len(chosen)
2287	        diag.append(rec)
2288	
2289	    return new_plans, diag
2290

2296	async def run_outline_agent_or_legacy(
2297	    research_question: str,
2298	    evidence: list[dict[str, Any]],
2299	    gen_model: str,
2300	    outline_temperature: float,
2301	    outline_max_tokens: int,
2302	    *,
2303	    domain: str = "",
2304	    finding_clusters: Any = None,
2305	    deliverable_spec: Any = None,
2306	    scope_spec: Any = None,
2307	    same_work_groups: Any = None,
2308	    checkpoint_dir: Optional[str] = None,
2309	    # S3 FEED (Research Planning Gate ↔ outline seam). All default None => the
2310	    # gate is inert and this function is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the champion: the seed
2311	    # is built by the legacy ``_call_outline`` and no gate scope / ledger / seeded
2312	    # gaps are threaded. When a pinned artifact is supplied, ``gate_contract`` +
2313	    # ``gate_projection`` + ``gate_coverage_matrix`` FEED the loop (scope the gap
2314	    # search, pre-load coverage obligations as PENDING gaps, protect binding
2315	    # locks) — WITHOUT replacing the evidence-aware outline agent (design §6).
2316	    gate_contract: Any = None,
2317	    gate_projection: Any = None,
2318	    gate_coverage_matrix: Any = None,
2319	    seed_outline: Any = None,
2320	) -> tuple[Any, bool, int, int]:
2321	    """Seam entry point. OFF (``PG_OUTLINE_AGENT`` unset/0) => calls ``_call_outline`` exactly
2322	    as before and returns its result untouched (byte-identical legacy path — this function does
2323	    NOT even import the agent-loop machinery on the OFF path beyond this module's top-level
2324	    imports, which are cheap/side-effect-free). ON => builds the seed (via the SAME ``_call_outline``
2325	    call using the CODE model per §9.1.8 lock, unless a ``seed_outline`` is supplied), then
2326	    delegates to :func:`refine_outline_from_seed` which runs the ``OutlineAgent`` loop over the
2327	    seeded plans and returns an updated result of the SAME shape the caller already expects.
2328	
2329	    This is now a thin adapter over :func:`refine_outline_from_seed` (design §6:
2330	    ``seed = supplied_seed or legacy_call_outline(...)``). The legacy path and every
2331	    existing caller signature are preserved; the four ``gate_*`` / ``seed_outline``
2332	    kwargs are all None-default so the OFF and no-gate paths are unchanged."""
2333	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import _call_outline  # noqa: PLC0415
2334	
2335	    if not outline_agent_enabled():
2336	        return await _call_outline(
2337	            research_question, evidence, gen_model, outline_temperature, outline_max_tokens,
2338	            domain=domain, finding_clusters=finding_clusters,
2339	            deliverable_spec=deliverable_spec, scope_spec=scope_spec,
2340	            same_work_groups=same_work_groups,
2341	        )
2342	
2343	    # seed = supplied_seed or legacy_call_outline(...)  (design §6). A supplied
2344	    # ``seed_outline`` (the gate's ``OutlineSeed`` list, already translated to
2345	    # SectionPlans) skips the legacy call; otherwise the legacy adapter builds the
2346	    # seed exactly as the champion does.
2347	    if seed_outline is not None:
2348	        parse_result, retry_attempted, in_tok, out_tok = seed_outline, False, 0, 0
2349	    else:
2350	        seed_model = outliner_code_model()
2351	        parse_result, retry_attempted, in_tok, out_tok = await _call_outline(
2352	            research_question, evidence, seed_model, outline_temperature, outline_max_tokens,
2353	            domain=domain, finding_clusters=finding_clusters,
2354	            deliverable_spec=deliverable_spec, scope_spec=scope_spec,
2355	            same_work_groups=same_work_groups,
2356	        )
2357	    if not getattr(parse_result, "plans", None):
2358	        logger.info("[outline_agent] seed produced zero plans — skipping agent loop (fail-open)")
2359	        return parse_result, retry_attempted, in_tok, out_tok
2360	
2361	    return await refine_outline_from_seed(
2362	        research_question=research_question,
2363	        evidence=evidence,
2364	        seed_parse_result=parse_result,
2365	        retry_attempted=retry_attempted,
2366	        in_tok=in_tok,
2367	        out_tok=out_tok,
2368	        domain=domain,
2369	        finding_clusters=finding_clusters,
2370	        deliverable_spec=deliverable_spec,
2371	        scope_spec=scope_spec,
2372	        same_work_groups=same_work_groups,
2373	        checkpoint_dir=checkpoint_dir,
2374	        contract=gate_contract,
2375	        retrieval_projection=gate_projection,
2376	        coverage_matrix=gate_coverage_matrix,
2377	    )

2380	async def refine_outline_from_seed(
2381	    *,
2382	    research_question: str,
2383	    evidence: list[dict[str, Any]],
2384	    seed_parse_result: Any,
2385	    retry_attempted: bool = False,
2386	    in_tok: int = 0,
2387	    out_tok: int = 0,
2388	    domain: str = "",
2389	    finding_clusters: Any = None,
2390	    deliverable_spec: Any = None,
2391	    scope_spec: Any = None,
2392	    same_work_groups: Any = None,
2393	    checkpoint_dir: Optional[str] = None,
2394	    contract: Any = None,
2395	    retrieval_projection: Any = None,
2396	    coverage_matrix: Any = None,
2397	) -> tuple[Any, bool, int, int]:
2398	    """The post-retrieval evidence-aware outline REFINER (design §6 FEED seam).
2399	
2400	    Takes an already-built SEED (``seed_parse_result``, an ``OutlineParseResult``

2401	    whose ``.plans`` are ``SectionPlan``s — from the legacy ``_call_outline`` or a
2402	    supplied gate ``OutlineSeed`` list) and runs the ``OutlineAgent`` loop over it.
2403	
2404	    When a pinned gate ``contract`` / ``retrieval_projection`` / ``coverage_matrix``
2405	    is supplied it FEEDS (never replaces) the loop:
2406	
2407	      * ``retrieval_projection`` -> the gate's ``(research_frame, protocol)`` scope,
2408	        threaded into the workspace so ``_tool_search_more_evidence`` runs the gap
2409	        search IN SCOPE (THE bug fix — both designers).
2410	      * ``contract`` -> its REQUIRED coverage obligations are pre-loaded as PENDING
2411	        gaps (``coverage_gap_seeds``), so the deep-think loop CLOSES contract gaps.
2412	        Required topics are coverage obligations, NOT automatically headings.
2413	      * ``contract`` + ``coverage_matrix`` -> a term ledger (``build_term_ledger``)
2414	        so ``update_outline`` rejects dropping an explicit lock or the last owner of
2415	        a binding term.
2416	
2417	    All three default None => byte-identical to the champion (no scope, no seeded
2418	    gaps, no ledger). The gate NEVER post-filters the banked corpus here — it only
2419	    ADDS gap obligations and ROUTES the (additive) gap search."""
2420	    parse_result = seed_parse_result
2421	
2422	    ev_store = {
2423	        str(row.get("evidence_id")): row for row in evidence
2424	        if isinstance(row, dict) and row.get("evidence_id")
2425	    }
2426	    basket_menu = None
2427	    try:
2428	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.outline_digest import build_outline_digest  # noqa: PLC0415
2429	        basket_menu = build_outline_digest(
2430	            evidence, finding_clusters or [], same_work_groups=same_work_groups,
2431	            prioritize_tier1=True,
2432	        )
2433	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — basket digest is an aid, never a hard requirement
2434	        logger.warning("[outline_agent] basket digest build failed (fail-open): %s", exc)
2435	
2436	    # Snapshot the SEED plan's per-section ev_ids BEFORE the loop runs, so a caller (and the W1
2437	    # acceptance harness) can measure whether the outline actually mutated instead of trusting a
2438	    # bare final count — and so this is the ACTUAL seed the loop started from, not a second
2439	    # independent (nondeterministic) outline call.
2440	    seed_ev_by_title = {p.title: list(p.ev_ids) for p in parse_result.plans}
2441	
2442	    # Iter-2 P2-1 fix (was mis-reported as "new_evidence_count=0" every run): ``OutlineWorkspace``
2443	    # is handed ``ev_store`` BY REFERENCE (not a copy) and the loop mutates it in place via
2444	    # ``search_more_evidence``'s fold-in, so ``final_ws.ev_store is ev_store`` — the SAME dict
2445	    # object. Any comparison of ``len(ev_store)`` computed AFTER the loop against
2446	    # ``len(final_ws.ev_store)`` is comparing an object against itself and is always 0. This also
2447	    # broke the "feed newly-fetched rows back into `evidence`" loop below (the exact same
2448	    # aliasing made every row look like it was "already in ev_store", so it silently fed back
2449	    # ZERO rows — the agent-fetched corpus never reached the caller's `evidence` list at all).
2450	    # Snapshot the id SET before the loop runs; every later comparison is against this snapshot,
2451	    # never against the (now-mutated) live dict.
2452	    ev_ids_before = set(ev_store.keys())
2453	    ev_store_size_before = len(ev_ids_before)
2454	
2455	    # Iter-2 fix: thread the deliverable's required-structure lock (if any) through to the
2456	    # workspace so `_tool_update_outline` (via `apply_revision_ops(required_titles=...)`) and the
2457	    # auto-assign fallback both respect it — an exact-N-in-order required structure must never
2458	    # silently grow a 5th section behind the caller's back. Mirrors `_call_outline`'s own
2459	    # `_spec_read(deliverable_spec, "required_sections", [])` read (dict OR object shape).
2460	    _required_titles = [
2461	        str(t).strip()
2462	        for t in (
2463	            deliverable_spec.get("required_sections", [])
2464	            if isinstance(deliverable_spec, dict)
2465	            else getattr(deliverable_spec, "required_sections", [])
2466	        ) or []
2467	        if str(t).strip()
2468	    ]
2469	    # THEME-COVERAGE floor (RACE-FLOOR fix, 2026-07-12): BEFORE the agent loop can non-deterministically
2470	    # merge corpus themes away, cluster the ev_store rows query-agnostically and ADD a dedicated seed
2471	    # section for any large (``>=PG_OUTLINE_THEME_FLOOR_MIN_FRAC`` of rows) corpus theme the seed
2472	    # under-covers. This raises the seed's OWN theme decomposition to the corpus's, then the section
2473	    # floor below pins that richer count so the loop cannot collapse back under it. Gate DEFAULT-OFF
2474	    # (A/B): ``PG_OUTLINE_THEME_FLOOR=1`` arms it. Skipped under a required-structure lock (the caller
2475	    # owns the structure). Faithfulness-NEUTRAL (only real ev_ids placed; no authored text).
2476	    if _env_flag("PG_OUTLINE_THEME_FLOOR", default_on=False) and not _required_titles:
2477	        try:
2478	            _theme_new_plans, _theme_diag = _derive_theme_coverage_sections(
2479	                evidence, parse_result.plans,
2480	                min_frac=_env_float("PG_OUTLINE_THEME_FLOOR_MIN_FRAC", 0.05),
2481	                max_new=_env_int("PG_OUTLINE_THEME_FLOOR_MAX_NEW", 3),
2482	                cover_thresh=_env_float("PG_OUTLINE_THEME_FLOOR_COVER", 0.5),
2483	            )
2484	        except Exception as _exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open, keep legacy outline
2485	            _theme_new_plans, _theme_diag = [], [{"error": str(_exc)}]
2486	            logger.warning("[outline_agent] theme-coverage floor failed (fail-open): %s", _exc)
2487	        logger.info("[outline_agent] theme-coverage floor diag: %s", _theme_diag)
2488	        if _theme_new_plans:
2489	            parse_result.plans = list(parse_result.plans) + list(_theme_new_plans)
2490	            logger.info(
2491	                "[outline_agent] theme-coverage floor ADDED %d dedicated section(s) for uncovered "
2492	                "corpus themes: %s", len(_theme_new_plans),
2493	                [getattr(p, "title", "") for p in _theme_new_plans],
2494	            )
2495	
2496	    # RACE-FLOOR fix: the corpus-derived thematic-coverage floor = the SEED outline's own section
2497	    # count. The seed is built by ``_call_outline`` from the evidence/baskets (query-agnostic), so
2498	    # its section count is the corpus's own theme decomposition — NOT a hardcoded task-72 structure.
2499	    # The loop may enrich (split/add/retitle/reassign/search) but must not NET-collapse below this
2500	    # count via ``merge``. Gate default-ON; ``PG_OUTLINE_SECTION_FLOOR=0`` restores the legacy
2501	    # (no-floor) behavior. A required-structure lock already pins the count, so it takes precedence.
2502	    _section_floor = 0
2503	    if _env_flag("PG_OUTLINE_SECTION_FLOOR", default_on=True) and not _required_titles:
2504	        _section_floor = len(parse_result.plans)
2505	    workspace = OutlineWorkspace(
2506	        research_question=research_question,
2507	        ev_store=ev_store,
2508	        outline_draft=list(parse_result.plans),
2509	        basket_menu=basket_menu,
2510	        checkpoint_dir=checkpoint_dir,
2511	        total_input_tokens=in_tok,
2512	        total_output_tokens=out_tok,
2513	        required_titles=_required_titles,
2514	        min_sections=_section_floor,
2515	    )
2516	    if _section_floor:
2517	        logger.info(
2518	            "[outline_agent] thematic-coverage floor armed: seed=%d sections; the loop may not "
2519	            "net-collapse below this via merge (RACE-FLOOR fix)", _section_floor,
2520	        )
2521	
2522	    # ── S3 FEED: thread the gate contract/projection/coverage-matrix into the
2523	    # workspace (design §6). Every branch is guarded on the gate object being
2524	    # supplied, so a no-gate call (contract=projection=matrix=None — every
2525	    # champion caller) leaves the workspace fields at their inert defaults and is
2526	    # BYTE-IDENTICAL. Fail-open: any FEED error is disclosed and skipped; the loop
2527	    # still runs on the seed.
2528	    if contract is not None or retrieval_projection is not None:
2529	        try:
2530	            from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_gate_feed import (  # noqa: PLC0415
2531	                build_term_ledger,
2532	                coverage_gap_seeds,
2533	                gate_scope_for_gap_search,
2534	            )
2535	            # (1) THE BUG FIX: the gate's retrieval scope for the gap search.
2536	            if retrieval_projection is not None:
2537	                frame, protocol = gate_scope_for_gap_search(
2538	                    retrieval_projection, base_question=research_question,
2539	                )
2540	                workspace.gate_research_frame = frame
2541	                workspace.gate_gap_protocol = protocol
2542	                if frame is not None or protocol is not None:
2543	                    logger.info(
2544	                        "[outline_agent] S3 FEED: gap search SCOPED to gate frame "
2545	                        "(evidence_needs=%s) — no longer unscoped",
2546	                        list(getattr(frame, "evidence_needs", []) or []),
2547	                    )
2548	            # (2) contract hash (provenance) + (3) term ledger (lock protection).
2549	            if contract is not None:
2550	                workspace.contract_hash = str(
2551	                    getattr(contract, "contract_sha256", "")
2552	                    or (
2553	                        contract.get("contract_sha256", "")
2554	                        if isinstance(contract, dict) else ""
2555	                    )
2556	                )
2557	                workspace.term_ledger = build_term_ledger(
2558	                    contract, coverage_matrix=coverage_matrix,
2559	                )
2560	                # (4) coverage obligations -> PENDING gaps (NOT headings).
2561	                seeds = coverage_gap_seeds(contract)
2562	                for s in seeds:
2563	                    workspace.gap_ledger.add(
2564	                        section=s.get("section") or "(unassigned)",
2565	                        aspect=s.get("aspect", ""),
2566	                        needed_kind="coverage",
2567	                        source="gate_contract",
2568	                    )
2569	                if seeds:
2570	                    workspace.disclose(
2571	                        f"S3 FEED: pre-loaded {len(seeds)} required-coverage "
2572	                        "obligation(s) as PENDING gaps (coverage obligations, NOT "
2573	                        "headings)"
2574	                    )
2575	                _lt = workspace.term_ledger.get("locked_titles") or []
2576	                _bo = workspace.term_ledger.get("binding_term_owners") or {}
2577	                if _lt or _bo:
2578	                    logger.info(
2579	                        "[outline_agent] S3 FEED: term ledger armed — %d locked "
2580	                        "title(s), %d binding-term owner set(s)", len(_lt), len(_bo),
2581	                    )
2582	        except Exception as _feed_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — FEED is additive, never fatal
2583	            logger.warning(
2584	                "[outline_agent] S3 FEED skipped (fail-open): %s", _feed_exc,
2585	            )
2586	
2587	    agent = OutlineAgent(workspace, domain=domain or None)
2588	    # W2 P0 fix (Fable-authoritative, 2026-07-11): this call used to be BARE. On the dense
2589	    # full-corpus real run a single glm-5.2 ``ReasoningFirstTruncationError`` (reasoning
2590	    # prelude alone exceeding the completion budget) — or ANY other exception, or the loop
2591	    # simply running past its own wall-clock without ``_decide``/``_run_checklist`` ever
2592	    # returning control — propagated straight out of ``agent.run()``, out of THIS function,
2593	    # and aborted the entire outline stage; the caller (``multi_section_generator``) then fell
2594	    # back to the PLAIN non-agentic outliner. That is a silent capability downgrade the operator
2595	    # never asked for (LAW II). The fix DEGRADES TO SEED instead: ``workspace`` is the exact
2596	    # same object ``agent`` was constructed with, and every mutation the loop makes (fold-ins,
2597	    # revisions, ledger updates) is applied to it IN PLACE and checkpointed after every turn —
2598	    # so on any failure ``workspace`` already holds the SEED outline merged with whatever
2599	    # agentic turns completed before the failure, never a blank/plain fallback. The outer
2600	    # ``asyncio.wait_for`` is belt-and-suspenders on top of the loop's own internal
2601	    # turns/wall-clock check (a single hung LLM call inside one turn must not be able to wedge
2602	    # the whole stage past the caller's own upstream timeout).
2603	    degraded_to_seed = False
2604	    degrade_reason = ""
2605	    try:
2606	        final_ws = await asyncio.wait_for(
2607	            agent.run(),
2608	            timeout=agent.wall_seconds + _env_int(
2609	                "PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_RUN_TIMEOUT_GRACE_SECONDS",
2610	                PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_RUN_TIMEOUT_GRACE_SECONDS_DEFAULT,
2611	            ),
2612	        )
2613	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — degrade to SEED, never abort the outline stage
2614	        degraded_to_seed = True
2615	        degrade_reason = f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
2616	        workspace.disclose(
2617	            f"agentic outline loop ABORTED after {workspace.turn} completed turn(s) "
2618	            f"({degrade_reason}) — DEGRADING TO SEED outline merged with whatever agentic "
2619	            "turns completed before the failure (never falls back to plain non-agentic "
2620	            "outline)"
2621	        )
2622	        workspace.checkpoint(event="agent_run_aborted_degrade_to_seed")
2623	        logger.warning(
2624	            "[outline_agent] agent.run() aborted, degrading to seed+partial-turns state: %s",
2625	            degrade_reason,
2626	        )
2627	        final_ws = workspace
2628	
2629	    parse_result.plans = list(final_ws.outline_draft)  # type: ignore[assignment]
2630	    parse_result.digest_stats = dict(parse_result.digest_stats or {})
2631	    parse_result.digest_stats["outline_agent"] = {
2632	        "cp4_used": "agentic-degraded-seed" if degraded_to_seed else "agentic",
2633	        "degraded_to_seed": degraded_to_seed,
2634	        "degrade_reason": degrade_reason,
2635	        "turns": final_ws.turn,
2636	        "elapsed_seconds": round(final_ws.elapsed_seconds(), 1),
2637	        "ev_store_size": len(final_ws.ev_store),
2638	        "ev_store_size_at_seed": ev_store_size_before,
2639	        "new_evidence_count": len(final_ws.ev_store) - ev_store_size_before,
2640	        "gap_ledger": final_ws.gap_ledger.as_list(),
2641	        "unfilled_gaps": [dataclasses.asdict(t) for t in final_ws.gap_ledger.unfilled],
2642	        "disclosures": list(final_ws.disclosures),
2643	        # W4: the computed values a successful execute_python produced. Before this the
2644	        # entry-point return carried NO notebook payload at all, so the value died here.
2645	        # EXPLORATORY: every row is renderable=False; the report may render a computed
2646	        # number ONLY through the verified [#calc:] lane. Manifest/telemetry only — grep
2647	        # confirms digest_stats never reaches a writer prompt.
2648	        "notebook_compute": list(final_ws.compute_results),
2649	        "seed_ev_by_title": seed_ev_by_title,
2650	        "final_ev_by_title": {
2651	            _plan_field(p, "title", ""): list(_plan_field(p, "ev_ids", []) or [])
2652	            for p in final_ws.outline_draft
2653	        },
2654	    }
2655	    # Feed the enlarged evidence pool back so downstream fold-in (evidence_for_gen etc.) sees
2656	    # the new rows too — the caller reads `evidence` by reference in most call sites, but to
2657	    # stay honest under any calling convention this is ALSO returned via digest_stats above.
2658	    # (iter-2 fix: compare against the PRE-loop id snapshot, not the aliased live dict — see note
2659	    # above; this is the actual corpus feed-back path and was silently a no-op before this fix.)
2660	    for eid, row in final_ws.ev_store.items():
2661	        if eid and eid not in ev_ids_before:
2662	            evidence.append(row)
2663	
2664	    # MOAT LIVE-SEAM: export the run-scoped verified-compute registry so the FULL-CORPUS
2665	    # ``generate_multi_section_report`` composer can gate a ``[#calc:]`` body sentence against the
2666	    # models the agentic loop actually computed. EMPTY ({}) => the consumer threads None =>
2667	    # byte-identical legacy verify (derived numbers still cannot reach the [#ev:] render path; the
2668	    # calc lane is the ONLY route and it is fail-closed on an empty/absent registry).
2669	    parse_result.quantified_models = dict(final_ws.quantified_models)
2670	
2671	    # MOAT DETERMINISTIC EMISSION: export the render-ready [#calc:] claim sentences keyed by their
2672	    # target section title, so the FULL-CORPUS composer can APPEND them into the matching section
2673	    # body deterministically (immediately before strict_verify), rather than trusting the LLM
2674	    # writer to copy an unguessable spec_hash verbatim. Records with no explicit section are
2675	    # auto-homed by matching their input ev_ids against the final outline's per-section ev_ids.
2676	    # EMPTY ({}) => the consumer threads None => byte-identical legacy (no deterministic append).
2677	    parse_result.calc_claims = _build_calc_claims_map(final_ws)
2678	
2679	    return parse_result, retry_attempted, final_ws.total_input_tokens, final_ws.total_output_tokens
2680	
2681	
2682	def _build_calc_claims_map(final_ws: "OutlineWorkspace") -> dict[str, list[str]]:
2683	    """Group the agent's verified-compute claim sentences by their TARGET section title.
2684	
2685	    A record with an explicit ``section`` is homed there verbatim. A record with an empty
2686	    ``section`` is AUTO-HOMED to the final-outline section whose ``ev_ids`` overlap most with the
2687	    claim's ``input_ev_ids`` (the sourced datapoints) — so a number derived from a section's own
2688	    evidence lands in that section even when the agent did not name it. A record that homes to no
2689	    section (no title, no ev_id overlap) is DROPPED from the emission map (it stays available to
2690	    the writer prose only); it can never render a phantom section.
2691	    Dedup: identical (section, calc_token) records collapse so a claim is appended at most once.
2692	    """
2693	    # section title -> UNION of ev_ids (from the FINAL outline draft the agent produced).
2694	    # Duplicate-section-title uniqueness guard: two draft plans may carry the SAME title. A plain
2695	    # list of (title, ev_ids) pairs would then split that title's ev_ids across two entries, so an
2696	    # auto-home overlap would be measured against only ONE half (under-counting, possible mis-home)
2697	    # and the emission key would be ambiguous. Fold duplicate titles into ONE entry with the UNION
2698	    # of their ev_ids (dict preserves first-seen order => deterministic) so each distinct title
2699	    # homes exactly once against its full evidence set.
2700	    sec_ev_map: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
2701	    for p in final_ws.outline_draft:
2702	        title = _plan_field(p, "title", "") or ""
2703	        if not title:
2704	            continue
2705	        sec_ev_map.setdefault(title, set()).update(_plan_field(p, "ev_ids", []) or [])
2706	    sec_ev: list[tuple[str, set[str]]] = list(sec_ev_map.items())
2707	    valid_titles = set(sec_ev_map)
2708	
2709	    out: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
2710	    seen: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()
2711	    for rec in (final_ws.computed_claims or []):
2712	        if not isinstance(rec, dict):
2713	            continue
2714	        sentence = str(rec.get("sentence") or "").strip()
2715	        token = str(rec.get("calc_token") or "").strip()
2716	        if not sentence or not token:
2717	            continue
2718	        target = str(rec.get("section") or "").strip()
2719	        if target not in valid_titles:
2720	            # Auto-home by ev_id overlap with the sourced datapoints.
2721	            claim_evs = set(rec.get("input_ev_ids") or [])
2722	            best_title, best_overlap = "", 0
2723	            for title, ev_ids in sec_ev:
2724	                overlap = len(claim_evs & ev_ids)
2725	                if overlap > best_overlap:
2726	                    best_title, best_overlap = title, overlap
2727	            target = best_title if best_overlap > 0 else ""
2728	        if not target:
2729	            continue  # unhomeable — never invent a section for it
2730	        dedup_key = (target, token)
2731	        if dedup_key in seen:
2732	            continue
2733	        seen.add(dedup_key)
2734	        out.setdefault(target, []).append(sentence)
2735	    return out

content.text: outline_agent.py finished, clean (no unprofessional names, though "MOAT" appears as a comment heading — not a name). Now outline_gate_feed.py.

content.content:
1	"""Outline FEED — the gate ↔ outline-agent seam (S3), additive + default-OFF.
2	
3	The Research Planning Gate produces ONE pinned :class:`PlanningGateArtifact`
4	(contract + plan). At the *post-retrieval* outline stage the gate does not
5	replace the evidence-aware outline agent; it **FEEDS** it (consolidated design
6	§6, Sol §5): it pre-loads the contract's coverage obligations as PENDING gaps so
7	the deep-think loop CLOSES contract gaps, and it threads the retrieval *scope*
8	(the same ``research_frame`` / ``protocol`` used by initial retrieval) into the
9	outline-stage gap search so a correctly scoped first pass is not diluted by an
10	unconstrained follow-up query.
11	
12	This module is PURE + deterministic (no network, no LLM, no I/O). It only reads
13	an already-pinned contract/projection and returns plain data:
14	
15	  * :func:`coverage_gap_seeds` — the contract's REQUIRED coverage obligations
16	    (``required_coverage`` / ``must_address``) as ``(section, aspect)`` gap
17	    seeds. **Required topics are coverage OBLIGATIONS, not automatically
18	    headings** — they are pre-loaded PENDING in the gap ledger, never mapped 1:1
19	    onto section titles (the round-1 coverage-to-heading bug the design forbids).
20	  * :func:`build_term_ledger` — the binding-term → owning-section map + the set
21	    of exact-title / order locks, so ``update_outline`` can reject a revision
22	    that drops an explicit lock or the last owner of a binding term.
23	  * :func:`gate_scope_for_gap_search` — the ``(research_frame, protocol)`` pair a
24	    :class:`RetrievalProjection` compiles, threaded into the outline-stage gap
25	    search (:func:`outline_agent._tool_search_more_evidence`).
26	
27	Guardrail posture
28	-----------------
29	* **ADD / ROUTE, never filter.** The FEED only ADDS PENDING gap todos (search
30	  MORE) and ROUTES the gap search to the gate's scope. It never drops a source,
31	  never filters the banked corpus, never removes a section that owns a binding
32	  term. There is no drop path here.
33	* **Coverage != heading.** Required coverage becomes a gap OBLIGATION with an
34	  ``(section="", aspect=<topic>)`` unassigned home, so the loop searches for it
35	  and the outliner homes it under whatever section fits — the topic is never
36	  forced to become its own heading.
37	* **Fail-open.** Every helper tolerates a degraded/empty/``None`` contract or
38	  projection: it returns empty data, so the caller reads the champion default
39	  (no seeded gaps, no ledger locks, no scoped gap search) and the run is
40	  byte-identical to today's champion.
41	* **Never invent a constraint.** Only ``force == hard`` terms (which the schema
42	  guarantees are ``origin == explicit``/user-backed) become binding-term ledger
43	  owners or locks. Inferred / preference terms contribute optional gap seeds
44	  only — never a lock, never a hard veto on a revision.
45	"""
46	
47	from __future__ import annotations
48	
49	from typing import Any, Optional
50	
51	# Keep this module import-light: the schema types are only used for isinstance /
52	# attribute reads, all guarded, so a caller may pass duck-typed objects too.
53	
54	# Coverage-requirement dimensions/keys that name a REQUIRED obligation. A gate
55	# contract carries these as ``CoverageRequirement`` objects; a looser upstream
56	# may hand a plain dict with ``required_coverage`` / ``must_address`` lists.
57	_REQUIRED_COVERAGE_KEYS: tuple[str, ...] = ("required_coverage", "must_address")
58	
59	
60	def _norm(s: Any) -> str:
61	    return str(s or "").strip()
62	
63	
64	def _term_value_text(term: Any) -> str:
65	    """The human-readable text of a ContractTerm-ish object (or plain value)."""
66	    if term is None:
67	        return ""
68	    val = getattr(term, "value", term)
69	    if isinstance(val, (list, tuple)):
70	        return " ".join(_norm(v) for v in val if _norm(v)).strip()
71	    return _norm(val)
72	
73	
74	def _is_hard(term: Any) -> bool:
75	    """True iff the term is a HARD force term (schema guarantees explicit/user)."""
76	    force = getattr(term, "force", None)
77	    if force is not None:
78	        return str(force).strip().lower() == "hard"
79	    # duck-typed dict shape
80	    if isinstance(term, dict):
81	        return str(term.get("force", "")).strip().lower() == "hard"
82	    return False
83	
84	
85	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
86	# FEED 1 — coverage obligations -> PENDING gap seeds (NOT headings)
87	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
88	
89	
90	def coverage_gap_seeds(contract: Any) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
91	    """The contract's REQUIRED coverage obligations as gap seeds.
92	
93	    Returns a list of ``{"section": ..., "aspect": ..., "term_id": ...}``. Each
94	    is a coverage OBLIGATION the deep-think loop should CLOSE — pre-loaded PENDING
95	    in the gap ledger by the caller, so ``next_pending()`` routes a scoped gap
96	    search for it. **The ``section`` is deliberately left EMPTY ("(unassigned)")**
97	    unless the coverage requirement is bound to an EXPLICIT section: a required
98	    topic is a coverage obligation, not automatically a heading (the round-1 bug).
99	
100	    Fail-open: a ``None`` / empty / shapeless contract yields ``[]`` (no seeds),
101	    so the caller keeps the champion behavior (no pre-loaded gaps).
102	    """
103	    if contract is None:
104	        return []
105	    out: list[dict[str, str]] = []
106	    seen: set[str] = set()
107	
108	    def _add(aspect: str, term_id: str = "", section: str = "") -> None:
109	        aspect = _norm(aspect)
110	        if not aspect:
111	            return
112	        key = f"{_norm(section).lower()}||{aspect.lower()}"
113	        if key in seen:
114	            return
115	        seen.add(key)
116	        out.append({
117	            "section": _norm(section),
118	            "aspect": aspect,
119	            "term_id": _norm(term_id),
120	        })
121	
122	    # 1) Structured contract: CoverageRequirement objects marked required.
123	    coverage = getattr(contract, "coverage", None)
124	    if isinstance(coverage, (list, tuple)):
125	        # map requirement_id -> the explicit section that owns it (if any), so a
126	        # coverage req the deliverable EXPLICITLY bound to a section keeps that
127	        # home; every other required topic stays unassigned (never a new heading).
128	        req_to_section = _explicit_req_sections(contract)
129	        for cr in coverage:
130	            required = getattr(cr, "required", True)
131	            if not required:
132	                continue
133	            aspect = _term_value_text(getattr(cr, "statement", None))
134	            if not aspect:
135	                # fall back to the requirement's own kind/id so it is still tracked
136	                aspect = _norm(getattr(cr, "requirement_id", ""))
137	            req_id = _norm(getattr(cr, "requirement_id", ""))
138	            term_id = _norm(getattr(getattr(cr, "statement", None), "term_id", "")) or req_id
139	            _add(aspect, term_id=term_id, section=req_to_section.get(req_id, ""))
140	
141	    # 2) Duck-typed dict shape (a looser upstream): required_coverage/must_address.
142	    if isinstance(contract, dict):
143	        for key in _REQUIRED_COVERAGE_KEYS:
144	            vals = contract.get(key)
145	            if isinstance(vals, (list, tuple)):
146	                for v in vals:
147	                    _add(_term_value_text(v) or _norm(v))
148	
149	    return out
150	
151	
152	def _explicit_req_sections(contract: Any) -> dict[str, str]:
153	    """Map ``requirement_id -> explicit section title`` for coverage reqs the
154	    deliverable EXPLICITLY placed under an exact-title-locked section. Only an
155	    explicit / user section counts (never an inferred/gate-proposed heading), so
156	    a required topic never silently becomes a heading."""
157	    out: dict[str, str] = {}
158	    sections = getattr(contract, "sections", None)
159	    if not isinstance(sections, (list, tuple)):
160	        return out
161	    for sec in sections:
162	        if not getattr(sec, "exact_title_lock", False):
163	            continue
164	        title = _term_value_text(getattr(sec, "title", None))
165	        if not title:
166	            continue
167	        for rid in (getattr(sec, "coverage_requirement_ids", None) or []):
168	            rid = _norm(rid)
169	            if rid:
170	                out[rid] = title
171	    return out
172	
173	
174	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
175	# FEED 2 — the term ledger (binding term -> owning sections + locks)
176	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
177	
178	
179	def build_term_ledger(
180	    contract: Any, coverage_matrix: Any = None,
181	) -> dict[str, Any]:
182	    """Build the immutable term ledger the outline workspace validates against.
183	
184	    Returns a plain dict::
185	
186	        {
187	          "binding_term_owners": {term_id: [section_title, ...]},  # last-owner guard
188	          "locked_titles": [title, ...],       # exact-title locks (explicit/user)
189	          "ordered_titles": [title, ...],      # order-locked titles (in order)
190	          "binding_term_ids": [term_id, ...],  # every hard/binding term id
191	        }
192	
193	    Only HARD (``force == hard``, schema-guaranteed explicit/user) terms are
194	    binding. Section ownership is read from the plan's ``coverage_matrix`` when
195	    supplied (a binding term -> its ``section_ids``); a section that owns a
196	    binding term is protected from being dropped as the LAST owner.
197	
198	    Fail-open: ``None`` / empty contract -> an empty ledger (no locks, no binding
199	    owners), so ``update_outline`` validation is a no-op and the run is
200	    byte-identical.
201	    """
202	    ledger: dict[str, Any] = {
203	        "binding_term_owners": {},
204	        "locked_titles": [],
205	        "ordered_titles": [],
206	        "binding_term_ids": [],
207	    }
208	    if contract is None:
209	        return ledger
210	
211	    # binding term ids: every HARD term across the contract.
212	    binding_ids: list[str] = []
213	    all_terms = getattr(contract, "all_terms", None)
214	    if callable(all_terms):
215	        try:
216	            for t in all_terms():
217	                if _is_hard(t):
218	                    tid = _norm(getattr(t, "term_id", ""))
219	                    if tid:
220	                        binding_ids.append(tid)
221	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open
222	            binding_ids = []
223	    ledger["binding_term_ids"] = _dedup(binding_ids)
224	
225	    # exact-title / order locks: explicit section requirements.
226	    locked: list[str] = []
227	    ordered: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
228	    sections = getattr(contract, "sections", None)
229	    if isinstance(sections, (list, tuple)):
230	        for sec in sections:
231	            title = _term_value_text(getattr(sec, "title", None))
232	            if not title:
233	                continue
234	            if getattr(sec, "exact_title_lock", False):
235	                locked.append(title)
236	            order = getattr(sec, "order", None)
237	            if isinstance(order, int) and getattr(sec, "exact_title_lock", False):
238	                ordered.append((order, title))
239	    ledger["locked_titles"] = _dedup(locked)
240	    ledger["ordered_titles"] = [t for _, t in sorted(ordered, key=lambda x: x[0])]
241	
242	    # binding-term -> owning sections (from the coverage matrix). Only bindings
243	    # whose term id is in binding_ids get last-owner protection.
244	    owners: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
245	    matrix = coverage_matrix
246	    if matrix is None:
247	        matrix = getattr(getattr(contract, "plan", None), "coverage_matrix", None)
248	    if isinstance(matrix, (list, tuple)):
249	        binding_set = set(ledger["binding_term_ids"])
250	        # map requirement/section ids to section TITLES via the contract sections.
251	        sec_id_to_title = _section_id_to_title(contract)
252	        for binding in matrix:
253	            tid = _norm(getattr(binding, "contract_term_id", ""))
254	            if tid and tid not in binding_set:
255	                # the coverage-matrix may bind a coverage requirement whose
256	                # statement term is hard; keep only genuinely-binding ids.
257	                continue
258	            sec_ids = getattr(binding, "section_ids", None) or []
259	            titles = [
260	                sec_id_to_title.get(_norm(sid), _norm(sid))
261	                for sid in sec_ids if _norm(sid)
262	            ]
263	            titles = [t for t in titles if t]
264	            if tid and titles:
265	                owners.setdefault(tid, [])
266	                for t in titles:
267	                    if t not in owners[tid]:
268	                        owners[tid].append(t)
269	    ledger["binding_term_owners"] = owners
270	    return ledger
271	
272	
273	def _section_id_to_title(contract: Any) -> dict[str, str]:
274	    out: dict[str, str] = {}
275	    sections = getattr(contract, "sections", None)
276	    if isinstance(sections, (list, tuple)):
277	        for sec in sections:
278	            sid = _norm(getattr(sec, "section_id", ""))
279	            title = _term_value_text(getattr(sec, "title", None))
280	            if sid and title:
281	                out[sid] = title
282	    return out
283	
284	
285	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
286	# FEED 3 — the gate's retrieval scope for the outline-stage gap search
287	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
288	
289	
290	def gate_scope_for_gap_search(
291	    projection: Any, *, base_question: str = "",
292	) -> tuple[Any, Optional[dict[str, Any]]]:
293	    """The ``(research_frame, protocol)`` a :class:`RetrievalProjection` compiles.
294	
295	    THE BUG FIX seam (both designers): ``outline_agent._tool_search_more_evidence``
296	    calls ``run_live_retrieval`` WITHOUT ``research_frame`` / ``protocol`` today, so
297	    the outline-stage gap search runs UNSCOPED even under a hard journal / language
298	    / jurisdiction contract. This returns the SAME scope levers the initial
299	    retrieval used, so the gap query keeps that scope.
300	
301	    Fail-open: a ``None`` projection (or one that routes nothing) yields
302	    ``(None, None)`` — exactly the champion ``run_live_retrieval`` defaults, so the
303	    gap search is byte-identical to today.
304	    """
305	    if projection is None:
306	        return (None, None)
307	    frame = None
308	    protocol = None
309	    to_frame = getattr(projection, "to_research_frame", None)
310	    if callable(to_frame):
311	        try:
312	            frame = to_frame()
313	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open
314	            frame = None
315	    to_proto = getattr(projection, "to_protocol", None)
316	    if callable(to_proto):
317	        try:
318	            protocol = to_proto(base_question=base_question)
319	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open
320	            protocol = None
321	    return (frame, protocol)
322	
323	
324	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
325	# update_outline validation — reject dropping a lock / last binding owner
326	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
327	
328	
329	def validate_revision_against_ledger(
330	    ledger: Optional[dict[str, Any]],
331	    *,
332	    titles_before: list[str],
333	    titles_after: list[str],
334	) -> list[str]:
335	    """Return a list of VIOLATION reasons if the revision drops an explicit lock or
336	    the last owner of a binding term. Empty list => the revision is allowed.
337	
338	    ``titles_before`` / ``titles_after`` are the section title lists before and
339	    after the proposed op set. A violation is:
340	
341	      * a ``locked_title`` present before but absent after (explicit title lock
342	        dropped), OR
343	      * a binding term whose EVERY owning section is absent after (the last owner
344	        of a binding requirement dropped).
345	
346	    Fail-open: a ``None`` / empty ledger => ``[]`` (no constraint), byte-identical.
347	    A retitle that keeps the same count but renames a locked title IS a violation
348	    (the locked title disappeared) — the caller may then defer the op.
349	    """
350	    if not ledger:
351	        return []
352	    before = {_norm(t) for t in (titles_before or []) if _norm(t)}
353	    after = {_norm(t) for t in (titles_after or []) if _norm(t)}
354	    violations: list[str] = []
355	
356	    for locked in (ledger.get("locked_titles") or []):
357	        locked_n = _norm(locked)
358	        if locked_n in before and locked_n not in after:
359	            violations.append(f"explicit_title_lock_dropped:{locked_n!r}")
360	
361	    owners: dict[str, list[str]] = ledger.get("binding_term_owners") or {}
362	    for tid, sec_titles in owners.items():
363	        present_before = [t for t in sec_titles if _norm(t) in before]
364	        if not present_before:
365	            # the binding term had no owner present before — nothing to protect.
366	            continue
367	        present_after = [t for t in sec_titles if _norm(t) in after]
368	        if not present_after:
369	            violations.append(f"last_owner_of_binding_term_dropped:{tid!r}")
370	
371	    return violations
372	
373	
374	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
375	# small utils
376	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
377	
378	
379	def _dedup(items: list[str]) -> list[str]:
380	    out: list[str] = []
381	    seen: set[str] = set()
382	    for it in items:
383	        it = _norm(it)
384	        if it and it.lower() not in seen:
385	            seen.add(it.lower())
386	            out.append(it)
387	    return out
388

1	"""T1 rich toolkit for the agentic outliner (docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md PART 4, step 1+5).
2	
3	``register_outline_toolkit(registry, workspace, agent_model, deadline)`` wires the read-only /
4	deterministic primitives + the moat ``verified_compute`` onto the driver's existing
5	``ToolRegistry``. The driver (``OutlineAgent``) does not change shape when tools are added — every
6	tool speaks the standard ``execute(evidence_store, data_points, client, **kw) -> ToolResult``
7	contract.
8	
9	Faithfulness posture of this toolkit:
10	  * Every tool here is READ-ONLY over the workspace (search_corpus, get_evidence, list_baskets,
11	    coverage_audit, preview_section_evidence) or DETERMINISTIC-COMPUTE (calculator,
12	    verified_compute). None fetches network content — so none re-enters evidence and none can
13	    widen the render surface.
14	  * ``calculator`` is EXPLORATORY: a bare arithmetic result carries no evidence span, so it is
15	    planner-facing only (``renderable=False``). To RENDER a derived number the agent must use
16	    ``verified_compute``, which re-derives it through the verified ``[#calc:]`` lane
17	    (outline/verified_compute.py). This is the same two-lane discipline as execute_python.
18	"""
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import asyncio
22	import re
23	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
24	
25	from src.polaris_graph.tools.tool_registry import ToolDefinition, ToolRegistry, ToolResult
26	
27	_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z0-9]+")
28	
29	# The evidence text fields, richest first (mirrors _fold_in / tradeoff_modeler ordering).
30	_TEXT_FIELDS = (
31	    "direct_quote", "statement", "full_text", "content", "extracted_text",
32	    "fetched_body", "raw_content", "raw_text", "page_text", "source_text", "body", "text",
33	)
34	
35	
36	def _row_text(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
37	    return max(
38	        (str(row.get(k) or "") for k in _TEXT_FIELDS),
39	        key=len, default="",
40	    )
41	
42	
43	def _tokens(text: str) -> list[str]:
44	    return _WORD_RE.findall(text.lower())
45	
46	
47	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
48	# calculator — single deterministic arithmetic expression (EXPLORATORY lane)
49	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
50	async def _tool_calculator(
51	    workspace: Any, *, expression: str = "", **_ignored: Any,
52	) -> ToolResult:
53	    """Evaluate ONE whitelisted-AST arithmetic expression (reuses tradeoff_modeler's validator +
54	    interpreter). Deterministic, no LLM, no sandbox. EXPLORATORY: the result carries no evidence
55	    span, so it is planner-facing (``renderable=False``) — to RENDER a derived number use
56	    verified_compute."""
57	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.tradeoff_modeler import (  # noqa: PLC0415
58	        _eval_formula, _formula_names,
59	    )
60	
61	    expr = str(expression or "").strip()
62	    if not expr:
63	        return ToolResult(
64	            success=False, tool_name="calculator",
65	            markdown="calculator requires an `expression`.", error="missing_expression",
66	        )
67	    ok, reason, refs = _formula_names(expr, set())
68	    if not ok:
69	        return ToolResult(
70	            success=False, tool_name="calculator",
71	            markdown=f"calculator rejected {expr!r}: {reason}", error=f"formula_invalid:{reason}",
72	        )
73	    if refs:
74	        return ToolResult(
75	            success=False, tool_name="calculator",
76	            markdown=f"calculator is constant-only; unknown names {sorted(refs)}",
77	            error="unbound_names",
78	        )
79	    try:
80	        value = _eval_formula(expr, {})
81	    except (ValueError, ZeroDivisionError, OverflowError) as exc:
82	        return ToolResult(
83	            success=False, tool_name="calculator",
84	            markdown=f"calculator failed on {expr!r}: {exc}", error=f"eval_error:{str(exc)[:80]}",
85	        )
86	    return ToolResult(
87	        success=True, tool_name="calculator",
88	        markdown=f"`{expr}` = {value} (EXPLORATORY — planner-facing; render via verified_compute)",
89	        statistics={"expression": expr, "value": value},
90	        insights=[f"{expr} = {value}"],
91	    )
92	
93	
94	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
95	# get_evidence — full text + metadata of ONE evidence row
96	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
97	async def _tool_get_evidence(
98	    workspace: Any, *, ev_id: str = "", max_chars: int = 4000, **_ignored: Any,
99	) -> ToolResult:
100	    """Return the FULL text + key metadata of one evidence row by id (the agent normally sees
101	    only the digest). Read-only, no network."""
102	    eid = str(ev_id or "").strip()
103	    row = workspace.ev_store.get(eid) if eid else None
104	    if not isinstance(row, dict):
105	        return ToolResult(
106	            success=False, tool_name="get_evidence",
107	            markdown=f"No evidence row {eid!r} in the pool.", error="ev_not_found",
108	        )
109	    text = _row_text(row)
110	    try:
111	        cap = int(max_chars)
112	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
113	        cap = 4000
114	    body = text[:cap]
115	    md = [
116	        f"**{eid}** [CITE:{eid}]",
117	        f"- title: {str(row.get('title') or row.get('source_title') or '')[:200]}",
118	        f"- url: {str(row.get('source_url') or row.get('url') or '')[:200]}",
119	        f"- text ({len(text)} chars{', truncated' if len(text) > cap else ''}):",
120	        body,
121	    ]
122	    return ToolResult(
123	        success=True, tool_name="get_evidence", markdown="\n".join(md),
124	        source_evidence_ids=[eid],
125	    )
126	
127	
128	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
129	# search_corpus — keyword search over the CURRENT ev_store (read-only, no network)
130	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
131	async def _tool_search_corpus(
132	    workspace: Any, *, query: str = "", top_k: int = 8, **_ignored: Any,
133	) -> ToolResult:
134	    """Rank rows already in the pool by query-term frequency; snippet per hit. The mandatory
135	    'do we already have this?' stop BEFORE any live fetch. Read-only, no network, no LLM."""
136	    q = str(query or "").strip()
137	    if not q:
138	        return ToolResult(
139	            success=False, tool_name="search_corpus",
140	            markdown="search_corpus requires a `query`.", error="missing_query",
141	        )
142	    try:
143	        k = max(1, int(top_k))
144	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
145	        k = 8
146	    q_tokens = set(_tokens(q))
147	    if not q_tokens:
148	        return ToolResult(
149	            success=False, tool_name="search_corpus",
150	            markdown="query had no searchable tokens.", error="empty_query_tokens",
151	        )
152	    scored: list[tuple[float, str, dict]] = []
153	    for eid, row in workspace.ev_store.items():
154	        if not isinstance(row, dict):
155	            continue
156	        text = f"{row.get('title') or ''} {_row_text(row)}"
157	        toks = _tokens(text)
158	        if not toks:
159	            continue
160	        counts = sum(toks.count(t) for t in q_tokens)
161	        if counts <= 0:
162	            continue
163	        # length-normalized term frequency (cheap BM25-ish) so a long junk page can't win on raw counts
164	        score = counts / (1.0 + 0.001 * len(toks))
165	        scored.append((score, str(eid), row))
166	    scored.sort(key=lambda t: (-t[0], t[1]))
167	    hits = scored[:k]
168	    if not hits:
169	        return ToolResult(
170	            success=True, tool_name="search_corpus",
171	            markdown=f"No rows in the pool match {q!r} (searched {len(workspace.ev_store)}).",
172	            statistics={"matches": 0, "searched": len(workspace.ev_store)},
173	        )
174	    lines = [f"**search_corpus {q!r}: {len(hits)} of {len(scored)} matches**", ""]
175	    for score, eid, row in hits:
176	        snippet = _snippet(_row_text(row), q_tokens)
177	        lines.append(f"- {eid} (score={score:.2f}): {snippet} [CITE:{eid}]")
178	    return ToolResult(
179	        success=True, tool_name="search_corpus", markdown="\n".join(lines),
180	        source_evidence_ids=[eid for _, eid, _ in hits],
181	        statistics={"matches": len(scored), "searched": len(workspace.ev_store)},
182	    )
183	
184	
185	def _snippet(text: str, q_tokens: set[str], width: int = 160) -> str:
186	    low = text.lower()
187	    pos = -1
188	    for t in q_tokens:
189	        i = low.find(t)
190	        if i >= 0 and (pos < 0 or i < pos):
191	            pos = i
192	    if pos < 0:
193	        return text[:width].strip()
194	    start = max(0, pos - width // 4)
195	    return ("..." if start > 0 else "") + text[start:start + width].strip()
196	
197	
198	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
199	# list_baskets — one-line-per-basket index of the whole corpus shape
200	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
201	async def _tool_list_baskets(
202	    workspace: Any, *, max_rows: int = 200, **_ignored: Any,
203	) -> ToolResult:
204	    """One line per basket: id, member count, work-level corroboration, assignment status. Lets
205	    the agent scan the whole corpus without N inspect_basket calls. Read-only."""
206	    menu = workspace.basket_menu
207	    member_map = dict(getattr(menu, "basket_member_ev_ids", {}) or {}) if menu is not None else {}
208	    if not member_map:
209	        return ToolResult(
210	            success=True, tool_name="list_baskets",
211	            markdown="No basket digest available (no multi-member clusters).",
212	            statistics={"baskets": 0},
213	        )
214	    corr_map = dict(getattr(menu, "basket_work_corroboration", {}) or {})
215	    assigned_ev = _assigned_ev_ids(workspace)
216	    try:
217	        cap = max(1, int(max_rows))
218	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
219	        cap = 200
220	    lines = [f"**{len(member_map)} baskets**", ""]
221	    for bid in sorted(member_map)[:cap]:
222	        members = list(member_map.get(bid) or [])
223	        n_assigned = sum(1 for m in members if m in assigned_ev)
224	        status = "assigned" if n_assigned == len(members) and members else (
225	            "unassigned" if n_assigned == 0 else f"partial({n_assigned}/{len(members)})"
226	        )
227	        lines.append(
228	            f"- {bid}: {len(members)} member(s), corroboration={corr_map.get(bid, len(members))}, {status}"
229	        )
230	    return ToolResult(
231	        success=True, tool_name="list_baskets", markdown="\n".join(lines),
232	        statistics={"baskets": len(member_map)},
233	    )
234	
235	
236	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
237	# coverage_audit — deterministic (never-LLM) coverage accounting
238	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
239	async def _tool_coverage_audit(workspace: Any, **_ignored: Any) -> ToolResult:
240	    """Deterministic accounting: unassigned basket ids, per-section basket/ev counts, residual
241	    fraction. The O2/O3 math on demand so the agent SEES its own coverage. Never-LLM."""
242	    from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_agent import _plan_field  # noqa: PLC0415
243	
244	    menu = workspace.basket_menu
245	    member_map = dict(getattr(menu, "basket_member_ev_ids", {}) or {}) if menu is not None else {}
246	    assigned_ev = _assigned_ev_ids(workspace)
247	
248	    unassigned_baskets = sorted(
249	        bid for bid, members in member_map.items()
250	        if not any(m in assigned_ev for m in (members or []))
251	    )
252	    total_ev = len(workspace.ev_store)
253	    n_assigned_ev = len(assigned_ev & set(workspace.ev_store.keys()))
254	    residual = 0.0 if total_ev == 0 else round(1.0 - n_assigned_ev / total_ev, 4)
255	
256	    per_section = []
257	    for p in workspace.outline_draft:
258	        title = str(_plan_field(p, "title", "") or "")
259	        ev_ids = list(_plan_field(p, "ev_ids", []) or [])
260	        basket_ids = list(_plan_field(p, "basket_ids", []) or [])
261	        per_section.append((title, len(ev_ids), len(basket_ids)))
262	
263	    lines = [
264	        f"**coverage_audit**: {total_ev} ev rows, {n_assigned_ev} assigned, "
265	        f"residual={residual:.2%}",
266	        f"- unassigned baskets ({len(unassigned_baskets)}): {unassigned_baskets[:40]}",
267	        "- per section (title: ev_ids, baskets):",
268	    ]
269	    for title, n_ev, n_b in per_section:
270	        floor_flag = " [BELOW FLOOR: 0 baskets]" if n_b == 0 else ""
271	        lines.append(f"    {title!r}: {n_ev} ev, {n_b} baskets{floor_flag}")
272	    return ToolResult(
273	        success=True, tool_name="coverage_audit", markdown="\n".join(lines),
274	        statistics={
275	            "total_ev": total_ev, "assigned_ev": n_assigned_ev, "residual": residual,
276	            "unassigned_baskets": len(unassigned_baskets),
277	            "sections": len(per_section),
278	            "sections_below_floor": sum(1 for _, _, n_b in per_section if n_b == 0),
279	        },
280	    )
281	
282	
283	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
284	# preview_section_evidence — what compose will receive for ONE section
285	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
286	async def _tool_preview_section_evidence(
287	    workspace: Any, *, section: str = "", **_ignored: Any,
288	) -> ToolResult:
289	    """For one section: its assigned ev rows' titles + gists — exactly what compose receives.
290	    Read-only ('walk in the reader's shoes')."""
291	    from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_agent import _plan_field  # noqa: PLC0415
292

327	async def _tool_verified_compute(
328	    workspace: Any, *, question: str = "", datapoints: Any = None, spec: Any = None,

331	    """Derive a number through the VERIFIED lane (build_quantified_spec -> execute_quantified_model)
332	    and return a render-eligible sentence carrying its ``[#calc:]`` token — the ONLY compute output
333	    allowed to render. Fail-closed: a bad spec yields success=False and renders nothing.
334	
335	    ``section`` names the outline section this number belongs in; the render-ready sentence is
336	    recorded on the workspace so the FULL-CORPUS composer can APPEND it into that section body
337	    DETERMINISTICALLY (the writer LLM cannot copy the unguessable spec_hash). If ``section`` is
338	    omitted the composer auto-homes the claim by matching the sourced datapoints' ev_ids against
339	    the section's ev_ids. Either way the appended sentence only survives strict_verify if the
340	    computed model verifies its token — emission is fail-closed."""
341	    from src.polaris_graph.outline.verified_compute import run_verified_compute  # noqa: PLC0415

347	            error="bad_args",
348	        )
349	    claim = await run_verified_compute(
350	        workspace, question=str(question or workspace.research_question),

352	    )
353	    if claim is None:

358	        )
359	    lead_text = str(lead or "").strip() or "The computed value is"
360	    sentence = claim.render_sentence(lead_text)
361	    # MOAT DETERMINISTIC EMISSION: record the render-ready sentence on the workspace so the
362	    # composer can inject it into the target section body verbatim (fail-closed: it renders only if
363	    # strict_verify re-verifies its [#calc:] token against the registered model).
364	    input_ev_ids = [
365	        str(dp.get("evidence_id"))
366	        for dp in datapoints

385	            "spec_hash": claim.spec_hash, "field_id": claim.field_id,

469	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
470	# find_contradictions — surface conflict (direction / magnitude outlier), never average
471	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
472	_NUM_RE = re.compile(r"[-+]?\d[\d,]*(?:\.\d+)?")
473	
474	
475	def _headline_numbers(text: str) -> list[float]:
476	    """All parseable magnitudes in a row's text (commas stripped), skipping bare 4-digit years."""
477	    vals: list[float] = []
478	    for m in _NUM_RE.findall(text or ""):
479	        raw = m.replace(",", "")
480	        try:
481	            v = float(raw)
482	        except ValueError:
483	            continue
484	        # skip a bare calendar year (1900-2099) so a date is not mistaken for a magnitude
485	        if raw.isdigit() and 1900 <= v <= 2099 and "." not in raw:
486	            continue
487	        vals.append(v)
488	    return vals
