491	async def _tool_find_contradictions(
492	    workspace: Any, *, ev_ids: Any = None, outlier_ratio: float = 100.0, **_ignored: Any,
493	) -> ToolResult:
494	    """Surface CONFLICT among a set of evidence rows on the same aspect — deterministic, spend-free.
495	
496	    Two conflict modes (design Category D #26): (1) DIRECTION conflict — one row asserts an explicit
497	    increase and another an explicit decrease on the endpoint; (2) MAGNITUDE outlier — one row's
498	    headline value is >= ``outlier_ratio`` x another's (the 1000x-off poison outlier, H22). Conflicts
499	    are surfaced as named material with BOTH ev_ids; nothing is averaged and nothing is deleted
500	    (§-1.3 weight, don't filter). Read-only: never mutates ``ev_store``.
501	
502	    NOTE: this is the deterministic core. Pairwise NLI-entailment via the mirror model is an
503	    OPTIONAL fail-open enrichment (not run here to stay spend-free); it can only ADD advisory
504	    conflicts, never suppress a deterministic one.
505	    """
506	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.contradiction_detector import _extract_direction  # noqa: PLC0415
507	
508	    ids = [str(e) for e in (ev_ids or list(workspace.ev_store.keys()))]
509	    rows = [(e, workspace.ev_store.get(e)) for e in ids]
510	    rows = [(e, r) for e, r in rows if isinstance(r, dict)]
511	    try:
512	        ratio = float(outlier_ratio)
513	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
514	        ratio = 100.0
515	
516	    raw_feats = []
517	    for e, r in rows:
518	        text = _row_text(r)
519	        raw_feats.append((e, _extract_direction(text), _headline_numbers(text)))
520	
521	    # Strip magnitudes that appear IDENTICALLY in every row (a shared denominator/unit such as the
522	    # "per 100,000" in a rate, or a common cohort size) — they are not distinguishing values and
523	    # would false-flag a magnitude outlier (rate vs its own denominator). Deterministic, order-free.
524	    shared: set[float] | None = None
525	    for _e, _d, nums in raw_feats:
526	        s = set(nums)
527	        shared = s if shared is None else (shared & s)
528	    shared = shared or set()
529	    feats = [
530	        (e, d, [n for n in nums if n not in shared] or list(nums))
531	        for e, d, nums in raw_feats
532	    ]
533	
534	    pairs: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
535	    involved: set[str] = set()
536	    for i in range(len(feats)):
537	        for j in range(i + 1, len(feats)):
538	            ei, di, ni = feats[i]
539	            ej, dj, nj = feats[j]
540	            if di and dj and di != dj:
541	                pairs.append({"a": ei, "b": ej, "type": "direction_conflict",
542	                              "direction": f"{ei}:{di} vs {ej}:{dj}"})
543	                involved.update((ei, ej))
544	                continue
545	            if ni and nj:
546	                hi, lo = max(ni), min(nj)
547	                hi2, lo2 = max(nj), min(ni)
548	                best = max(
549	                    (hi / lo if lo else float("inf")),
550	                    (hi2 / lo2 if lo2 else float("inf")),
551	                )
552	                if best >= ratio:
553	                    pairs.append({"a": ei, "b": ej, "type": "magnitude_outlier",
554	                                  "ratio": round(best, 2)})
555	                    involved.update((ei, ej))
556	
557	    if not pairs:
558	        return ToolResult(
559	            success=True, tool_name="find_contradictions",
560	            markdown=f"No direction/magnitude conflict among {len(rows)} row(s) "
561	                     "(agreement or unresolved — never silently averaged).",
562	            statistics={"conflicts": 0, "rows": len(rows), "pairs": []},
563	        )
564	    lines = [f"**find_contradictions: {len(pairs)} conflict pair(s)** across {len(rows)} rows", ""]
565	    for p in pairs:
566	        if p["type"] == "direction_conflict":
567	            lines.append(f"- DIRECTION CONFLICT {p['a']} <-> {p['b']}: {p['direction']} "
568	                         f"[CITE:{p['a']}] [CITE:{p['b']}]")
569	        else:
570	            lines.append(f"- MAGNITUDE OUTLIER {p['a']} <-> {p['b']}: {p['ratio']}x apart "
571	                         f"[CITE:{p['a']}] [CITE:{p['b']}]")
572	    lines.append("")
573	    lines.append("These are CONFLICTING-evidence material (surface both sides; never average, "
574	                 "never delete — §-1.3 weight, don't filter).")
575	    return ToolResult(
576	        success=True, tool_name="find_contradictions", markdown="\n".join(lines),
577	        source_evidence_ids=sorted(involved),
578	        statistics={"conflicts": len(pairs), "rows": len(rows), "pairs": pairs},
579	    )
580

582	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
583	# corroboration_profile — "is this claim REALLY multi-source?" (distinct WORKS, not rows)
584	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
585	def _identity_tokens(row: dict[str, Any]) -> set[str]:
586	    """The STRONG shared-identity tokens of a row: normalized DOI, normalized source-url, and
587	    normalized title. Two rows sharing ANY token are the same underlying work.
588	
589	    This is intentionally STRICTER (merges MORE) than the cp3 digest's url-FIRST ``_same_work_key``:
590	    that key folds on url first, so same-DOI-different-URL copies (a paper mirrored at two hosts)
591	    read as two works. For a 'is this claim REALLY multi-source?' check we must NOT over-claim
592	    corroboration, so we collapse on DOI too (a DOI is the canonical work identity). A row with no
593	    usable token is its own singleton work."""
594	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.outline_digest import _normalized_title_key  # noqa: PLC0415
595	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.finding_dedup import (  # noqa: PLC0415
596	        _normalize_doi, _normalize_source_url,
597	    )
598	    toks: set[str] = set()
599	    try:
600	        doi = _normalize_doi(row.get("doi"))
601	        if doi:
602	            toks.add("doi:" + doi)
603	        url = _normalize_source_url(dict(row))
604	        if url:
605	            toks.add("url:" + url)
606	        tkey = _normalized_title_key(str(row.get("title") or ""))
607	        if tkey:
608	            toks.add(tkey)
609	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a malformed row must not crash the profile; treat as own work
610	        return set()
611	    return toks
612	
613	
614	def _group_distinct_works(members: list[str], tokens_of: dict[str, set[str]]) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
615	    """Union-find over members: two members merge iff they share a strong-identity token
616	    (``_identity_tokens``). Merges are transitive (A~B via DOI, B~C via url => one work). Members
617	    with no token stay singletons. Returns {representative_ev_id: [member ev_ids]} — the distinct
618	    works, keyed deterministically by the lexicographically-smallest member id in each component."""
619	    parent: dict[str, str] = {m: m for m in members}
620	
621	    def find(x: str) -> str:
622	        while parent[x] != x:
623	            parent[x] = parent[parent[x]]
624	            x = parent[x]
625	        return x
626	
627	    def union(a: str, b: str) -> None:
628	        ra, rb = find(a), find(b)
629	        if ra != rb:
630	            parent[max(ra, rb)] = min(ra, rb)
631	
632	    token_owner: dict[str, str] = {}
633	    for m in members:
634	        for tok in tokens_of.get(m, ()):  # empty set => stays a singleton
635	            if tok in token_owner:
636	                union(token_owner[tok], m)
637	            else:
638	                token_owner[tok] = m
639	    works: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
640	    for m in members:
641	        works.setdefault(find(m), []).append(m)
642	    return works
643	
644	
645	def _profile_one_basket(workspace: Any, bid: str, members: list[str]) -> dict[str, Any]:
646	    """Compute the distinct-WORK corroboration profile of one basket from its member rows.
647	
648	    distinct_works = number of connected components under strong-identity union (the HONEST
649	    corroboration count — 4 rows of one paper = 1 work, not 4 sources). ``masquerade`` fires when a
650	    basket has >= 2 member rows but they collapse to ONE distinct work: what LOOKS like multi-source
651	    corroboration is a single work cited N times (redesign.md:217 — this silently counted as
652	    multi-work is a FAIL, so we surface the collapse loudly)."""
653	    from collections import Counter  # noqa: PLC0415
654	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.finding_dedup import _host_of, _normalize_source_url  # noqa: PLC0415
655	
656	    tokens_of: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
657	    tier_counter: Counter[str] = Counter()
658	    hosts: set[str] = set()
659	    for m in members:
660	        row = workspace.ev_store.get(m) or {}
661	        row = row if isinstance(row, dict) else {}
662	        tokens_of[m] = _identity_tokens(row)
663	        tier_counter[str(row.get("tier") or row.get("source_tier") or "?").strip() or "?"] += 1
664	        host = _host_of(_normalize_source_url(dict(row)))
665	        if host:
666	            hosts.add(host)
667	    works = _group_distinct_works(members, tokens_of)
668	    distinct_works = len(works)
669	    # derivative groups = one underlying work represented by >= 2 member rows (copies/chunks)
670	    derivative = {wk: ms for wk, ms in works.items() if len(ms) >= 2}
671	    masquerade = len(members) >= 2 and distinct_works == 1
672	    return {
673	        "basket_id": bid,
674	        "members": len(members),
675	        "distinct_works": distinct_works,
676	        "independent_hosts": len(hosts),
677	        "tier_spread": dict(tier_counter),
678	        "derivative_groups": {wk: ms for wk, ms in derivative.items()},
679	        "single_work_masquerade": masquerade,
680	    }
681	
682	
683	async def _tool_corroboration_profile(
684	    workspace: Any, *, basket_id: str = "", **_ignored: Any,
685	) -> ToolResult:
686	    """Per-basket corroboration at WORK level — the 'is this claim really multi-source?' tool.
687	
688	    Surfaces DISTINCT-WORK counts (never member/row counts): 4 rows of one paper corroborate a claim
689	    ONCE, not four times. For each basket it reports {member rows, distinct works, independent hosts,
690	    tier spread, derivative-copy groups} and FLAGS the single-work masquerade (>= 2 rows collapsing to
691	    ONE work) — the redesign.md:217 failure where single-work corroboration is silently counted as
692	    multi-work. When the cp3 digest's own ``basket_work_corroboration`` DISAGREES with the row-level
693	    recompute, both are shown (a silent-overcount tripwire). Read-only, deterministic, no LLM."""
694	    menu = workspace.basket_menu
695	    member_map = dict(getattr(menu, "basket_member_ev_ids", {}) or {}) if menu is not None else {}
696	    if not member_map:
697	        return ToolResult(
698	            success=True, tool_name="corroboration_profile",
699	            markdown="No basket digest available (no multi-member clusters to profile).",
700	            statistics={"baskets": 0, "profiles": []},
701	        )
702	    digest_corr = dict(getattr(menu, "basket_work_corroboration", {}) or {})
703	    bid = str(basket_id or "").strip()
704	
705	    if bid:
706	        members = list(member_map.get(bid) or [])
707	        if not members:
708	            return ToolResult(
709	                success=False, tool_name="corroboration_profile",
710	                markdown=f"Basket {bid!r} not found in the digest.", error="basket_not_found",
711	            )
712	        prof = _profile_one_basket(workspace, bid, members)
713	        digest_wc = digest_corr.get(bid)
714	        prof["digest_work_corroboration"] = digest_wc
715	        prof["digest_disagreement"] = (
716	            digest_wc is not None and int(digest_wc) != prof["distinct_works"]
717	        )
718	        lines = [_render_profile_line(prof), ""]
719	        if prof["single_work_masquerade"]:
720	            lines.append(
721	                "⚠ SINGLE-WORK MASQUERADE: this basket's rows collapse to ONE distinct work — it is "
722	                "NOT multi-source corroboration. Do NOT present it as independently corroborated."
723	            )
724	        for wk, ms in prof["derivative_groups"].items():
725	            lines.append(f"  derivative copies of one work ({len(ms)} rows): {ms}")
726	        if prof["digest_disagreement"]:
727	            lines.append(
728	                f"  NOTE: cp3 digest reports {digest_wc} works but the member rows recompute to "
729	                f"{prof['distinct_works']} — investigate the silent overcount."
730	            )
731	        return ToolResult(
732	            success=True, tool_name="corroboration_profile", markdown="\n".join(lines),
733	            source_evidence_ids=members,
734	            statistics={"baskets": 1, "profiles": [prof]},
735	        )
736	
737	    # whole-corpus scan: profile every basket, surface masquerades first
738	    profiles = [
739	        _profile_one_basket(workspace, b, list(member_map.get(b) or []))
740	        for b in sorted(member_map)
741	    ]
742	    masquerades = [p for p in profiles if p["single_work_masquerade"]]
743	    lines = [
744	        f"**corroboration_profile: {len(profiles)} baskets, "
745	        f"{len(masquerades)} single-work masquerade(s)**", "",
746	    ]
747	    if masquerades:
748	        lines.append("SINGLE-WORK MASQUERADES (rows > 1 but only 1 distinct work — NOT multi-source):")
749	        for p in masquerades:
750	            lines.append(f"- {_render_profile_line(p)}")
751	        lines.append("")
752	    lines.append("All baskets:")
753	    for p in profiles:
754	        lines.append(f"- {_render_profile_line(p)}")
755	    return ToolResult(
756	        success=True, tool_name="corroboration_profile", markdown="\n".join(lines),
757	        statistics={
758	            "baskets": len(profiles), "masquerades": len(masquerades), "profiles": profiles,
759	        },
760	    )
761	
762	
763	def _render_profile_line(p: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
764	    flag = " [SINGLE-WORK MASQUERADE]" if p["single_work_masquerade"] else ""
765	    tiers = ",".join(f"{k}:{v}" for k, v in sorted(p["tier_spread"].items()))
766	    return (
767	        f"{p['basket_id']}: {p['members']} rows -> {p['distinct_works']} distinct work(s), "
768	        f"{p['independent_hosts']} host(s), tiers[{tiers}]{flag}"
769	    )
770	
771	
772	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
773	# helpers + registration
774	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
775	def _assigned_ev_ids(workspace: Any) -> set[str]:
776	    from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_agent import _plan_field  # noqa: PLC0415
777	    return {
778	        eid
779	        for p in workspace.outline_draft
780	        for eid in (_plan_field(p, "ev_ids", None) or [])
781	    }
782	
783	
784	def register_outline_toolkit(
785	    registry: ToolRegistry,
786	    workspace: Any,
787	    agent_model: str,
788	    deadline: Optional[float] = None,
789	) -> list[str]:
790	    """Wire the T1 read-only/deterministic tools + verified_compute onto ``registry``. Returns the
791	    list of registered tool names. The driver iterates the registry unchanged."""
792	
793	    def _bind(fn):
794	        async def _exec(evidence_store, data_points, client, **kw):  # noqa: ANN001
795	            return await fn(workspace, **kw)
796	        return _exec
797	
798	    def _bind_am(fn):
799	        # fetch_url needs agent_model (for the fold-in topic judge); capture it in the closure so
800	        # the driver's uniform execute(...) contract is unchanged.
801	        async def _exec(evidence_store, data_points, client, **kw):  # noqa: ANN001
802	            return await fn(workspace, agent_model, **kw)
803	        return _exec
804	
805	    specs = [
806	        ("fetch_url", _tool_fetch_url,
807	         "Targeted re-fetch of ONE named URL — turns 'source B cites source A' into actually "
808	         "reading A. Re-enters evidence through the SAME fold-in seam as search_more_evidence.",
809	         {"url": "the URL to fetch", "max_chars": "optional body cap (default 20000)"}),
810	        ("calculator", _tool_calculator,
811	         "Evaluate ONE arithmetic expression (deterministic, no LLM). EXPLORATORY — render a "
812	         "derived number via verified_compute, not this.",
813	         {"expression": "a pure arithmetic expression, e.g. (1493602-903095)*1000"}),
814	        ("get_evidence", _tool_get_evidence,
815	         "Return the FULL text + metadata of one evidence row by id.",
816	         {"ev_id": "the evidence id", "max_chars": "optional text cap (default 4000)"}),
817	        ("search_corpus", _tool_search_corpus,
818	         "Keyword search over the evidence ALREADY in the pool (no network). The 'do we already "
819	         "have this?' stop before any live fetch.",
820	         {"query": "keywords to search", "top_k": "max hits (default 8)"}),
821	        ("list_baskets", _tool_list_baskets,
822	         "One line per basket (id, members, corroboration, assignment) — scan the whole corpus "
823	         "shape at once.",
824	         {}),
825	        ("coverage_audit", _tool_coverage_audit,
826	         "Deterministic coverage accounting: unassigned baskets, per-section counts, residual "
827	         "fraction, sections below floor.",
828	         {}),
829	        ("preview_section_evidence", _tool_preview_section_evidence,
830	         "Preview one section's assigned evidence — exactly what compose will receive.",
831	         {"section": "the section title"}),
832	        ("find_contradictions", _tool_find_contradictions,
833	         "Surface CONFLICT among evidence rows (opposite direction, or a magnitude outlier) — both "
834	         "sides cited, never averaged, never deleted. Deterministic, no network, no LLM.",
835	         {"ev_ids": "list of evidence ids to compare (default: all in the pool)",
836	          "outlier_ratio": "magnitude-outlier threshold (default 100)"}),
837	        ("corroboration_profile", _tool_corroboration_profile,
838	         "Per-basket corroboration at WORK level: distinct works (not row count), independent hosts, "
839	         "tier spread, and a SINGLE-WORK MASQUERADE flag when N rows collapse to one work. The 'is "
840	         "this claim really multi-source?' tool. Deterministic, no network, no LLM.",
841	         {"basket_id": "basket to profile in detail (default: scan all baskets, masquerades first)"}),
842	        ("verified_compute", _tool_verified_compute,
843	         "Derive a number through the VERIFIED lane and render it via a [#calc:] token (the ONLY "
844	         "compute output allowed to render).",
845	         {"question": "what is being computed",
846	          "datapoints": "list of sourced datapoints {evidence_id,label,context,value,unit}",
847	          "spec": "the model spec {model_id,title,inputs,outputs}",
848	          "render_field": "optional output field to render (default first)",
849	          "lead": "optional lead prose for the rendered sentence",
850	          "section": "outline section title this number belongs in — the composer appends the "
851	                     "verified sentence into that section body deterministically (default: "
852	                     "auto-home by the sourced datapoints' ev_ids)"}),
853	    ]
854	    _agent_model_tools = {"fetch_url"}
855	    # Decide-prompt scaling metadata (redesign "Scaling the decide step past 20 tools"): the CORE set
856	    # is always listed in full; the rest collapse into the categorized index past the threshold.
857	    _meta = {
858	        "fetch_url": (True, "retrieval"),
859	        "search_corpus": (True, "retrieval"),
860	        "get_evidence": (True, "retrieval"),
861	        "list_baskets": (False, "retrieval"),
862	        "calculator": (True, "compute"),
863	        "verified_compute": (True, "compute"),
864	        "coverage_audit": (True, "outline_ops"),
865	        "preview_section_evidence": (False, "outline_ops"),
866	        "find_contradictions": (False, "cross_source"),
867	        "corroboration_profile": (False, "cross_source"),
868	    }
869	    registered: list[str] = []
870	    for name, fn, desc, params in specs:
871	        binder = _bind_am if name in _agent_model_tools else _bind
872	        core, category = _meta.get(name, (False, "other"))
873	        registry.register(ToolDefinition(
874	            name=name, description=desc, requires_data=False, requires_llm=False,
875	            parameters=params, execute=binder(fn), tags=[category], core=core,
876	        ))
877	        registered.append(name)
878	    return registered
879

1	"""The verified-compute RENDER lane for the agentic outliner (W3-render, 2026-07-11).
2	
3	This module closes the render half of the compute-reachability P1: it is the ONLY path by
4	which a number the agent DERIVED (a delta / ratio / sum) may reach rendered outline text. It
5	does so without ever widening the faithfulness surface, because it renders that number ONLY as
6	a ``[#calc:model_id:spec_hash:field]`` token — which ``strict_verify`` force-routes to
7	``verify_modeled_atom`` (provenance_generator.py:1885), NOT to the ``[#ev:...]`` / ``[CITE:...]``
8	evidence-span path. A derived number can never satisfy the evidence-span path (its digits appear
9	in no single source span → ``number_not_in_any_cited_span``), so it is structurally impossible to
10	launder an unverified computed value through a citation. Proven end-to-end in
11	tests/polaris_graph/outline/test_verified_compute_render.py.
12	
13	The moat vs. frontier products: the number is not asserted, it is COMPUTED and PROVED. The tool
14	supplies {question, sourced inputs (ev_id + raw_literal via a datapoint match), formula intent};
15	``build_quantified_spec`` (tradeoff_modeler.py:782) validates fail-closed —
16	``literal_span_is_faithful``, the pure-arithmetic AST whitelist, exact-one datapoint match, and
17	the material-dependency perturbation gate — then ``execute_quantified_model``
18	(quantified_analysis.py:650) RE-DERIVES the number by executing the rendered (non-LLM) script in
19	the sandbox and pins its canonical ``display_value``. Regime-C verification later re-checks the
20	rendered digits against that re-execution.
21	
22	SPEND-FREE: no network, no codegen LLM. The spec is supplied by the caller and validated
23	deterministically; a bad spec returns ``None`` (the whole compute is skipped, fail-closed).
24	"""
25	from __future__ import annotations
26	
27	import logging
28	from dataclasses import dataclass
29	from typing import Any, Optional
30	
31	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
32	
33	
34	@dataclass(frozen=True)
35	class ComputedClaim:
36	    """A verified, render-eligible computed number.
37	
38	    ``calc_token`` is the ONLY thing that may be emitted into outline text next to
39	    ``display_value``; ``strict_verify`` (given the workspace's ``quantified_models`` registry)
40	    keeps the sentence iff the number immediately before the token canonicalizes to exactly
41	    ``display_value`` and every sourced input resolves. Anything else about this claim is
42	    telemetry.
43	    """
44	
45	    model_id: str
46	    spec_hash: str
47	    field_id: str
48	    display_value: str
49	    calc_token: str
50	    question: str
51	    formula: str
52	
53	    def render_sentence(self, lead: str) -> str:
54	        """A minimal, verifier-passing sentence: ``<lead> <display_value> <calc_token>.``
55	
56	        ``lead`` is caller prose (e.g. "Adobe's operating income rose by"); the display value
57	        is placed IMMEDIATELY before the calc token (token adjacency, brief §1.4) so
58	        ``verify_modeled_atom`` binds the token to exactly this rendered number.
59	        """
60	        lead = lead.rstrip()
61	        return f"{lead} {self.display_value} {self.calc_token}."
62	
63	
64	async def run_verified_compute(
65	    workspace: Any,
66	    *,
67	    question: str,
68	    datapoints: list[dict[str, Any]],
69	    raw_spec: dict[str, Any],
70	    render_field: Optional[str] = None,
71	    timeout: int | None = None,
72	    on_reject: Optional[Any] = None,
73	) -> Optional[ComputedClaim]:
74	    """Build → validate → execute → REGISTER a verified quantified model; return a
75	    ``ComputedClaim`` for ONE output field (``render_field``, default: the first output).
76	
77	    Fail-closed: returns ``None`` (and discloses the reject reason on the workspace) on ANY
78	    validation or execution failure. On success, the ``QuantifiedResult`` is registered in
79	    ``workspace.quantified_models`` keyed by ``(model_id, spec_hash)`` — exactly the shape
80	    ``strict_verify(..., quantified_models=<registry>)`` consumes — so the returned claim's
81	    ``calc_token`` will verify when it is later rendered into outline text.
82	
83	    Parameters mirror the ``build_quantified_spec`` contract:
84	      * ``datapoints`` — the candidate sourced numbers (cp-format: evidence_id/label/context/
85	        value/unit); each referenced input matches EXACTLY one of these.
86	      * ``raw_spec`` — {model_id, title, inputs:[{name, datapoint_ref|modeled...}], outputs:
87	        [{name, formula, unit, display_kind}]}. The agent supplies the FORMULA INTENT here; the
88	        span derivation and re-execution are the tool's, not the agent's.
89	    """
90	    # Lazy imports: this module is loaded at agent import time; keep the heavy synthesis /
91	    # generator graph off the import path until a compute is actually requested.
92	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.quantified_analysis import execute_quantified_model  # noqa: PLC0415
93	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.tradeoff_modeler import build_quantified_spec  # noqa: PLC0415
94	
95	    ev_store = getattr(workspace, "ev_store", {}) or {}
96	
97	    reject_reasons: list[str] = []
98	
99	    def _reject_sink(reason: str) -> None:
100	        reject_reasons.append(reason)
101	        if on_reject is not None:
102	            try:
103	                on_reject(reason)
104	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a telemetry sink must never break the compute
105	                pass
106	
107	    def _spec_llm(_q: str, _s: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> dict[str, Any]:
108	        # Deterministic: the caller already produced the validated-intent spec. build_quantified_spec
109	        # still runs EVERY fail-closed gate over it (no LLM, no codegen, spend-free).
110	        return raw_spec
111	
112	    try:
113	        spec = build_quantified_spec(
114	            question, datapoints, ev_store,
115	            spec_llm=_spec_llm, on_reject=_reject_sink,
116	        )
117	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-closed: a builder defect skips the compute
118	        _disclose(workspace, f"verified_compute: spec build raised ({str(exc)[:120]}) — no number rendered")
119	        return None
120	
121	    if spec is None:
122	        _disclose(
123	            workspace,
124	            f"verified_compute: spec REJECTED (fail-closed, no number rendered) "
125	            f"reasons={reject_reasons or ['unknown']}",
126	        )
127	        return None
128	
129	    try:
130	        result = await execute_quantified_model(spec, ev_store, timeout=timeout)
131	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — sandbox/exec failure is a clean skip
132	        _disclose(workspace, f"verified_compute: execution raised ({str(exc)[:120]}) — no number rendered")
133	        return None
134	
135	    if result is None:
136	        _disclose(workspace, "verified_compute: model did not execute cleanly — no number rendered (fail-closed)")
137	        return None
138	
139	    # Choose the field to render. Default: the first declared output name (its field_id == name).
140	    field_id = render_field or (spec.outputs[0].name if spec.outputs else "")
141	    disp = result.display_value(field_id)
142	    if not field_id or disp is None:
143	        _disclose(
144	            workspace,
145	            f"verified_compute: field {field_id!r} produced no display_value — no number rendered",
146	        )
147	        return None
148	
149	    # REGISTER: this is what makes the calc token verifiable downstream. Keyed identically to
150	    # strict_verify's consumption ((model_id, spec_hash)).
151	    registry = _ensure_registry(workspace)
152	    registry[result.key()] = result
153	
154	    token = result.calc_token(field_id)
155	    formula = ""
156	    out = spec.output_by_name(field_id.split("@", 1)[0])
157	    if out is not None:
158	        formula = out.formula
159	
160	    _disclose(
161	        workspace,
162	        f"verified_compute: RENDER-ELIGIBLE {disp} via {token} "
163	        f"(formula={formula!r}, inputs={[s.name for s in spec.sourced_inputs]}) — "
164	        "renders ONLY through the verified [#calc:] lane, never [#ev:]/[CITE:]",
165	    )
166	
167	    return ComputedClaim(
168	        model_id=result.model_id,
169	        spec_hash=result.spec_hash,
170	        field_id=field_id,
171	        display_value=disp,
172	        calc_token=token,
173	        question=question,
174	        formula=formula,
175	    )
176	
177	
178	def _ensure_registry(workspace: Any) -> dict:
179	    reg = getattr(workspace, "quantified_models", None)
180	    if not isinstance(reg, dict):
181	        reg = {}
182	        try:
183	            workspace.quantified_models = reg
184	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a frozen/odd workspace still gets a usable local registry
185	            pass
186	    return reg
187	
188	
189	def _disclose(workspace: Any, text: str) -> None:
190	    fn = getattr(workspace, "disclose", None)
191	    if callable(fn):
192	        fn(text)
193	    else:
194	        logger.info("[verified_compute] %s", text)
195

1	"""
2	pipeline_a_ui_adapter — BUG-B-102 R2c (pipeline-B UI parity via pipeline-A).
3	
4	The Docker default `serve` path (scripts/live_server.py → FastAPI) has
5	three legacy graph variants (v1/v2/v3) that do NOT enforce any of the
6	5-round pipeline-A hardening (strict_verify, corpus_approval,
7	delimiter sanitization, abort statuses, two-family evaluator).
8	Codex full-audit pass 1 flagged this as a blocker (B-102).
9	
10	v4 is a thin shim: it accepts the v1/v2/v3 UI signature, synthesizes a
11	pipeline-A query dict, calls `scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3.run_one_query`
12	(the battle-tested, 10+ rounds-hardened orchestrator), then adapts the
13	pipeline-A manifest + report into the UI's JSON contract at
14	`outputs/polaris_graph/{vector_id}.json`.
15	
16	Trace events (`pipeline_start`, `report_assembled`, `pipeline_end`)
17	are emitted through the existing PipelineTracer so the live_server
18	SSE endpoint keeps working without frontend changes.
19	
20	Design choices:
21	- No refactor of run_one_query. Call it as-is with a synthesized `q`.
22	- Domain inference: if the caller's `application` field matches a known
23	  domain key, use it; else default to "custom" (see R2b scope template).
24	- No forced amplification — UI queries come in raw; the retriever will
25	  do whatever query amplification it normally does.
26	- Pipeline-A artifacts go to a run directory; a pointer is added to
27	  the UI JSON so consumers can drill down.
28	"""
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	import asyncio
32	import datetime
33	import json
34	import logging
35	import os
36	import re
37	import time
38	from pathlib import Path
39	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
40	
41	_DOC_ID_RE = re.compile(r"[a-f0-9]{16}")
42	
43	
44	def _load_uploaded_documents(
45	    document_ids: list[str], ingester: Any = None, chunk_size: int = 1500,
46	) -> list[dict]:
47	    """Load chunks for given document_ids from DocumentIngester.
48	
49	    Per I-f3-001. Validates each doc_id against 16-hex format (matches
50	    `hashlib.sha256(file_bytes).hexdigest()[:16]` per document_ingester.py:162)
51	    BEFORE filesystem lookup to prevent path traversal. Skips invalid /
52	    missing IDs with a logged warning. Raises RuntimeError if every
53	    requested ID failed (LAW II — fail loud).
54	    """
55	    if not document_ids:
56	        return []
57	    if ingester is None:
58	        from src.polaris_graph.document_ingester import DocumentIngester
59	        ingester = DocumentIngester()
60	    out: list[dict] = []
61	    for doc_id in document_ids:
62	        if not _DOC_ID_RE.fullmatch(doc_id):
63	            logger.warning("[v4 graph] invalid doc_id format: %r", doc_id)
64	            continue
65	        doc = ingester.get_document(doc_id)
66	        if doc is None:
67	            logger.warning("[v4 graph] doc_id %s not found", doc_id)
68	            continue
69	        content = doc.get("content", "")
70	        if not content:
71	            logger.warning("[v4 graph] doc_id %s has empty content", doc_id)
72	            continue
73	        meta = doc.get("metadata", {})
74	        name = meta.get("original_filename") or meta.get("filename") or doc_id
75	        chunks = [content[i : i + chunk_size] for i in range(0, len(content), chunk_size)]
76	        for idx, chunk_text in enumerate(chunks):
77	            out.append({
78	                "document_id": doc_id, "filename": name,
79	                "chunk_index": idx, "text": chunk_text,
80	            })
81	    if not out:
82	        raise RuntimeError(
83	            f"_load_uploaded_documents: every requested document_id "
84	            f"({len(document_ids)} ids) failed to resolve"
85	        )
86	    return out
87	
88	
89	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
90	
91	
92	_DOMAIN_HINTS = {
93	    "clinical": "clinical",
94	    "medical": "clinical",
95	    "pharma": "clinical",
96	    "health": "clinical",
97	    "tech": "tech",
98	    "technology": "tech",
99	    "software": "tech",
100	    "ai": "tech",
101	    "ml": "tech",
102	    "policy": "policy",
103	    "regulation": "policy",
104	    "regulatory": "policy",
105	    "due_diligence": "due_diligence",
106	    "dd": "due_diligence",
107	    "finance": "due_diligence",
108	    "investment": "due_diligence",
109	}
110	
111	
112	def _infer_domain(application: str, query: str) -> str:
113	    """Return a scope-template domain. Default to 'custom' for
114	    free-form UI queries that don't hint at clinical/tech/policy/dd."""
115	    a = (application or "").lower()
116	    if a in _DOMAIN_HINTS:
117	        return _DOMAIN_HINTS[a]
118	    q = (query or "").lower()
119	    for key, dom in _DOMAIN_HINTS.items():
120	        if key in q:
121	            return dom
122	    return "custom"
123	
124	
125	def _safe_load_json(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
126	    """Best-effort JSON load; empty dict if missing / malformed."""
127	    if not path.exists():
128	        return {}
129	    try:
130	        return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
131	    except Exception:
132	        return {}
133	
134	
135	def _adapt_pipeline_a_to_ui_json(
136	    summary: dict[str, Any],
137	    vector_id: str,
138	    run_dir: Path,
139	) -> dict[str, Any]:
140	    """Convert pipeline-A's summary + on-disk artifacts into the JSON
141	    shape live_server.py reads at
142	    `outputs/polaris_graph/{vector_id}.json`."""
143	    manifest = summary.get("manifest", {}) or _safe_load_json(
144	        run_dir / "manifest.json"
145	    )
146	    report_md_path = run_dir / "report.md"
147	    report_md = (
148	        report_md_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
149	        if report_md_path.exists() else ""
150	    )
151	    biblio = _safe_load_json(run_dir / "bibliography.json")
152	    contradictions = _safe_load_json(run_dir / "contradictions.json")
153	
154	    # Pipeline-A manifest.status is the single authoritative verdict.
155	    status = manifest.get("status", summary.get("status", "unknown"))
156	
157	    # UI expects certain fields — populate defensively. Downstream
158	    # consumers that use `manifest.status` are routed through
159	    # pipeline-A's unified taxonomy (see UNIFIED_STATUS_VALUES).
160	    return {
161	        "vector_id": vector_id,
162	        "original_query": summary.get("question", ""),
163	        "status": status,
164	        # BUG-M-205: release-gating flag surfaced for UI
165	        "release_allowed": manifest.get("release_allowed", status == "success"),
166	        "final_report": report_md,
167	        "bibliography": biblio if isinstance(biblio, list) else [],
168	        "contradictions": contradictions if isinstance(contradictions, list) else [],
169	        "quality_metrics": {
170	            "total_words": manifest.get("generator", {}).get("words", 0),
171	            "sentences_verified": manifest.get("generator", {}).get("sentences_verified", 0),
172	            "sentences_dropped": manifest.get("generator", {}).get("sentences_dropped", 0),
173	            "sections_kept": manifest.get("generator", {}).get("sections_kept", 0),
174	        },
175	        "evaluator_gate": manifest.get("evaluator_gate", {}),
176	        "evidence_selection": manifest.get("evidence_selection", {}),
177	        "cost_usd": manifest.get("cost_usd", 0.0),
178	        "budget_cap_usd": manifest.get("budget_cap_usd", 5.0),
179	        "run_dir": str(run_dir),
180	        "graph_version": "v4",
181	        "timestamps": {
182	            "completed": datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
183	        },
184	    }
185	
186	
187	async def build_and_run_v4(
188	    vector_id: str,
189	    query: str,
190	    application: str = "",
191	    region: str = "",
192	    stage: int = 1,
193	    max_iterations: int = 3,
194	    max_execution_minutes: int = 60,
195	    resume: bool = False,
196	    enable_dashboard: bool = True,
197	    document_ids: Optional[list[str]] = None,
198	    steer_callback: Optional[Callable] = None,
199	    research_brief: str = "",
200	) -> dict[str, Any]:
201	    """Pipeline-B UI entry via pipeline-A. Signature matches v1/v2/v3
202	    for live_server compatibility.
203	
204	    Returns a dict with at least `status` so the caller's existing
205	    status-routing code keeps working. The full UI JSON is written
206	    to `outputs/polaris_graph/{vector_id}.json` as a side effect
207	    (matches the v1/v2/v3 convention).
208	    """
209	    # Deferred import: scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3 imports heavy pipeline-A
210	    # modules; deferring keeps graph_v4 import cheap.
211	    from scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3 import run_one_query
212	
213	    started_at = datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc)
214	    domain = _infer_domain(application, query)
215	
216	    # Initialize tracer (same pattern as v3). The live_server SSE
217	    # tailer watches logs/pg_trace_{vector_id}.jsonl.
218	    tracer = None
219	    try:
220	        from src.polaris_graph.tracing import PipelineTracer
221	        tracer = PipelineTracer(vector_id)
222	        tracer.log_event("pipeline_start", data={
223	            "query": query,
224	            "application": application,
225	            "region": region,
226	            "vector_id": vector_id,
227	            "graph_version": "v4",
228	            "domain": domain,
229	            "pipeline_a_backed": True,
230	        })
231	    except Exception as exc:
232	        logger.warning("[v4 graph] Tracer init failed: %s", str(exc)[:100])
233	
234	    # Synthesize pipeline-A `q` dict.
235	    q = {
236	        "slug": vector_id,
237	        "domain": domain,
238	        "question": query,
239	        "amplified": [],  # pipeline A's retriever amplifies on its own
240	    }
241	    if document_ids:  # truthy: handles None and []
242	        q["uploaded_documents"] = _load_uploaded_documents(document_ids)
243	
244	    # Run directory for pipeline-A artifacts.
245	    out_root = Path(os.getenv(
246	        "PG_V4_OUT_ROOT", "outputs/polaris_graph_v4_runs",
247	    ))
248	    out_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
249	    expected_run_dir = out_root / domain / vector_id
250	
251	    # Delegate to pipeline A.
252	    try:
253	        summary = await run_one_query(q, out_root)
254	    except Exception as exc:
255	        logger.error(
256	            "[v4 graph] run_one_query raised: %s", exc, exc_info=True,
257	        )
258	        # Emit a synthetic error result matching UI contract.
259	        ui_json = {
260	            "vector_id": vector_id,
261	            "original_query": query,
262	            "status": "error_unexpected",
263	            "release_allowed": False,
264	            "final_report": "",
265	            "bibliography": [],
266	            "contradictions": [],
267	            "quality_metrics": {},
268	            "evaluator_gate": {},
269	            "evidence_selection": {},
270	            "cost_usd": 0.0,
271	            "budget_cap_usd": 5.0,
272	            "run_dir": str(expected_run_dir),
273	            "graph_version": "v4",
274	            "error": str(exc)[:500],
275	            "timestamps": {
276	                "started": started_at.isoformat(),
277	                "completed": datetime.datetime.now(
278	                    datetime.timezone.utc,
279	                ).isoformat(),
280	            },
281	        }
282	        _write_ui_json(vector_id, ui_json)
283	        if tracer:
284	            tracer.log_event("pipeline_end", data={
285	                "status": "error_unexpected", "error": str(exc)[:200],
286	            })
287	        return {"status": "error_unexpected", "error": str(exc)[:500]}
288	
289	    # Summary is from pipeline-A; the actual run_dir is inside it.
290	    run_dir = Path(summary.get("run_dir", str(expected_run_dir)))
291	
292	    # Emit report_assembled event if we produced a report.
293	    if tracer:
294	        manifest_status = summary.get("manifest", {}).get(
295	            "status", summary.get("status", ""),
296	        )
297	        is_content_report = (
298	            manifest_status == "success"
299	            or manifest_status.startswith("partial_")
300	        )
301	        tracer.log_event("report_assembled", data={
302	            "status": manifest_status,
303	            "is_content_report": is_content_report,
304	            "has_pipeline_verdict_artifact": manifest_status.startswith("abort_")
305	            or manifest_status.startswith("error_"),
306	        })
307	
308	    # Adapt pipeline-A output into UI JSON.
309	    ui_json = _adapt_pipeline_a_to_ui_json(summary, vector_id, run_dir)
310	    _write_ui_json(vector_id, ui_json)
311	
312	    if tracer:
313	        tracer.log_event("pipeline_end", data={
314	            "status": ui_json["status"],
315	            "release_allowed": ui_json["release_allowed"],
316	            "cost_usd": ui_json["cost_usd"],
317	        })
318	
319	    return {
320	        "status": ui_json["status"],
321	        "release_allowed": ui_json["release_allowed"],
322	        "run_dir": str(run_dir),
323	        "graph_version": "v4",
324	    }
325	
326	
327	def _write_ui_json(vector_id: str, data: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
328	    """Write the UI-shape JSON to the path live_server.py reads.
329	    Uses a tmp + rename pattern so a reader never sees a half-written file."""
330	    out_dir = Path("outputs/polaris_graph")
331	    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
332	    out_path = out_dir / f"{vector_id}.json"
333	    tmp_path = out_dir / f"{vector_id}.json.tmp"
334	    tmp_path.write_text(
335	        json.dumps(data, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str) + "\n",
336	        encoding="utf-8",
337	    )
338	    os.replace(tmp_path, out_path)
339

content.text: Notable: "battle-tested" descriptor in comment (line 12), "graph_v4"/"v4"/"v1/v2/v3" version-name pattern used as filenames/function-name `build_and_run_v4`. Let's check where run_honest_sweep_r3 is defined too — that's an existing file elsewhere, not in this shard. Now check pipeline_definition.py.

content.content:
1	"""
2	Pipeline definition models for custom research pipelines.
3	
4	Two-tier hierarchy (Amendment A4):
5	  PipelineDefinition → MacroStage → PipelineStage
6	
7	A flat SVG DAG with 175 nodes is unreadable. MacroStages are collapsible
8	groups that become LangGraph sub-graphs. The editor shows ~5 macro boxes
9	that expand to reveal internal stages on click.
10	
11	Stage types map to existing polaris_graph node functions:
12	  plan, search, storm_interviews, analyze, verify, evaluate, synthesize,
13	  search_gaps, custom_llm, filter, merge
14	"""
15	
16	import os
17	import uuid
18	from datetime import datetime, timezone
19	from enum import Enum
20	from pathlib import Path
21	from typing import Any, Optional
22	
23	import yaml
24	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator, model_validator
25	
26	
27	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
28	# Stage type registry — maps stage_type strings to node function paths
29	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
30	
31	class StageType(str, Enum):
32	    """Supported pipeline stage types."""
33	    PLAN = "plan"
34	    SEARCH = "search"
35	    STORM_INTERVIEWS = "storm_interviews"
36	    ANALYZE = "analyze"
37	    VERIFY = "verify"
38	    EVALUATE = "evaluate"
39	    SYNTHESIZE = "synthesize"
40	    SEARCH_GAPS = "search_gaps"
41	    CUSTOM_LLM = "custom_llm"
42	    FILTER = "filter"
43	    MERGE = "merge"
44	
45	
46	# Maps stage types to their module paths for dynamic import
47	STAGE_TYPE_REGISTRY: dict[str, str] = {
48	    "plan": "src.polaris_graph.agents.planner",
49	    "search": "src.polaris_graph.agents.searcher",
50	    "storm_interviews": "src.polaris_graph.agents.storm_interviews",
51	    "analyze": "src.polaris_graph.agents.analyzer",
52	    "verify": "src.polaris_graph.agents.verifier",
53	    "evaluate": "src.polaris_graph.graph",
54	    "synthesize": "src.polaris_graph.agents.synthesizer",
55	    "search_gaps": "src.polaris_graph.agents.synthesizer",
56	    "custom_llm": "src.polaris_graph.graph",
57	    "filter": "src.polaris_graph.graph",
58	    "merge": "src.polaris_graph.graph",
59	}
60	
61	
62	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	# Models
64	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
65	
66	class PipelineStage(BaseModel):
67	    """A single stage (node) within a macro-stage."""
68	
69	    stage_id: str = Field(
70	        ...,
71	        description="Unique ID within the pipeline (e.g., 'web_search', 'nli_verify')",
72	    )
73	    stage_type: StageType = Field(
74	        ...,
75	        description="Node type — maps to a registered handler function",
76	    )
77	    label: str = Field(
78	        default="",
79	        description="Human-readable label for the UI",
80	    )
81	    description: str = Field(
82	        default="",
83	        description="What this stage does",
84	    )
85	    config: dict[str, Any] = Field(
86	        default_factory=dict,
87	        description="Stage-specific configuration (thresholds, prompts, concurrency)",
88	    )
89	    depends_on: list[str] = Field(
90	        default_factory=list,
91	        description="Stage IDs this stage depends on (within the same macro-stage)",
92	    )
93	    timeout_seconds: int = Field(
94	        default=300,
95	        description="Max execution time for this stage",
96	    )
97	    retries: int = Field(
98	        default=1,
99	        description="Number of retry attempts on failure",
100	    )
101	
102	    @field_validator("stage_id")
103	    @classmethod
104	    def validate_stage_id(cls, v: str) -> str:
105	        if not v or not v.replace("_", "").replace("-", "").isalnum():
106	            raise ValueError(f"stage_id must be alphanumeric with underscores: {v}")
107	        return v
108	
109	    @field_validator("label", mode="before")
110	    @classmethod
111	    def default_label(cls, v: str, info) -> str:
112	        if not v and "stage_id" in info.data:
113	            return info.data["stage_id"].replace("_", " ").title()
114	        return v
115	
116	
117	class MacroStage(BaseModel):
118	    """A collapsible group of stages — becomes a LangGraph sub-graph."""
119	
120	    macro_id: str = Field(
121	        ...,
122	        description="Unique ID for the macro-stage (e.g., 'collection', 'verification')",
123	    )
124	    label: str = Field(
125	        ...,
126	        description="Human-readable label (e.g., 'Evidence Collection')",
127	    )
128	    description: str = Field(
129	        default="",
130	        description="What this macro-stage accomplishes",
131	    )
132	    stages: list[PipelineStage] = Field(
133	        ...,
134	        min_length=1,
135	        description="Internal stages within this macro-stage",
136	    )
137	    depends_on_macros: list[str] = Field(
138	        default_factory=list,
139	        description="Macro-stage IDs this macro depends on",
140	    )
141	    color: str = Field(
142	        default="#4A90D9",
143	        description="Visual color in the pipeline editor",
144	    )
145	    estimated_minutes: float = Field(
146	        default=5.0,
147	        description="Estimated execution duration for the UI",
148	    )
149	
150	    @field_validator("macro_id")
151	    @classmethod
152	    def validate_macro_id(cls, v: str) -> str:
153	        if not v or not v.replace("_", "").replace("-", "").isalnum():
154	            raise ValueError(f"macro_id must be alphanumeric with underscores: {v}")
155	        return v
156	
157	    @property
158	    def stage_count(self) -> int:
159	        """Number of internal stages."""
160	        return len(self.stages)
161	
162	    def get_stage(self, stage_id: str) -> Optional[PipelineStage]:
163	        """Find a stage by ID within this macro-stage."""
164	        for s in self.stages:
165	            if s.stage_id == stage_id:
166	                return s
167	        return None
168	
169	    def get_entry_stages(self) -> list[PipelineStage]:
170	        """Stages with no internal dependencies — entry points of the sub-graph."""
171	        all_ids = {s.stage_id for s in self.stages}
172	        return [
173	            s for s in self.stages
174	            if not any(dep in all_ids for dep in s.depends_on)
175	        ]
176	
177	    def get_exit_stages(self) -> list[PipelineStage]:
178	        """Stages that nothing depends on — exit points of the sub-graph."""
179	        depended_on = set()
180	        for s in self.stages:
181	            depended_on.update(s.depends_on)
182	        return [s for s in self.stages if s.stage_id not in depended_on]
183	
184	
185	class PipelineDefinition(BaseModel):
186	    """Full pipeline = ordered list of MacroStages.
187	
188	    Top-level model that defines a complete research pipeline.
189	    Can be serialized to/from YAML for storage and sharing.
190	    """
191	
192	    pipeline_id: str = Field(
193	        default_factory=lambda: f"pipe_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}",
194	        description="Unique pipeline identifier",
195	    )
196	    name: str = Field(
197	        ...,
198	        description="Human-readable pipeline name",
199	    )
200	    description: str = Field(
201	        default="",
202	        description="What this pipeline is designed for",
203	    )
204	    version: str = Field(
205	        default="1.0.0",
206	        description="Semantic version of the pipeline definition",
207	    )
208	    author: str = Field(
209	        default="system",
210	        description="Who created this pipeline",
211	    )
212	    created_at: str = Field(
213	        default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
214	        description="Creation timestamp",
215	    )
216	    macro_stages: list[MacroStage] = Field(
217	        ...,
218	        min_length=1,
219	        description="Ordered list of macro-stages",
220	    )
221	    tags: list[str] = Field(
222	        default_factory=list,
223	        description="Tags for categorization and search",
224	    )
225	    is_template: bool = Field(
226	        default=False,
227	        description="Whether this is a built-in template (read-only)",
228	    )
229	
230	    # Quality gate overrides (optional per-pipeline config)
231	    config_overrides: dict[str, Any] = Field(
232	        default_factory=dict,
233	        description="Per-pipeline environment variable overrides (e.g., PG_MAX_ITERATIONS=5)",
234	    )
235	
236	    @model_validator(mode="after")
237	    def validate_dependencies(self) -> "PipelineDefinition":
238	        """Ensure all macro-stage dependencies reference existing macro IDs."""
239	        macro_ids = {m.macro_id for m in self.macro_stages}
240	        for macro in self.macro_stages:
241	            for dep in macro.depends_on_macros:
242	                if dep not in macro_ids:
243	                    raise ValueError(
244	                        f"MacroStage '{macro.macro_id}' depends on unknown "
245	                        f"macro '{dep}'. Available: {macro_ids}"
246	                    )
247	            # Validate internal stage dependencies
248	            stage_ids = {s.stage_id for s in macro.stages}
249	            for stage in macro.stages:
250	                for dep in stage.depends_on:
251	                    if dep not in stage_ids:
252	                        raise ValueError(
253	                            f"Stage '{stage.stage_id}' in macro '{macro.macro_id}' "
254	                            f"depends on unknown stage '{dep}'. "
255	                            f"Available in macro: {stage_ids}"
256	                        )
257	        return self
258	
259	    @model_validator(mode="after")
260	    def validate_no_cycles(self) -> "PipelineDefinition":
261	        """Ensure no circular dependencies between macro-stages."""
262	        visited: set[str] = set()
263	        path: set[str] = set()
264	        macro_map = {m.macro_id: m for m in self.macro_stages}
265	
266	        def _dfs(mid: str) -> None:
267	            if mid in path:
268	                raise ValueError(f"Circular dependency detected: {mid}")
269	            if mid in visited:
270	                return
271	            path.add(mid)
272	            for dep in macro_map[mid].depends_on_macros:
273	                _dfs(dep)
274	            path.discard(mid)
275	            visited.add(mid)
276	
277	        for m in self.macro_stages:
278	            _dfs(m.macro_id)
279	        return self
280	
281	    def flatten(self) -> list[PipelineStage]:
282	        """Return all stages across all macro-stages as a flat list."""
283	        result = []
284	        for macro in self.macro_stages:
285	            result.extend(macro.stages)
286	        return result
287	
288	    @property
289	    def total_nodes(self) -> int:
290	        """Total number of stages across all macro-stages."""
291	        return sum(m.stage_count for m in self.macro_stages)
292	
293	    @property
294	    def total_estimated_minutes(self) -> float:
295	        """Total estimated execution time."""
296	        return sum(m.estimated_minutes for m in self.macro_stages)
297	
298	    def get_macro(self, macro_id: str) -> Optional[MacroStage]:
299	        """Find a macro-stage by ID."""
300	        for m in self.macro_stages:
301	            if m.macro_id == macro_id:
302	                return m
303	        return None
304	
305	    def get_execution_order(self) -> list[str]:
306	        """Topological sort of macro-stage IDs based on dependencies."""
307	        macro_map = {m.macro_id: m for m in self.macro_stages}
308	        in_degree: dict[str, int] = {mid: 0 for mid in macro_map}
309	        for m in self.macro_stages:
310	            for dep in m.depends_on_macros:
311	                in_degree[m.macro_id] += 1
312	
313	        queue = [mid for mid, deg in in_degree.items() if deg == 0]
314	        result: list[str] = []
315	        while queue:
316	            mid = queue.pop(0)
317	            result.append(mid)
318	            for m in self.macro_stages:
319	                if mid in m.depends_on_macros:
320	                    in_degree[m.macro_id] -= 1
321	                    if in_degree[m.macro_id] == 0:
322	                        queue.append(m.macro_id)
323	        return result
324	
325	    def to_yaml(self) -> str:
326	        """Serialize to YAML string."""
327	        data = self.model_dump(exclude_none=True)
328	        return yaml.dump(data, default_flow_style=False, sort_keys=False, allow_unicode=True)
329	
330	    @classmethod
331	    def from_yaml(cls, yaml_str: str) -> "PipelineDefinition":
332	        """Deserialize from YAML string."""
333	        data = yaml.safe_load(yaml_str)
334	        return cls(**data)
335	
336	    @classmethod
337	    def from_yaml_file(cls, path: Path) -> "PipelineDefinition":
338	        """Load from a YAML file."""
339	        content = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
340	        return cls.from_yaml(content)
341	
342	
343	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
344	# Template loading
345	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
346	
347	TEMPLATES_DIR = Path(os.getenv(
348	    "PG_PIPELINE_TEMPLATES_DIR",
349	    str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent / "config" / "pipeline_templates"),
350	))
351	
352	
353	def list_templates() -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
354	    """List available pipeline templates (metadata only)."""
355	    templates = []
356	    if not TEMPLATES_DIR.exists():
357	        return templates
358	    for f in sorted(TEMPLATES_DIR.glob("*.yaml")):
359	        try:
360	            pipeline = PipelineDefinition.from_yaml_file(f)
361	            templates.append({
362	                "pipeline_id": pipeline.pipeline_id,
363	                "name": pipeline.name,
364	                "description": pipeline.description,
365	                "total_nodes": pipeline.total_nodes,
366	                "macro_count": len(pipeline.macro_stages),
367	                "estimated_minutes": pipeline.total_estimated_minutes,
368	                "tags": pipeline.tags,
369	                "file": f.name,
370	            })
371	        except Exception:
372	            continue
373	    return templates
374	
375	
376	def load_template(template_name: str) -> Optional[PipelineDefinition]:
377	    """Load a specific template by file stem name."""
378	    path = TEMPLATES_DIR / f"{template_name}.yaml"
379	    if not path.exists():
380	        return None
381	    return PipelineDefinition.from_yaml_file(path)

1	"""
2	Pipeline wizard — conversational engine that interviews users about their
3	research problem and generates a complete PipelineDefinition.
4	
5	Amendment A3: Multi-turn chat wizard replacing the "Polish with AI" button.
6	6 interview stages: Problem → Sources → Analysis → Verification → Output → Constraints.
7	After 4-8 exchanges, generates a validated pipeline YAML.
8	
9	Sessions are stored in-memory (dict). For production persistence,
10	swap with SQLite (same pattern as campaign_store.py).
11	"""
12	
13	import json
14	import logging
15	import os
16	import uuid
17	from datetime import datetime, timezone
18	from typing import Any, Optional
19	
20	from src.polaris_graph.pipeline_definition import (
21	    MacroStage,
22	    PipelineDefinition,
23	    PipelineStage,
24	)
25	
26	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
27	
28	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
29	# Wizard stages
30	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
31

33	    "problem",        # What is the research question?
34	    "sources",        # What types of sources?
35	    "analysis",       # How should evidence be evaluated?
36	    "verification",   # How rigorous must fact-checking be?
37	    "output",         # Report length? Sections? Exports?
38	    "constraints",    # Budget? Time? Geographic scope?
39	]

43	    "sources": "Data Sources",
44	    "analysis": "Analysis Strategy",
45	    "verification": "Verification Needs",
46	    "output": "Output Format",

108	# Quick-reply chips for each stage
109	STAGE_CHIPS = {
110	    "problem": ["Technology analysis", "Literature review", "Competitive analysis", "Policy research"],
111	    "sources": ["Web + Academic", "Academic only", "Web only", "Include my documents"],
112	    "analysis": ["Comprehensive (50+ queries)", "Focused (20 queries)", "Expert interviews enabled"],
113	    "verification": ["Standard (80%)", "Strict (85%)", "Maximum (90%+)"],
114	    "output": ["Standard (8-12K words)", "Short (2-4K words)", "Long (15-20K words)"],

119	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
120	# Wizard session
121	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
122	
123	class WizardSession:
124	    """In-memory state for a single wizard conversation."""
125	
126	    def __init__(self, session_id: str):
127	        self.session_id = session_id
128	        self.stage_index = 0
129	        self.history: list[dict[str, str]] = []  # [{"role": "wizard"|"user", "text": ...}]
130	        self.collected: dict[str, str] = {}  # stage -> user response summary
131	        self.pipeline_draft: Optional[PipelineDefinition] = None
132	        self.created_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
133	        self.finalized = False
134	
135	    @property
136	    def current_stage(self) -> str:
137	        if self.stage_index >= len(WIZARD_STAGES):
138	            return "complete"
139	        return WIZARD_STAGES[self.stage_index]
140	
141	    @property
142	    def completion_pct(self) -> float:
143	        return min(100.0, (self.stage_index / len(WIZARD_STAGES)) * 100)
144	
145	    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
146	        return {
147	            "session_id": self.session_id,
148	            "stage": self.current_stage,
149	            "stage_label": STAGE_LABELS.get(self.current_stage, "Complete"),
150	            "stage_index": self.stage_index,
151	            "total_stages": len(WIZARD_STAGES),
152	            "completion_pct": self.completion_pct,
153	            "history": self.history,
154	            "collected": self.collected,
155	            "has_draft": self.pipeline_draft is not None,
156	            "finalized": self.finalized,
157	            "created_at": self.created_at,
158	        }
159	
160	
161	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
162	# Pipeline Wizard
163	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
164	
165	# In-memory session store
166	_sessions: dict[str, WizardSession] = {}
167	
168	
169	class PipelineWizard:
170	    """Conversational wizard that interviews users to build pipeline definitions."""
171	
172	    def start_session(self) -> dict:
173	        """Create a new wizard session, return greeting."""
174	        session_id = f"wiz_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
175	        session = WizardSession(session_id)
176	
177	        # Add greeting
178	        greeting = (
179	            "Welcome to the POLARIS Pipeline Wizard.\n\n"
180	            "I'll ask you a few questions about your research needs, "
181	            "then generate a custom pipeline definition tailored to your requirements.\n\n"
182	            + STAGE_PROMPTS["problem"]
183	        )
184	        session.history.append({"role": "wizard", "text": greeting})
185	        _sessions[session_id] = session
186	
187	        return {
188	            "session_id": session_id,
189	            "response": greeting,
190	            "stage": session.current_stage,
191	            "stage_label": STAGE_LABELS[session.current_stage],
192	            "completion_pct": session.completion_pct,
193	            "chips": STAGE_CHIPS.get(session.current_stage, []),
194	            "pipeline_draft": None,
195	        }
196	
197	    def chat(self, session_id: str, user_message: str) -> dict:
198	        """Process a user message and advance the wizard.
199	
200	        Returns response dict with wizard reply, stage info, and optional pipeline draft.
201	        """
202	        session = _sessions.get(session_id)
203	        if not session:
204	            return {"error": f"Session '{session_id}' not found"}
205	        if session.finalized:
206	            return {"error": "Session already finalized"}
207	
208	        # Record user message
209	        session.history.append({"role": "user", "text": user_message})
210	        session.collected[session.current_stage] = user_message
211	
212	        # Advance to next stage
213	        session.stage_index += 1
214	
215	        if session.current_stage == "complete":
216	            # All stages collected — generate pipeline
217	            pipeline = self._generate_pipeline(session)
218	            session.pipeline_draft = pipeline
219	
220	            response = (
221	                "I've analyzed your requirements and generated a custom pipeline.\n\n"
222	                f"**Pipeline: {pipeline.name}**\n"
223	                f"- {pipeline.total_nodes} stages across {len(pipeline.macro_stages)} macro-stages\n"
224	                f"- Estimated time: {pipeline.total_estimated_minutes:.0f} minutes\n"
225	                f"- Tags: {', '.join(pipeline.tags)}\n\n"
226	                "Click **Use This Pipeline** to save and start using it, "
227	                "or **Edit Manually** to fine-tune in the visual editor."
228	            )
229	            session.history.append({"role": "wizard", "text": response})
230	
231	            return {
232	                "session_id": session_id,
233	                "response": response,
234	                "stage": "complete",
235	                "stage_label": "Complete",
236	                "completion_pct": 100.0,
237	                "chips": [],
238	                "pipeline_draft": pipeline.model_dump(),
239	            }
240	        else:
241	            # Send next stage prompt
242	            prompt = STAGE_PROMPTS[session.current_stage]
243	            session.history.append({"role": "wizard", "text": prompt})
244	
245	            return {
246	                "session_id": session_id,
247	                "response": prompt,
248	                "stage": session.current_stage,
249	                "stage_label": STAGE_LABELS[session.current_stage],
250	                "completion_pct": session.completion_pct,
251	                "chips": STAGE_CHIPS.get(session.current_stage, []),
252	                "pipeline_draft": None,
253	            }
254	
255	    def get_draft(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:
256	        """Get the current pipeline draft for a session."""
257	        session = _sessions.get(session_id)
258	        if not session or not session.pipeline_draft:
259	            return None
260	        return session.pipeline_draft.model_dump()
261	
262	    def get_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:
263	        """Get full session state."""
264	        session = _sessions.get(session_id)
265	        if not session:
266	            return None
267	        return session.to_dict()
268	
269	    def finalize(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:
270	        """Finalize the pipeline draft — mark session as complete."""
271	        session = _sessions.get(session_id)
272	        if not session or not session.pipeline_draft:
273	            return None
274	        session.finalized = True
275	        return session.pipeline_draft.model_dump()
276	
277	    def _generate_pipeline(self, session: WizardSession) -> PipelineDefinition:
278	        """Generate a PipelineDefinition from collected wizard responses.
279	
280	        Uses heuristic rules to map user preferences to pipeline configuration.
281	        In future, this could use an LLM call for more nuanced interpretation.
282	        """
283	        responses = session.collected
284	        problem = responses.get("problem", "").lower()
285	        sources = responses.get("sources", "").lower()
286	        analysis = responses.get("analysis", "").lower()
287	        verification = responses.get("verification", "").lower()
288	        output = responses.get("output", "").lower()
289	        constraints = responses.get("constraints", "").lower()
290	
291	        # Determine pipeline characteristics from responses
292	        is_academic = any(kw in sources for kw in ["academic", "peer", "literature", "papers"])
293	        is_quick = any(kw in constraints for kw in ["15 min", "quick", "fast", "short"])
294	        is_deep = any(kw in constraints for kw in ["3 hour", "deep", "exhaustive", "180"])
295	        is_strict = any(kw in verification for kw in ["strict", "85", "maximum", "90"])
296	        is_compliance = any(kw in problem for kw in ["compliance", "regulatory", "legal", "contract"])
297	        wants_storm = "interview" in analysis or "expert" in analysis or "perspective" in analysis
298	        wants_docs = "document" in sources or "upload" in sources or "pdf" in sources
299	        wants_long = any(kw in output for kw in ["long", "15", "20"])
300	        wants_short = any(kw in output for kw in ["short", "2-4", "brief"])
301	
302	        # Base config overrides
303	        config: dict[str, str] = {}
304	
305	        # Query count
306	        if is_quick:
307	            query_count = 15
308	            config["PG_QUERIES_PER_VECTOR"] = "15"
309	            config["PG_MAX_ITERATIONS"] = "1"
310	            config["PG_MAX_EXECUTION_MINUTES"] = "15"
311	        elif is_deep:
312	            query_count = 80
313	            config["PG_QUERIES_PER_VECTOR"] = "80"
314	            config["PG_MAX_ITERATIONS"] = "5"
315	            config["PG_MAX_EXECUTION_MINUTES"] = "180"
316	        else:
317	            query_count = 50
318	            config["PG_MAX_ITERATIONS"] = "3"
319	            config["PG_MAX_EXECUTION_MINUTES"] = "60"
320	
321	        # Word count
322	        if wants_long:
323	            config["PG_TARGET_TOTAL_WORDS"] = "20000"
324	            config["PG_MIN_TOTAL_WORDS"] = "15000"
325	        elif wants_short:
326	            config["PG_TARGET_TOTAL_WORDS"] = "4000"
327	            config["PG_MIN_TOTAL_WORDS"] = "2000"
328	
329	        # Faithfulness threshold
330	        if is_strict:
331	            config["PG_MIN_FAITHFULNESS"] = "0.85"
332	        elif is_compliance:
333	            config["PG_MIN_FAITHFULNESS"] = "0.85"
334	
335	        # STORM
336	        if not is_quick and (wants_storm or not is_quick):
337	            config["PG_STORM_ENABLED"] = "1"
338	        else:
339	            config["PG_STORM_ENABLED"] = "0"
340	
341	        # Build macro-stages
342	        macro_stages: list[MacroStage] = []
343	
344	        # 1. Planning
345	        macro_stages.append(MacroStage(
346	            macro_id="planning",
347	            label="Planning" if not is_compliance else "Compliance Planning",
348	            description="Generate research sub-queries",
349	            color="#6C5CE7",
350	            estimated_minutes=2.0 if not is_deep else 3.0,
351	            stages=[PipelineStage(
352	                stage_id="plan_queries",
353	                stage_type="plan",
354	                label="Query Planning",
355	                config={"queries_per_vector": query_count},
356	            )],
357	        ))
358	
359	        # 2. Collection
360	        collection_stages: list[PipelineStage] = [
361	            PipelineStage(
362	                stage_id="search",
363	                stage_type="search",
364	                label="Search",
365	                config={"academic_priority": is_academic},
366	            ),
367	        ]
368	        if config.get("PG_STORM_ENABLED") == "1":
369	            collection_stages.append(PipelineStage(
370	                stage_id="storm",
371	                stage_type="storm_interviews",
372	                label="STORM Interviews",
373	                depends_on=["search"],
374	            ))
375	        macro_stages.append(MacroStage(
376	            macro_id="collection",
377	            label="Evidence Collection",
378	            description="Search web, academic, and uploaded sources",
379	            color="#00B894",
380	            estimated_minutes=10.0 if not is_deep else 20.0,
381	            depends_on_macros=["planning"],
382	            stages=collection_stages,
383	        ))
384	
385	        # 3. Analysis
386	        macro_stages.append(MacroStage(
387	            macro_id="analysis",
388	            label="Analysis",
389	            description="Extract and score evidence",
390	            color="#FDCB6E",
391	            estimated_minutes=15.0 if not is_deep else 25.0,
392	            depends_on_macros=["collection"],
393	            stages=[PipelineStage(
394	                stage_id="analyze",
395	                stage_type="analyze",
396	                label="Evidence Extraction",
397	            )],
398	        ))
399	
400	        # 4. Verification (skip for quick scan)
401	        if not is_quick:
402	            verify_stages: list[PipelineStage] = [
403	                PipelineStage(
404	                    stage_id="verify",
405	                    stage_type="verify",
406	                    label="NLI Verification",
407	                ),
408	                PipelineStage(
409	                    stage_id="evaluate",
410	                    stage_type="evaluate",
411	                    label="Quality Evaluation",
412	                    depends_on=["verify"],
413	                ),
414	            ]
415	            macro_stages.append(MacroStage(
416	                macro_id="verification",
417	                label="Verification",
418	                description="NLI verification and quality evaluation",
419	                color="#E17055",
420	                estimated_minutes=10.0 if not is_deep else 20.0,
421	                depends_on_macros=["analysis"],
422	                stages=verify_stages,
423	            ))
424	
425	        # 5. Synthesis
426	        synth_depends = ["verification"] if not is_quick else ["analysis"]
427	        synth_stages: list[PipelineStage] = [
428	            PipelineStage(
429	                stage_id="synthesize",
430	                stage_type="synthesize",
431	                label="Report Generation",
432	            ),
433	        ]
434	        if not is_quick:
435	            synth_stages.append(PipelineStage(
436	                stage_id="gap_search",
437	                stage_type="search_gaps",
438	                label="Gap Search",
439	                depends_on=["synthesize"],
440	            ))
441	        macro_stages.append(MacroStage(
442	            macro_id="synthesis",
443	            label="Synthesis",
444	            description="Generate research report with citations",
445	            color="#0984E3",
446	            estimated_minutes=20.0 if not is_deep else 30.0,
447	            depends_on_macros=synth_depends,
448	            stages=synth_stages,
449	        ))
450	
451	        # Generate tags
452	        tags: list[str] = ["custom"]
453	        if is_academic:
454	            tags.append("academic")
455	        if is_compliance:
456	            tags.append("compliance")
457	        if is_quick:
458	            tags.append("quick")
459	        if is_deep:
460	            tags.append("deep")
461	        if wants_docs:
462	            tags.append("documents")
463	
464	        # Build name from first words of problem
465	        problem_text = responses.get("problem", "Custom Research")
466	        name_words = problem_text.split()[:5]
467	        name = " ".join(name_words)
468	        if len(name) > 50:
469	            name = name[:50] + "..."
470	
471	        return PipelineDefinition(
472	            name=f"Custom: {name}",
473	            description=f"Custom pipeline generated by wizard. Problem: {problem_text[:200]}",
474	            macro_stages=macro_stages,
475	            tags=tags,
476	            config_overrides=config,
477	        )
478	
479	
480	# Module-level singleton
481	wizard = PipelineWizard()
482

1	"""Field-agnostic research-planning package (I-meta-005 Phase 1, #985).

5	+ `_ALLOWED_SECTIONS` decomposition path with a field-invariant frame,
6	faceted sub-queries, and an archetype-tagged section outline.

8	This is a SHADOW build: nothing here runs unless the on-flag is set AND the
9	caller explicitly threads the plan through. OFF behavior is byte-identical to

1	"""Candidate constraint adapter (Research Planning Gate — S0).
2	
3	Reconciles the two independent deterministic "rule readers" the champion tree
4	now carries into ONE list of candidate constraints, each with exact prompt
5	spans and an origin tag:
6	
7	  * the ported adversarial rule-reader
8	    (:mod:`src.polaris_graph.instruction.constraint_extractor`), which returns a
9	    :class:`~...instruction.constraint_extractor.Constraints` bundle
10	    (source_types / languages / recency / required_coverage / exclusions /
11	    format / length / tone); and
12	  * the champion intake extractors
13	    (:mod:`src.polaris_graph.retrieval.intake_constraint_extractor`) — the
14	    deterministic regex passes ``extract_constraints_regex`` (dates + language +
15	    journal-only + timeline strictness), ``extract_instruction_slots_regex``
16	    (comparisons / enumerations / topics / structure), and
17	    ``extract_scope_constraints_regex`` (source-type / jurisdiction facets +
18	    named sources).
19	
20	Design contract (S0 — port + reconcile, NO behavior change)
21	-----------------------------------------------------------
22	* **This is a candidate list, NOT a pinned contract.** The output is the
23	  high-recall raw material a later contract compiler validates and pins. Nothing
24	  here hard-gates, excludes, or filters anything, and nothing here is wired into
25	  the pipeline. Callers decide what (if anything) to do with the result.
26	* **Deterministic wins on overlap.** When a rule-reader value and an intake
27	  regex value describe the same ``(dimension, canonical value)``, the intake
28	  (regex) candidate is authoritative for span + force; the rule-reader only
29	  *fills gaps* it uniquely found. Origin is recorded so the merge is auditable.
30	* **Exact spans preserved.** Every candidate carries the verbatim prompt spans
31	  that fired it, recovered as ``prompt[start:end]`` with quote equality. A span
32	  is emitted ONLY when the trigger substring is actually located in the prompt;
33	  an offset is never fabricated. Values whose trigger phrase cannot be located
34	  keep the value but carry an empty span list (so the compiler can still see the
35	  candidate, but can never treat it as span-supported / ``explicit``).
36	* **Force is observed, never invented.** ``hard`` appears only where a
37	  deterministic source already marked an exclusivity / prohibition token (intake
38	  ``strictness == 'hard'``, a named-exclude, or a rule-reader exclusion). Every
39	  other candidate is ``prefer``. This module never promotes a soft phrasing to
40	  hard — that is the mechanical no-invention guarantee at the candidate layer.
41	* **Pure + offline.** No network, no LLM construction. The rule-reader's live
42	  pass is the caller's responsibility; this adapter takes its already-computed
43	  ``Constraints`` (or dict) as input. Default: if the caller passes no
44	  rule-reader result, the adapter reconciles the intake extractors alone.
45	"""
46	
47	from __future__ import annotations
48	
49	import re
50	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
51	from typing import Any, Optional

53	from src.polaris_graph.instruction.constraint_extractor import Constraints
54	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.intake_constraint_extractor import (
55	    _JOURNAL_ONLY_RE,
56	    InstructionSlot,
57	    ScopeConstraints,
58	    UserConstraints,
59	    extract_constraints_regex,
60	    extract_instruction_slots_regex,
61	    extract_scope_constraints_regex,
62	)
