64	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
65	# Origin + force vocab (candidate layer — deliberately small)
66	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
67	
68	# Where a candidate came from. `deterministic` = one of the intake regex passes
69	# (authoritative on overlap). `rule_reader` = the ported adversarial extractor
70	# (gap-filler). `merged` = both sources agreed on the same (dimension, value).
71	ORIGIN_DETERMINISTIC = "deterministic"
72	ORIGIN_RULE_READER = "rule_reader"
73	ORIGIN_MERGED = "merged"
74	
75	# Observed force. NEVER invented: `hard` only when a deterministic source already
76	# marked an exclusivity/prohibition; everything else is `prefer`.
77	FORCE_HARD = "hard"
78	FORCE_PREFER = "prefer"
79	
80	
81	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
82	# GENERIC candidate→canonical REGISTRY (data-driven; NOT per-type if-branches)
83	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
84	# One row per candidate DIMENSION. Each row declares, generically:
85	#   canonical      the canonical contract dimension the projection reads
86	#   subject        the IR subject axis (source / evidence / output / section / topic)
87	#   attribute      the IR attribute axis (kind / quality / language / published_at / ...)
88	#   operator       the default generic operator this dimension asserts
89	#   stage_owner    which stage OWNS the term (no category leakage: a deliverable
90	#                  format term is owned by render, never retrieval)
91	# Adding a new constraint family = one row here, not a new Python branch. Source
92	# KIND normalization is driven by the ONTOLOGY facet's own ``dimension`` field
93	# (see ``_from_scope_constraints``), never by substring "journal"/"peer" matching.
94	#
95	# ``operator`` here is the GENERIC relation used to build the IR; the legacy
96	# ``dimension``/``value`` still carry the flat value the current projection reads.
97	
98	@dataclass(frozen=True)
99	class _RegistryRow:
100	    canonical: str
101	    subject: str
102	    attribute: str
103	    operator: str          # IN / NOT_IN / EQ / GTE / BETWEEN / REQUIRE / PREFER
104	    stage_owner: str       # retrieval / ranking / eligibility / compose / render
105	
106	
107	# Operators mirror planning_gate_schema.OPERATORS (kept as bare strings so this
108	# module stays import-light; validate_contract checks membership).
109	_OP_IN = "IN"
110	_OP_NOT_IN = "NOT_IN"
111	_OP_EQ = "EQ"
112	_OP_GTE = "GTE"
113	_OP_BETWEEN = "BETWEEN"
114	_OP_REQUIRE = "REQUIRE"
115	_OP_PREFER = "PREFER"
116	
117	CANDIDATE_REGISTRY: dict[str, _RegistryRow] = {
118	    # source-side constraints → shape the citable source set (retrieval/eligibility)
119	    "source.types":       _RegistryRow("scope.source_types",     "source", "kind",         _OP_IN,      "eligibility"),
120	    "source.quality":     _RegistryRow("scope.source_quality",   "source", "quality",      _OP_EQ,      "eligibility"),
121	    "source.language":    _RegistryRow("scope.source_languages", "source", "language",     _OP_IN,      "eligibility"),
122	    "source.scope_facet": _RegistryRow("scope.source_types",     "source", "kind",         _OP_IN,      "eligibility"),
123	    "source.jurisdiction":_RegistryRow("scope.jurisdiction",     "source", "jurisdiction", _OP_IN,      "retrieval"),
124	    "source.named":       _RegistryRow("scope.named_sources",    "source", "identity",     _OP_IN,      "retrieval"),
125	    "source.named_exclude": _RegistryRow("scope.excluded_sources", "source", "identity",   _OP_NOT_IN,  "retrieval"),
126	    "date.recency":       _RegistryRow("scope.date",             "source", "published_at", _OP_GTE,     "retrieval"),
127	    # F1: a source EXCLUSION is a NEGATIVE SCOPE predicate ("no blogs" / "do not
128	    # cite blogs"), NOT a content-coverage requirement. Its canonical is a scope
129	    # dimension so ``_author_deterministic_terms`` files it in ``contract.scope``
130	    # (not coverage) and ``retrieval_projection`` routes it to
131	    # ``RetrievalPolicy.excluded_source_kinds`` + an ``op=exclude`` facet.
132	    "content.exclusion":  _RegistryRow("scope.excluded_source_kinds", "source", "kind",    _OP_NOT_IN,  "eligibility"),
133	    "content.coverage":   _RegistryRow("content.coverage",       "topic",  "coverage",     _OP_REQUIRE, "compose"),
134	    "content.comparison": _RegistryRow("content.comparison",     "topic",  "comparison",   _OP_REQUIRE, "compose"),
135	    "deliverable.structure": _RegistryRow("deliverable.structure", "output", "structure",  _OP_REQUIRE, "render"),
136	    # deliverable / rhetoric → render/compose only (NEVER retrieval — no leakage)
137	    "deliverable.format": _RegistryRow("deliverable.format",     "output", "format",       _OP_EQ,      "render"),
138	    "deliverable.length": _RegistryRow("deliverable.length",     "output", "length",       _OP_EQ,      "render"),
139	    "rhetoric.tone":      _RegistryRow("rhetoric.tone",          "output", "tone",         _OP_PREFER,  "compose"),
140	}
141	
142	# The subject/attribute pair for a candidate dimension, for merge reconciliation.
143	# Two candidates that share (subject, attribute) describe the SAME axis even if
144	# their raw ``(dimension, value)`` differ (e.g. rule-reader source.types
145	# "journal_article" and intake source.scope_facet "peer_reviewed_journal") — the
146	# old exact-(dimension,value) overlap key missed that (RECON-2 §3).
147	
148	
149	# Value-level normalization: some source-type TOKENS are really QUALITY markers,
150	# not kinds (a "high_quality"/"peer_reviewed" directive selects a quality profile
151	# over metadata, per RECON-2 §4 — it must NOT be treated as a literal source kind
152	# nor invented from a plain "journal" facet). This is a small DATA map, keyed by
153	# value, applied uniformly wherever a source-type value arrives — it never fires
154	# off substring "journal"/"peer" in a facet id (that was the invented-quality bug).
155	_QUALITY_VALUE_TOKENS: dict[str, str] = {
156	    "high_quality": "high",
157	    "high": "high",
158	    "peer_reviewed": "high",
159	    "peer-reviewed": "high",
160	    "peer_review": "high",
161	}
162	
163	
164	def _ontology_source_type_index(
165	    ontology: "dict[str, Any] | None",
166	) -> dict[str, str]:
167	    """Build a synonym→facet_id index for ``source_type`` facets from the ontology.
168	
169	    This is the generic candidate→canonical alias table (RECON-2 §4): it maps
170	    every surface phrasing AND the canonical facet_id itself to the facet_id, so a
171	    rule-reader alias ("journal_article"/"journal article") and an intake facet
172	    ("peer_reviewed_journal") reconcile to ONE canonical value BEFORE the overlap
173	    key runs — closing the alias-under-different-dimensions merge gap (RECON-2 §3).
174	    Adding a source kind is a YAML block, not a Python branch. Fail-soft to {}.
175	    """
176	    idx: dict[str, str] = {}
177	    try:
178	        if ontology is None:
179	            from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.scope_facet_classifier import (  # noqa: PLC0415
180	                load_scope_ontology,
181	            )
182	            ontology = load_scope_ontology()
183	        for facet in (ontology.get("facets") or []):
184	            if not isinstance(facet, dict):
185	                continue
186	            if str(facet.get("dimension") or "source_type") != "source_type":
187	                continue
188	            fid = str(facet.get("id") or "").strip()
189	            if not fid:
190	                continue
191	            idx[fid.lower()] = fid
192	            idx[fid.replace("_", " ").lower()] = fid
193	            for syn in (facet.get("synonyms") or []):
194	                s = str(syn).strip().lower()
195	                if s:
196	                    idx[s] = fid
197	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open: no alias index, no reconciliation
198	        return {}
199	    return idx
200	
201	
202	def _canonicalize_source_type(
203	    value: Optional[str], alias_index: dict[str, str]
204	) -> tuple[Optional[str], bool]:
205	    """Map a source-type value to its ontology facet_id. Returns (canonical,
206	    mapped). Tries the value, its underscore/space variants, and a plural-stripped
207	    form. Unmapped → (value, False) so an unknown kind stays a first-class OPAQUE
208	    value, never dropped."""
209	    if not value:
210	        return value, False
211	    for cand in (
212	        value.strip().lower(),
213	        value.strip().replace("_", " ").lower(),
214	        value.strip().replace(" ", "_").lower(),
215	        value.strip().rstrip("s").lower(),
216	    ):
217	        if cand in alias_index:
218	            return alias_index[cand], True
219	    return value, False
220	
221	
222	def _registry_row(dimension: str) -> Optional[_RegistryRow]:
223	    return CANDIDATE_REGISTRY.get(dimension)
224	
225	
226	def _reroute_quality(dimension: str, value: Optional[str]) -> tuple[str, Optional[str]]:
227	    """If ``value`` is a QUALITY marker on a source-type dimension, reroute it to
228	    ``source.quality`` with the canonical quality value. Otherwise pass through.
229	    Data-driven (``_QUALITY_VALUE_TOKENS``), never substring branching."""
230	    if dimension in ("source.types",):
231	        q = _QUALITY_VALUE_TOKENS.get((value or "").strip().lower())
232	        if q is not None:
233	            return "source.quality", q
234	    return dimension, value
235	
236	
237	def candidate_term_id(c: "CandidateConstraint") -> str:
238	    """A STABLE term_id for a candidate, so the deterministic-authoritative core
239	    and the monotonicity validator can address the same term across compile.
240	
241	    Keyed by canonical (subject, attribute, casefolded value) — stable across
242	    re-runs and independent of list order. This is the id the compiler pins and
243	    ``validate_monotonicity`` checks survived."""
244	    row = _registry_row(c.dimension)
245	    subj = row.subject if row else "source"
246	    attr = row.attribute if row else (c.dimension.split(".", 1)[-1] or "value")
247	    val = (c.value or "").strip().casefold()
248	    val = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "_", val).strip("_") or "any"
249	    return f"det.{subj}.{attr}.{val}"

252	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
253	# Types
254	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
255	
256	@dataclass(frozen=True)
257	class PromptSpan:
258	    """A verbatim prompt span. ``quote`` MUST equal ``prompt[start:end]``.
259	
260	    Constructed only via :func:`_locate_span`, which guarantees that invariant;
261	    a value whose trigger phrase is not found in the prompt yields NO span (the
262	    candidate is emitted with an empty span list) rather than a fabricated one.
263	    """
264	
265	    start: int
266	    end: int
267	    quote: str
268	
269	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
270	        return {"start": self.start, "end": self.end, "quote": self.quote}
271	
272	
273	@dataclass
274	class CandidateConstraint:
275	    """One reconciled candidate constraint (NOT a pinned contract term).
276	
277	    ``dimension`` is a stable string in the same open family the gate schema
278	    uses (e.g. ``source.types``, ``source.language``, ``date.recency``,
279	    ``content.coverage``, ``content.comparison``, ``deliverable.format``,
280	    ``deliverable.length``, ``rhetoric.tone``, ``source.named``,
281	    ``source.scope_facet``, ``source.jurisdiction``).
282	    """
283	
284	    dimension: str
285	    value: Optional[str]
286	    force: str = FORCE_PREFER          # observed force; never invented as hard
287	    origin: str = ORIGIN_DETERMINISTIC
288	    spans: list[PromptSpan] = field(default_factory=list)
289	    # free-form provenance so the reconciliation stays auditable (which raw
290	    # source(s) contributed, the raw trigger phrase, any op/strictness, etc.).
291	    detail: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
292	    # generic-IR annotations (registry-derived; OPTIONAL). ``normalization_status``
293	    # is 'opaque' for a kind the registry couldn't map — preserved, never dropped.
294	    subject: str = ""
295	    attribute: str = ""
296	    operator: str = ""
297	    stage_owner: str = ""
298	    normalization_status: str = ""     # exact | proposed | opaque
299	
300	    def canonical_dimension(self) -> str:
301	        row = _registry_row(self.dimension)
302	        return row.canonical if row else self.dimension
303	
304	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
305	        out = {
306	            "dimension": self.dimension,
307	            "value": self.value,
308	            "force": self.force,
309	            "origin": self.origin,
310	            "spans": [s.to_dict() for s in self.spans],
311	            "detail": dict(self.detail),
312	        }
313	        if self.subject:
314	            out["subject"] = self.subject
315	        if self.attribute:
316	            out["attribute"] = self.attribute
317	        if self.operator:
318	            out["operator"] = self.operator
319	        if self.stage_owner:
320	            out["stage_owner"] = self.stage_owner
321	        if self.normalization_status:
322	            out["normalization_status"] = self.normalization_status
323	        return out
324	
325	
326	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
327	# Span recovery (exact, quote-equality-guaranteed, never fabricated)
328	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
329	
330	def _locate_span(prompt: str, phrase: Optional[str]) -> Optional[PromptSpan]:
331	    """Return the first exact occurrence of ``phrase`` in ``prompt`` as a span.
332	
333	    Guarantees ``span.quote == prompt[span.start:span.end]``. Returns ``None``
334	    (no span) when ``phrase`` is empty or cannot be located verbatim — the
335	    adapter never invents an offset for a phrase it can't find.
336	    """
337	    if not prompt or not phrase:
338	        return None
339	    idx = prompt.find(phrase)
340	    if idx == -1:
341	        # Fall back to a case-insensitive locate, but keep the ACTUAL prompt
342	        # substring as the quote so quote-equality still holds exactly.
343	        low_i = prompt.lower().find(phrase.lower())
344	        if low_i == -1:
345	            return None
346	        return PromptSpan(low_i, low_i + len(phrase), prompt[low_i:low_i + len(phrase)])
347	    return PromptSpan(idx, idx + len(phrase), prompt[idx:idx + len(phrase)])
348	
349	
350	def _spans_for(prompt: str, phrases: list[Optional[str]]) -> list[PromptSpan]:
351	    """Locate each phrase; drop those not found; dedup by (start, end)."""
352	    out: list[PromptSpan] = []
353	    seen: set[tuple[int, int]] = set()
354	    for ph in phrases:
355	        sp = _locate_span(prompt, ph)
356	        if sp is None:
357	            continue
358	        key = (sp.start, sp.end)
359	        if key in seen:
360	            continue
361	        seen.add(key)
362	        out.append(sp)
363	    return out
364	
365	
366	def _stamp_ir(c: CandidateConstraint, *, opaque: bool = False) -> CandidateConstraint:
367	    """Fill a candidate's generic-IR fields from the registry (in place).
368	
369	    The candidate's RAW dimension keys the registry; the row supplies the
370	    canonical subject/attribute/operator/stage_owner. ``normalization_status`` is
371	    ``exact`` when the registry recognized the dimension, else ``opaque`` — an
372	    opaque candidate is a FIRST-CLASS constraint we preserve verbatim, never drop
373	    (the monotonic-lossless core principle). Returns the same candidate.
374	    """
375	    row = _registry_row(c.dimension)
376	    if row is not None:
377	        c.subject = row.subject
378	        c.attribute = row.attribute
379	        # exclusion flips IN→NOT_IN already encoded in the row; a hard "only X"
380	        # keeps the row operator. A soft candidate stays PREFER-shaped only when
381	        # the row itself is PREFER; otherwise the operator reflects the relation
382	        # (force carries hard/soft separately).
383	        c.operator = row.operator
384	        c.stage_owner = row.stage_owner
385	        c.normalization_status = "opaque" if opaque else "exact"
386	    else:
387	        # unknown dimension: keep it, mark opaque, route generically.
388	        c.subject = "source"
389	        c.attribute = c.dimension.split(".", 1)[-1] or "value"
390	        c.normalization_status = "opaque"
391	    return c
392	
393	
394	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
395	# Per-dimension candidate builders from each deterministic source
396	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
397	
398	def _from_user_constraints(
399	    prompt: str, uc: UserConstraints
400	) -> list[CandidateConstraint]:
401	    """Intake ``extract_constraints_regex`` -> candidates (dates, language,
402	    journal-only, timeline strictness)."""
403	    out: list[CandidateConstraint] = []
404	
405	    # Language (ISO code). Recover the span from the raw directive that named it.
406	    if uc.language:
407	        spans = _spans_for(prompt, list(uc.raw_directives))
408	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
409	            dimension="source.language",
410	            value=uc.language,
411	            force=FORCE_PREFER,
412	            origin=ORIGIN_DETERMINISTIC,
413	            spans=spans,
414	            detail={"source": "intake_regex", "raw_directives": list(uc.raw_directives)},
415	        )))
416	
417	    # Date window (start/end). Force reflects the extractor's own strictness.
418	    if uc.date_start_year is not None or uc.date_end_year is not None:
419	        force = FORCE_HARD if uc.timeline_strictness == "hard" else FORCE_PREFER
420	        trigger_phrases: list[Optional[str]] = list(uc.raw_directives)
421	        if uc.timeline_trigger_span:
422	            trigger_phrases.append(uc.timeline_trigger_span)
423	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
424	            dimension="date.recency",
425	            value=_date_value(uc),
426	            force=force,
427	            origin=ORIGIN_DETERMINISTIC,
428	            spans=_spans_for(prompt, trigger_phrases),
429	            detail={
430	                "source": "intake_regex",
431	                "date_start_iso": uc.date_start_iso(),
432	                "date_end_iso": uc.date_end_iso(),
433	                "timeline_strictness": uc.timeline_strictness,
434	                "timeline_trigger_span": uc.timeline_trigger_span,
435	            },
436	        )))
437	
438	    # Journal-only is EXTRACTED-but-DORMANT in the champion (operator veto). It
439	    # is a real deterministic signal, so it becomes a candidate — never a hard
440	    # gate from this path alone. SPAN-LOSS FIX (RECON-2 §2.2): the regex DID match
441	    # a verbatim substring; re-locate that exact match phrase in the prompt so the
442	    # candidate carries a real span and is promotable/explicit — not spans=[].
443	    if uc.journal_only:
444	        m = _JOURNAL_ONLY_RE.search(prompt or "")
445	        spans = _spans_for(prompt, [m.group(0)]) if m else []
446	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
447	            dimension="source.types",
448	            value="journal_article",
449	            force=FORCE_PREFER,
450	            origin=ORIGIN_DETERMINISTIC,

content.text: Continuing to the rest of the file.

content.content:
451	            spans=spans,
452	            detail={
453	                "source": "intake_regex",
454	                "journal_only_dormant": True,
455	                "raw_trigger": m.group(0) if m else "",
456	            },
457	        )))
458	
459	    return out
460	
461	
462	def _date_value(uc: UserConstraints) -> Optional[str]:
463	    lo = uc.date_start_iso()
464	    hi = uc.date_end_iso()
465	    if lo and hi:
466	        return f"{lo}..{hi}"
467	    return lo or hi

469	
470	def _from_instruction_slots(
471	    prompt: str, slots: list[InstructionSlot]
472	) -> list[CandidateConstraint]:
473	    """Intake ``extract_instruction_slots_regex`` -> content/coverage candidates.
474	
475	    Comparisons, enumerations, requested topics and structure are coverage
476	    obligations (NOT headings — that mapping is deliberately not ported). Each
477	    slot's verbatim ``text`` recovers its span.
478	    """
479	    out: list[CandidateConstraint] = []
480	    for slot in slots:
481	        dim = {
482	            "comparison": "content.comparison",
483	            "enumeration": "content.coverage",
484	            "topic": "content.coverage",
485	            "structure": "deliverable.structure",
486	        }.get(slot.kind, "content.coverage")
487	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
488	            dimension=dim,
489	            value=", ".join(slot.entities) if slot.entities else slot.text,
490	            force=FORCE_PREFER,
491	            origin=ORIGIN_DETERMINISTIC,
492	            spans=_spans_for(prompt, [slot.text]),
493	            detail={
494	                "source": "intake_regex",
495	                "slot_id": slot.slot_id,
496	                "slot_kind": slot.kind,
497	                "entities": list(slot.entities),
498	            },
499	        )))
500	    return out
501	
502	
503	def _from_scope_constraints(
504	    prompt: str, sc: ScopeConstraints
505	) -> list[CandidateConstraint]:
506	    """Intake ``extract_scope_constraints_regex`` -> scope-facet / named-source
507	    candidates. Force mirrors the facet's own strictness (hard only where the
508	    extractor already saw an exclusivity/prohibition token)."""
509	    out: list[CandidateConstraint] = []
510	
511	    for facet in sc.facets:
512	        # GENERIC candidate→canonical (RECON-2 §4): route by the ONTOLOGY facet's
513	        # OWN ``dimension`` field, NOT by substring "journal"/"peer" matching. A
514	        # ``source_type`` facet → source.scope_facet (→ scope.source_types); a
515	        # ``language`` facet → source.language; jurisdiction/geography → their own
516	        # canonical dimension. Adding a facet family is data (a YAML block), not
517	        # a Python branch.
518	        fdim = (facet.dimension or "source_type").strip().lower()
519	        dim = {
520	            "jurisdiction": "source.jurisdiction",
521	            "geography": "source.jurisdiction",
522	            "language": "source.language",
523	        }.get(fdim, "source.scope_facet")
524	        # An ``exclude`` op is a NOT_IN exclusion, not an inclusion.
525	        if facet.op == "exclude":
526	            dim = "content.exclusion"
527	        force = FORCE_HARD if facet.strictness == "hard" else FORCE_PREFER
528	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
529	            dimension=dim,
530	            value=facet.facet_id,
531	            force=force,
532	            origin=ORIGIN_DETERMINISTIC,
533	            # locate the RAW trigger phrase the user wrote (facet.trigger_span is a
534	            # verbatim prompt slice), NOT the canonical facet_id — the span-loss-
535	            # safe pattern this whole rebuild generalizes (RECON-2 §2.4).
536	            spans=_spans_for(prompt, [facet.trigger_span]),
537	            detail={
538	                "source": "intake_regex",
539	                "op": facet.op,
540	                "strictness": facet.strictness,
541	                "facet_dimension": facet.dimension,
542	                "raw_trigger": facet.trigger_span,
543	            },
544	        )))
545	
546	    for named in list(sc.named_include) + list(sc.named_exclude):
547	        # A named-exclude is always hard (identity-enforced downstream); a
548	        # named-include is a boost (prefer). F1: a named EXCLUDE ("don't use
549	        # Reuters") is a NEGATIVE named predicate (``source.named_exclude`` →
550	        # ``scope.excluded_sources``), NOT a content-coverage requirement — routing
551	        # it through ``content.exclusion`` used to invert it into a required
552	        # coverage lane that made the gate GO FIND the forbidden source.
553	        force = FORCE_HARD if named.strictness == "hard" else FORCE_PREFER
554	        dim = "source.named" if named.op != "exclude" else "source.named_exclude"
555	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
556	            dimension=dim,
557	            value=named.label,
558	            force=force,
559	            origin=ORIGIN_DETERMINISTIC,
560	            spans=_spans_for(prompt, [named.label]),
561	            detail={
562	                "source": "intake_regex",
563	                "op": named.op,
564	                "strictness": named.strictness,
565	                "identity": dict(named.identity),
566	                "raw_trigger": named.label,
567	            },
568	        )))
569	
570	    return out
571	
572	
573	def _from_rule_reader(
574	    prompt: str, rr: Constraints
575	) -> list[CandidateConstraint]:
576	    """Ported adversarial rule-reader ``Constraints`` -> candidates.
577	
578	    The rule-reader carries no character offsets, so spans are recovered by
579	    locating each value verbatim in the prompt. When a value's phrase is not
580	    present verbatim (the LLM paraphrased it), the candidate is still emitted but
581	    with an empty span list — never a fabricated offset. Exclusions are the only
582	    rule-reader dimension that carries ``hard`` force (an explicit "exclude").
583	    """
584	    out: list[CandidateConstraint] = []
585	
586	    for st in rr.source_types:
587	        # SPAN-LOSS FIX (RECON-2 §2.1): the rule-reader emits a CANONICAL token
588	        # ("journal_article"); the prompt says "journal articles". Locating the
589	        # canonical token verbatim misses. Try the canonical token first, then its
590	        # de-canonicalized surface form (underscore→space) so a source-type the
591	        # user DID write recovers a real span (and can be promoted/explicit).
592	        # A quality marker ("high_quality") reroutes to source.quality (data map).
593	        dim, val = _reroute_quality("source.types", st)
594	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
595	            dimension=dim, value=val, force=FORCE_PREFER,
596	            origin=ORIGIN_RULE_READER,
597	            spans=_spans_for(prompt, [st, str(st).replace("_", " ")]),
598	            detail={"source": "rule_reader", "raw_value": st},
599	        )))
600	    for lang in rr.languages:
601	        # The rule-reader emits a NORMALIZED ISO code (e.g. "en"); a bare 2-char
602	        # code matches stray substrings ("...intellig-en-ce..."), so we do NOT
603	        # try to span-locate it. The deterministic intake pass owns the real
604	        # language span; on overlap the merge keeps that authoritative span.
605	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
606	            dimension="source.language", value=lang, force=FORCE_PREFER,
607	            origin=ORIGIN_RULE_READER, spans=[],
608	            detail={"source": "rule_reader", "no_span": "normalized_iso_code"},
609	        )))
610	    if rr.recency:
611	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
612	            dimension="date.recency", value=rr.recency, force=FORCE_PREFER,
613	            origin=ORIGIN_RULE_READER, spans=_spans_for(prompt, [rr.recency]),
614	            detail={"source": "rule_reader"},
615	        )))
616	    for cov in rr.required_coverage:
617	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
618	            dimension="content.coverage", value=cov, force=FORCE_PREFER,
619	            origin=ORIGIN_RULE_READER, spans=_spans_for(prompt, [cov]),
620	            detail={"source": "rule_reader"},
621	        )))
622	    for exc in rr.exclusions:
623	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
624	            dimension="content.exclusion", value=exc, force=FORCE_HARD,
625	            origin=ORIGIN_RULE_READER, spans=_spans_for(prompt, [exc]),
626	            detail={"source": "rule_reader", "kind": "exclusion"},
627	        )))
628	    if rr.format:
629	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
630	            dimension="deliverable.format", value=rr.format, force=FORCE_PREFER,
631	            origin=ORIGIN_RULE_READER, spans=_spans_for(prompt, [rr.format]),
632	            detail={"source": "rule_reader"},
633	        )))
634	    if rr.length:
635	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
636	            dimension="deliverable.length", value=rr.length, force=FORCE_PREFER,
637	            origin=ORIGIN_RULE_READER, spans=_spans_for(prompt, [rr.length]),
638	            detail={"source": "rule_reader"},
639	        )))
640	    if rr.tone:
641	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
642	            dimension="rhetoric.tone", value=rr.tone, force=FORCE_PREFER,
643	            origin=ORIGIN_RULE_READER, spans=_spans_for(prompt, [rr.tone]),
644	            detail={"source": "rule_reader"},
645	        )))
646	
647	    return out
648	
649	
650	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
651	# Reconciliation (deterministic wins on overlap; rule-reader fills gaps)
652	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
653	
654	# Overlap is keyed by the CANONICAL (subject, attribute, value) — NOT the raw
655	# (dimension, value). This reconciles aliases expressed under different raw
656	# dimensions (rule-reader ``source.types=journal_article`` and intake
657	# ``source.scope_facet=peer_reviewed_journal`` both canonicalize to
658	# source/kind/journal…), which the old exact-(dimension,value) key never merged
659	# (RECON-2 §3). Values still normalize the same way across the two sources.
660	def _overlap_key(c: CandidateConstraint) -> tuple[str, str, str]:
661	    row = _registry_row(c.dimension)
662	    subj = row.subject if row else (c.subject or "source")
663	    attr = row.attribute if row else (c.attribute or c.dimension)
664	    return (subj, attr, (c.value or "").strip().casefold())
665	
666	
667	def _merge_pair(
668	    det: CandidateConstraint, rr: CandidateConstraint
669	) -> CandidateConstraint:
670	    """Deterministic candidate wins on span + force; record that both agreed and
671	    union the spans (the rule-reader may have located a span the regex path
672	    didn't, and vice-versa)."""
673	    merged_spans = list(det.spans)
674	    seen = {(s.start, s.end) for s in merged_spans}
675	    for s in rr.spans:
676	        if (s.start, s.end) not in seen:
677	            merged_spans.append(s)
678	            seen.add((s.start, s.end))
679	    detail = dict(det.detail)
680	    detail["also_seen_by"] = "rule_reader"
681	    detail["rule_reader_detail"] = dict(rr.detail)
682	    return _stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
683	        dimension=det.dimension,
684	        value=det.value,            # deterministic value is authoritative
685	        force=det.force,            # deterministic force is authoritative
686	        origin=ORIGIN_MERGED,
687	        spans=merged_spans,
688	        detail=detail,
689	    ))
690	
691	
692	def reconcile_candidates(
693	    prompt: str,
694	    *,
695	    rule_reader: "Constraints | dict[str, Any] | None" = None,
696	    ontology: "dict[str, Any] | None" = None,
697	) -> list[CandidateConstraint]:
698	    """Reconcile the deterministic intake extractors with the rule-reader.
699	
700	    Parameters
701	    ----------
702	    prompt:
703	        The raw task prompt (spans are recovered against this exact text).
704	    rule_reader:
705	        The ported adversarial rule-reader result — a
706	        :class:`~...instruction.constraint_extractor.Constraints`, its
707	        ``to_dict()`` form, or ``None`` to reconcile the intake extractors
708	        alone. This adapter NEVER runs the live rule-reader itself; the caller
709	        supplies an already-computed result (keeping the adapter offline/pure).
710	    ontology:
711	        Optional scope ontology override forwarded to
712	        ``extract_scope_constraints_regex`` (tests inject a fixture; production
713	        loads the default).
714	
715	    Returns
716	    -------
717	    list[CandidateConstraint]
718	        High-recall candidates with exact spans + origin. Deterministic
719	        candidates come first (they win on overlap); rule-reader-only candidates
720	        follow. This is a candidate list, NOT a pinned contract.
721	    """
722	    prompt = prompt or ""
723	
724	    # --- deterministic pass (the three champion intake regex extractors) ---
725	    uc = extract_constraints_regex(prompt)
726	    slots = extract_instruction_slots_regex(prompt)
727	    try:
728	        sc = extract_scope_constraints_regex(prompt, ontology=ontology)
729	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open: no scope facets on ontology miss
730	        sc = ScopeConstraints(source="regex")
731	
732	    deterministic: list[CandidateConstraint] = []
733	    deterministic.extend(_from_user_constraints(prompt, uc))
734	    deterministic.extend(_from_instruction_slots(prompt, slots))
735	    deterministic.extend(_from_scope_constraints(prompt, sc))
736	
737	    # --- rule-reader pass (gap-filler) ---
738	    rr_obj: Optional[Constraints] = None
739	    if isinstance(rule_reader, Constraints):
740	        rr_obj = rule_reader
741	    elif isinstance(rule_reader, dict):
742	        rr_obj = _constraints_from_dict(rule_reader)
743	    rule_candidates = _from_rule_reader(prompt, rr_obj) if rr_obj is not None else []
744	
745	    # --- ONTOLOGY ALIAS NORMALIZATION (RECON-2 §3/§4): canonicalize every
746	    # source-type value to its ontology facet_id so a rule-reader alias
747	    # ("journal_article") and an intake facet ("peer_reviewed_journal") collapse
748	    # to ONE canonical value and MERGE (deterministic-hard wins). An unmapped kind
749	    # is left verbatim + marked opaque (first-class, never dropped).
750	    alias_index = _ontology_source_type_index(ontology)
751	    if alias_index:
752	        for cand in deterministic + rule_candidates:
753	            if cand.dimension in ("source.types", "source.scope_facet"):
754	                canon, mapped = _canonicalize_source_type(cand.value, alias_index)
755	                cand.value = canon
756	                cand.dimension = "source.types"
757	                if not mapped:
758	                    cand.normalization_status = "opaque"
759	
760	    # --- reconcile: deterministic wins on overlap; rule-reader fills gaps ---
761	    by_key: dict[tuple[str, str], CandidateConstraint] = {}
762	    ordered: list[CandidateConstraint] = []
763	    for c in deterministic:
764	        key = _overlap_key(c)
765	        by_key[key] = c
766	        ordered.append(c)
767	
768	    for rc in rule_candidates:
769	        key = _overlap_key(rc)
770	        existing = by_key.get(key)
771	        if existing is not None and existing.origin != ORIGIN_RULE_READER:
772	            # overlap -> merge into the deterministic winner in place
773	            idx = ordered.index(existing)
774	            merged = _merge_pair(existing, rc)
775	            ordered[idx] = merged
776	            by_key[key] = merged
777	        elif existing is None:
778	            by_key[key] = rc
779	            ordered.append(rc)
780	        # (a rule-reader/rule-reader dup collapses onto the first)
781	
782	    return ordered
783	
784	
785	def _constraints_from_dict(d: dict[str, Any]) -> Constraints:
786	    """Rebuild a :class:`Constraints` from its ``to_dict()`` form (tolerant)."""
787	    def _list(v: Any) -> list[str]:
788	        return [str(x) for x in v] if isinstance(v, (list, tuple)) else ([] if v in (None, "") else [str(v)])
789	
790	    def _opt(v: Any) -> Optional[str]:
791	        return None if v in (None, "") else str(v)
792	
793	    return Constraints(
794	        source_types=_list(d.get("source_types")),
795	        languages=_list(d.get("languages")),
796	        recency=_opt(d.get("recency")),
797	        required_coverage=_list(d.get("required_coverage")),
798	        exclusions=_list(d.get("exclusions")),
799	        format=_opt(d.get("format")),
800	        length=_opt(d.get("length")),
801	        tone=_opt(d.get("tone")),
